000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

얇은 shim, 두꺼운 플러그인

처음 쓰는 분을 위한 친절한 입문 가이드 — 101 6장 + 개발자 문서 28장

Terminal
$ npx lazycodex-ai install
$ codex # with omo plugin
가이드 시작공식
34 섹션14 코퍼스 축KO / EN

101 입문 가이드

00. 시작하기 전에02. LazyCodex가 뭐예요?03. 설치하고 준비하기04. 첫 사용 5분05. 매일 이렇게 써요06. 설정하고, 더 배우기

개발자 문서

소스 코드 수준의 깊은 분석입니다.

개발자 심화

01. 코퍼스 읽기 지도 (000)07. 1. Identity08. 2. Distribution shim09. 3. Aggregate plugin `omo`10. 4. Axis synthesis11. 5. Axis synthesis12. 6. Axis synthesis13. 7. Axis synthesis14. 8. Axis synthesis15. 9. Axis synthesis16. 10. Axis synthesis17. 11. TUI

심화 분석

18. 축 01: TUI 설계19. 축 02: 프롬프트·컨텍스트20. 축 03: 도구 런타임21. 축 04: 스킬 시스템22. 축 05: 서브에이전트23. 축 06: 앱·서버 표면24. 축 07: 샌드박스·정책25. 축 08: 설정 시스템26. 축 09: MCP 오케스트레이션27. 축 10: 메모리 파이프라인28. 축 11: 플러그인 시스템29. 축 12: 가디언·승인30. 축 13: 기타31. 축 14: 웹 검색32. 역공학 (015)33. 기술 참조 & 합성
00

시작하기 전에

이 가이드는 누구를 위한 건지, 무엇을 배우는지

이 가이드는 이런 분을 위해 준비했어요

Codex를 연동하고 다루고 싶지만 설정 과정이 너무 까다롭고 귀찮게 느껴지시는 분, 여러 AI 에이전트를 한 번에 다스리며 복잡한 소프트웨어를 만들어보고 싶으신 분들을 위해 준비했어요. 어려운 개념과 용어는 아주 쉽게 풀어서 설명해 드릴 테니 부담 없이 가볍게 읽어주세요.

이 글에서 배우는 것

  • LazyCodex가 무엇이고 어떤 원리로 복잡한 개발 설정을 생략해 주는지
  • 번거로운 전역 설치 없이 5분 만에 세팅을 끝내는 방법
  • 계획 수립부터 개발 완료까지 스스로 반복 수행하는 특별한 명령들

미리 알아두면 좋은 것

원활한 실습을 위해 아래 두 가지만 미리 확인해 주세요.

  1. 내 컴퓨터에 **Bun(번)**이 설치되어 있어야 해요. (Bun은 자바스크립트 프로그램을 매우 빠르게 실행해 주는 최신 도구예요)
  2. 작업을 진행할 코드 폴더가 준비되어 있어요.

설정이 막막하셨다면 전혀 걱정하지 않으셔도 돼요. 다음 장에서 LazyCodex가 무엇인지부터 차근차근 알아볼게요.

000/01_corpus_navigation_map.md

Corpus Navigation Map

This repository is a comparative research corpus for coding agents and harnesses. Use this map to decide where to read first without physically moving existing data.

For reversible old-to-new bucket proposals, see /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/02_corpus_move_candidates.md.

For the canonical relationship between /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/, see /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/03_overview_canonical_map.md.

Physical Rename Rule

Before a folder is moved, add an explicit mapping row using:

old_path -> proposed_path | classification | move_status | evidence

For gitlinks, also reconcile .gitmodules before moving.

Top-Level Triage

Use this when scanning the root directory. Status is intentionally conservative: keep-current means the current path remains the source of truth for now.

Current Path Triage Status
`/Users/jun/Developer/codex/.agen

000/03_overview_canonical_map.md

Overview Canonical Map

This map resolves the apparent duplication between /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ without deleting or moving files.

Canonical Rule

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ is the root overview index and short-entry reading spine.
  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ is the deep overview source set.
  • If the same filename exists in both folders and content differs, keep both:
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> first for orientation,
    • read /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> for detailed analysis.
  • If content is identical, treat /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/<name> as the navigation copy and /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/<name> as the deep-overview location until a later link audit decides whether deduplication is safe.

Paired Overview Files

Filename /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/ bytes /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/ bytes Equal Rule
00_ag_overview.md 27224 15790 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ai_overview.md 25716 25716 yes duplicate content; defer dedupe until link audit
00_ay_overview.md 3815 798 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cc_overview.md 25990 27537 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_co_overview.md 41569 17061 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cp_overview.md 23525 6854 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_cr_overview.md 3216 595 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ge_overview.md 47345 35414 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_gr_overview.md 3310 546 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_ki_overview.md 3360 692 no keep both: orientation plus detail
00_oc_overview.md 43650 33628 no keep both: orientation plus detail

000/00_harness-index.md (발췌)

Harness Source Index

This file is the non-conflicting 000/ index for source-repository harnesses.

The canonical deep overview files stay under /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/. Files in /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000 named 00_harness-index_* are short routing/index notes only. They intentionally avoid the canonical 00_<prefix>_overview.md basename so wiki/backlink indexers do not see two files with the same 00_*_overview identity.

Routing

Prefix Index stub Canonical overview Classification
hermes /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_hermes.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_hermes_overview.md standalone source-based agent runtime
omc /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omc.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omc_overview.md Claude Code host-dependent orchestration harness
omx /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omx.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omx_overview.md Codex host-dependent control plane
omo /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000/00_harness-index_omo.md /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_omo_overview.md OpenCode host-dependent harness plus Codex Light adapter
lc n/a /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_lc_overview.md LazyCodex / Codex Light distribution wrapper for OmO
sp n/a /Users/jun/Developer/codex/000_overview/00_sp_overview.md Superpowers multi-harness skills/bootstrap methodology layer

02

LazyCodex가 뭐예요?

한 문장 정의와 비유로 이해하기

한 문장으로 말하면

LazyCodex는 복잡한 설정 고민 없이 명령어 단 한 줄로 강력한 AI 개발 프레임워크인 OmO를 구축하고 Codex 설정을 자동으로 마무리해 주는 초간편 도구예요.

비유로 이해하기

컴퓨터에 복잡한 개발 프로그램을 설치할 때 수많은 옵션과 설정을 일일이 비교하며 공부할 필요 없이, "알아서 가장 완벽하고 인기 있는 옵션들만 골라서 한 번에 다 깔아주는 자동 설치 팩"을 누르는 느낌이에요. 마치 리눅스의 우분투나 빔 에디터의 편리한 세팅 번들(LazyVim)처럼, Codex 개발 환경을 초심자도 즉시 사용하도록 포장해 둔 패키지라고 보면 돼요.

이런 걸 할 수 있어요

  • 복잡한 가상 비서 협업 세동작을 원클릭으로 한 번에 설치할 수 있어요.
  • 지시를 내리면 에이전트들이 스스로 설계를 짜고, 역할을 나누어 병렬(여러 작업을 동시에 실행하는 것)로 작업하도록 조율해 줘요.
  • 기획서 자동 작성부터 완성 검증 단계까지 알아서 무한 루프를 돌며 코드를 완성해 내요.

왜 좋냐면요

보통 에이전트 협업 시스템을 직접 구현하려면 설정 파일을 직접 만져야 해서 진입 장벽이 높아요. 하지만 LazyCodex는 검증된 표준 조합으로 알아서 빌드해 줘서 초보 개발자라도 즉시 Hephaestus(대장장이 에이전트)나 Oracle(판정 에이전트) 같은 똑똑한 인공지능 비서들로 구성된 개발 팀을 내 컴퓨터에 바로 거느릴 수 있게 돼요.

더 세밀한 아키텍처(OmO 프레임워크의 상세 작동 원리나 멀티 모델 라우팅)가 궁금하시면 상단 메뉴의 개발자 문서에서 깊이 있게 다뤄요. 지금은 편안하게 자동화 세팅을 완료하는 도구라는 점만 느끼고 다음 단계로 넘어가도 괜찮아요.

03

설치하고 준비하기

설치 + API 키 준비 + 막힐 때 팁

전역 설치 없이 바로 사용해요

LazyCodex는 컴퓨터에 프로그램을 영구적으로 깔아두는 글로벌(전역) 설치가 필요 없어요. 대신 bunx라는 도구를 써서 실행할 때만 필요한 파일을 실시간으로 임시 다운로드해서 실행하도록 지원해요. 덕분에 컴퓨터 용량을 낭비하지 않고 항상 최신 버전을 간편하게 쓸 수 있어요.

Bun이 이미 준비되어 있다면 아래 명령어 한 줄로 설치 및 환경 구성을 즉시 진행해 보세요.

bunx lazycodex-ai install

비주얼 화면 없이 자동 설치하기

만약 터미널에서 화살표를 움직이는 설정 화면(TUI)조차 띄우고 싶지 않고 완전히 자동으로 자동화 세팅을 끝내고 싶다면, 아래와 같이 특수 옵션을 덧붙여 실행하면 돼요.

bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

막힐 때 팁

  • bunx 명령어를 실행할 수 없다는 에러가 발생한다면, 컴퓨터에 Bun(번)이 깔려 있는지 먼저 점검해 보세요. 터미널에 bun --version을 쳤을 때 숫자가 나오지 않으면 Bun 공식 웹사이트 안내에 따라 Bun부터 준비해 주셔야 해요.
  • 실행 중 권한 에러 등이 뜨면 임시 폴더 쓰기 권한이 막혀 있는지 체크해 보세요.
04

첫 사용 5분

따라 하면 되는 첫 실습

5분 만에 작업 기획안 작성하기

설치를 끝내면 작업 중인 에디터 대화 세션에 강력한 개발 보조 명령어들이 추가돼요. 실제로 어떻게 기획안을 도출해 내는지 바로 실험해 볼게요.

따라 해보기

  1. 내 작업 폴더 안에서 평소 사용하시는 코딩 에이전트(인공지능 코딩 프로그램)를 실행해 주세요.
  2. 대화 입력 창에 아래와 같이 입력해서 기획 및 분석을 요청해 보세요.
/ulw-plan "파이썬으로 간단한 할 일 관리 애플리케이션 만들기"
  1. 지시를 받은 에이전트가 코드를 막바로 작성하지 않고, 먼저 구조와 상세 기능의 청사진(기획 계획서)을 짤 거예요. 완성된 계획은 plans/ 폴더에 마크다운 문서로 안전하게 자동 생성돼요.

작성된 기획 결과 보기

프로젝트 폴더 내에 새로 만들어진 plans/ 디렉터리(폴더)를 확인해 보세요. 어떤 방식으로 에러를 다룰 것인지, 검증 과정은 어떻게 거칠 것인지가 조밀하게 계획서 형식으로 들어가 있어요.

다음 장에서는 이렇게 작성한 계획서 기반으로 실코딩 작업을 에이전트에게 실제로 위임하여 수행하는 일상 작업 리듬을 살펴볼게요.

05

매일 이렇게 써요

실제 일상 사용 흐름과 비결

매일 쓰는 3단계 자동 흐름

세팅이 손에 익으면 매일 아침 출근해서 아래의 세 단계를 반복하며 개발을 진행하게 돼요.

  1. 설계하기: /ulw-plan 명령어로 무엇을 개발할지 설계 문서를 먼저 구성해요.

  2. 자동 개발 시작: 만들어진 기획서 이름을 넣고 실행해요.

    /start-work 내-기획서-이름

    이 명령어를 치면 에이전트들이 기획서 안에 기재된 체크박스 항목을 하나씩 물리치며 코드를 스스로 완성해 가요.

  3. 자율 수정 루프: 특정 버그를 해결하거나 완성도 판단(Oracle 검증)이 끝날 때까지 스스로 계속 수정을 가하는 무한 반복이 필요할 때 아래 명령을 요청해요.

    /ulw-loop "특정 부분의 테스트가 통과할 때까지 고쳐줘"

꼭 지켜야 할 일상 리듬

  • 기획서 작성 시점과 개발 시점 구분하기: ulw-plan은 절대로 제품용 본문 코드를 직접 생산하지 않고 오로지 마크다운 문서(기획안)만 써요. 먼저 기획안이 내 마음에 들게 나왔는지 검토한 뒤에 start-work 명령으로 자동 작업을 트리거(실행 지시)하는 방식을 생활화해 보세요.
  • 체크박스 위주로 나누기: 기획 문서 안의 할 일(체크리스트)들이 구체적이고 명확할수록 에이전트들이 도중에 헷갈려 하지 않고 마지막 단계까지 완벽하게 도달해요.
06

설정하고, 더 배우기

내 입맛대로 설정 + 다음 단계 안내

제로 설정으로 가볍게

LazyCodex는 "설정할 게 없는 것"이 가장 큰 특징이에요. 복잡하게 환경 변수를 맞추고 컴포넌트 간 연결점을 이어줄 필요 없이, 대다수의 사용자에게 최적인 기본 설정값들이 미리 튜닝(최적화 설정)되어 있어요.

기본 사양 외에 특별히 나만의 에이전트 행동 규칙(프롬프트 규칙)이나 협업 가상 비서의 구성을 더 뜯어고치고 싶다면 프로젝트 루트의 설정 파일을 직접 편집할 수도 있어요.

비용 최적화와 라우팅

여러 에이전트가 동시에 일하는 만큼 사용 비용이 걱정될 수 있어요. 기본 설정값은 가벼운 작업은 저렴한 모델로, 고난도 설계나 판정 작업은 똑똑한 대형 모델로 자동 라우팅(목적지에 맞춰 경로를 배정해 주는 것)하도록 알맞게 정비되어 있어요.

다음 단계로

이제 LazyCodex의 작동 양상과 기본 명령어들의 쓰임새를 마스터하셨어요. 앞으로는 아래 방향으로 활용 범위를 넓혀 보세요.

  • 간단한 테스트 위주의 코딩 기능부터 자동 위임해 보기
  • 내 코딩 보조 에이전트의 페르소나(역할 정의) 커스텀하기
  • 실시간으로 에이전트들이 소통하는 모니터링 콘솔 켜기

만약 백엔드에서 작동하는 상세한 흐름 제어, 훅 시스템 설계 구조, 자율 판단 주기 같은 심화 아키텍처에 관심이 생기셨다면 상단 메뉴의 개발자 문서를 확인해 보세요. 소스 코드 세부 설명과 모듈 구조까지 자세하게 확인하실 수 있어요.

그럼 편안하고 게으른 인공지능 협업 코딩을 시작해 볼까요? 😊

07

1. Identity

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex는 독립 코딩 에이전트가 아니다. README가 명시한다:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Identity — distribution layer, not agent runtime

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/README.md:79-81
LazyCodex is a thin distribution layer. The core engine is oh-my-openagent (OmO),
included as a submodule under `src/`.

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

1. Identity — distribution layer, not agent runtime

LazyCodex는 독립 코딩 에이전트가 아니다. README가 명시한다:

// 161_lazycodex/README.md:79-81
LazyCodex is a thin distribution layer. The core engine is oh-my-openagent (OmO),
included as a submodule under `src/`.
Responsibility Owner
Terminal TUI, transcript rendering Codex (121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tui)
Model loop, tool dispatch, compaction Codex
OS sandbox, permission_mode, approvals Codex
web_search, webfetch, spawn_agent Codex
npm install shorthand, marketplace snapshot LazyCodex
Hook subprocesses, LSP MCP, skills sync LC plugins/omo
Ultrawork TOML roles, keyword directive LC plugins/omo

.gitmodules는 src를 oh-my-openagent로 선언하지만, Codex 플랫폼 경로의 실질 증거는 wrapper + plugins/omo 번들이다. OpenCode 전용 ctx.client.tui.showToast 같은 API는 submodule에 존재하나 --platform=codex install 시 활성화되지 않는다 (01_lc_tui.md §5).

08

2. Distribution shim

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

전체 런타임은 28줄 Node forwarder다:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Distribution shim — bin/lazycodex-ai.js

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-20
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

2. Distribution shim — bin/lazycodex-ai.js

전체 런타임은 28줄 Node forwarder다:

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-20
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})
Line Behavior
:6-7 --dry-run prints resolved argv without executing
:9-11 install injects --platform=codex
:18-20 stdio: "inherit" — child owns stdin/stdout (Codex/OmO TUI if any)
:22-27 Propagates spawn error / exit status

테스트가 계약을 고정한다: 161_lazycodex/test/lazycodex-ai-bin.test.mjs — install vs non-install forwarding.

Negative finding: grep of 161_lazycodex/bin/ shows no config parser, no TOML reader, no model router — only spawnSync.

09

3. Aggregate plugin `omo`

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

Manifest:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Aggregate plugin omo — workspace topology

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker",
  "components/rules",
  "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp",
  "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal",
  "components/ultrawork"
],

}

"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",

  "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"]
}

README / docs

  "name": "omo",
  "source": "./plugins/omo",
  "policy": { "installation": "AVAILABLE", "authentication": "ON_INSTALL" }
}]

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

3. Aggregate plugin omo — workspace topology

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker",
  "components/rules",
  "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp",
  "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal",
  "components/ultrawork"
],
"scripts": {
  "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present"
}

Manifest:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14-23
"skills": "./skills/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
"interface": {
  "displayName": "OMO",
  "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"]
}

Marketplace registration:

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:6-15
"plugins": [{
  "name": "omo",
  "source": "./plugins/omo",
  "policy": { "installation": "AVAILABLE", "authentication": "ON_INSTALL" }
}]

사용자는 sisyphuslabs marketplace에서 하나의 플러그인만 설치하면 6개 컴포넌트가 함께 활성화된다.

10

4. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex는 provider message JSON을 조립하지 않는다. Context는 Codex hook 이벤트로 주입된다.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — prompt & context (002)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json — event map
"SessionStart": [ rules session-start, telemetry session-start ],
"UserPromptSubmit": [ rules, ultrawork-detector.py, ultragoal ],
"PostToolUse": [ comment-checker, lsp, rules (apply_patch) ],
"PostCompact": [ rules post-compact ]

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

4. Axis synthesis — prompt & context (002)

LazyCodex는 provider message JSON을 조립하지 않는다. Context는 Codex hook 이벤트로 주입된다.

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json — event map
"SessionStart": [ rules session-start, telemetry session-start ],
"UserPromptSubmit": [ rules, ultrawork-detector.py, ultragoal ],
"PostToolUse": [ comment-checker, lsp, rules (apply_patch) ],
"PostCompact": [ rules post-compact ]

Rules static injection pipeline (components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:212-247):

  1. Read env config — skip if disabled, mode === "off", or mode === "dynamic"
  2. engine.loadStaticRules(cwd) discovers instruction files
  3. Filter rules already in transcript (:250-274)
  4. Emit JSON with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (:455-462)

Ultrawork keyword trigger:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

Hook은 항상 exit 0 — advisory injection, permission gate 아님.

11

5. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

LC-owned tool surface = LSP MCP stdio server 하나뿐:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — tool runtime & MCP (003, 009)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

5. Axis synthesis — tool runtime & MCP (003, 009)

LC-owned tool surface = LSP MCP stdio server 하나뿐:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

MCP handler (components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:44-76):

  • initialize → capabilities { tools: { listChanged: false } }
  • tools/list → lsp_diagnostics, lsp_rename, symbols, etc.
  • tools/call → language server subprocess with shell: false

PostToolUse hooks는 모델이 호출하는 tool이 아님 — Codex lifecycle에서 subprocess로 실행:

Event Matcher Timeout
PostToolUse ^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|…)$ comment-checker 30s, lsp 60s

Exa, Tavily, browser, filesystem MCP — snapshot에 없음 (aggregate.test.mjs:60-70).

12

6. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

Skills는 build-time sync로 aggregate된다:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — skill system (004)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:6-14
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

README / docs

// sync-skills.mjs:24-30
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)` |

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

6. Axis synthesis — skill system (004)

Skills는 build-time sync로 aggregate된다:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:6-14
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

OpenCode-only tool names in skill examples get Codex compatibility preamble:

// sync-skills.mjs:24-30
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)` |

Checked-in inventory includes: comment-checker, lsp, rules, ultragoal, programming, debugging, frontend-ui-ux, refactor, review-work, remove-ai-slops. Invocation은 Codex host lazy-load — LC는 packaging만.

13

7. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

Four bundled Codex agent roles:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — subagents & collaboration (005)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
# READ-ONLY — never edit, write, apply_patch, spawn_agent

README / docs

# librarian.toml:1-6 — external OSS/docs; uses host web_search + webfetch
# plan.toml:1-6 — planner only; refuses implementation
# codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17 — binary APPROVE vs REJECTION gate

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

7. Axis synthesis — subagents & collaboration (005)

Four bundled Codex agent roles:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
# READ-ONLY — never edit, write, apply_patch, spawn_agent

# librarian.toml:1-6 — external OSS/docs; uses host web_search + webfetch
# plan.toml:1-6 — planner only; refuses implementation
# codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17 — binary APPROVE vs REJECTION gate
API Owner
spawn_agent, wait_agent, send_message Codex host
Agent TOML definitions LC components/ultrawork/agents/
Ultrawork workflow directive LC ultrawork-detector.py
OpenCode call_omo_agent, task, tmux orchestration OmO submodule — not Codex path
14

8. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

Surfaces LC owns:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — surfaces, sandbox, config (006–008)

소스 코드 스니펫

161_lazycodex/README.md

🚀 Install

One line. No global install, no npm i -g. Always use bunx:

bunx lazycodex-ai install

This is shorthand for bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex. For a fully autonomous, no-TUI setup:

bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

8. Axis synthesis — surfaces, sandbox, config (006–008)

Surfaces LC owns:

Surface Protocol Role
lazycodex-ai npm bin Process spawn Install/command alias
Codex plugin omo Codex plugin API hooks, skills, MCP
LSP MCP stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 Language intelligence
packages/web HTTPS (Cloudflare Worker) Marketing + docs

Surfaces LC does NOT own: codex-rs/app-server, OpenCode server, IDE JSON-RPC daemons.

Sandbox: LC configures hook subprocess timeouts and PostToolUse decision: block — not seatbelt/bwrap/seccomp. Codex permission_mode in hook stdin is read-only consumption.

Config: No .lazycodexrc. Three layers: (1) zero-parser CLI shim, (2) frozen .agents/plugins/marketplace.json, (3) component env knobs (CODEX_RULES_*, .codex/lsp-client.json, .omo/ultragoal/*.json). Real ~/.codex/config.toml mutations happen inside OmO installer.

15

9. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

Memory taxonomy — LC has no vector index or MEMORY.md pipeline:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — memory & guardian (010, 012)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot?: string): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

9. Axis synthesis — memory & guardian (010, 012)

Memory taxonomy — LC has no vector index or MEMORY.md pipeline:

Layer Path Role
Rules cache ~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/<id>.json Dedupe injected rule keys
Ultragoal ledger .omo/ultragoal/goals.json, ledger.jsonl Repo-native goals + audit
Ultrawork notepad /tmp/ulw-*.md Ephemeral evidence trail
Telemetry dedupe posthog-activity.json Daily event dedupe only 
// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot?: string): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

Guardian layers:

Gate Owner Mechanism
Pre-tool file/shell/MCP approval Codex permission_mode
Comment checker PostToolUse LC decision: "block" (codex-hook.ts:60-65)
LSP diagnostics PostToolUse LC decision: "block" on errors
Ultrawork reviewer LC TOML APPROVE vs REJECTION strings
Ultrawork detector LC exit 0 always — never blocks

hooks.json has no PreToolUse or PermissionRequest events — pre-execution gates are host-native.

16

10. Axis synthesis

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

LC does not ship web-search MCP or browser runtime. Research flows through librarian agent instructions:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. Axis synthesis — web search (014)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:43-49
# Phase 0.5 — use web_search + webfetch for official docs
# gh search / gh api / clone into ${TMPDIR} allowed

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

10. Axis synthesis — web search (014)

LC does not ship web-search MCP or browser runtime. Research flows through librarian agent instructions:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:43-49
# Phase 0.5 — use web_search + webfetch for official docs
# gh search / gh api / clone into ${TMPDIR} allowed

LSP symbols tool with scope: workspace queries local LSP index, not the public internet (tools.ts:619-636).

Verdict: classify lc as host-mediated web/search — librarian orchestrates Codex web_search/webfetch; explorer is read-only local.

17

11. TUI

DEV

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

기초 설명

LC renders zero terminal frames. Codex displays hook statusMessage strings during subprocess execution:

이 문서에서 다루는 것

    1. TUI — host chrome + hook statusMessage (001)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:7-11
"statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

11. TUI — host chrome + hook statusMessage (001)

LC renders zero terminal frames. Codex displays hook statusMessage strings during subprocess execution:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:7-11
"statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"
Hook event statusMessage
SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit rules loading OMO project rules
UserPromptSubmit ultragoal checking OMO ultragoal steering
PostToolUse comment-checker checking OMO comments
PostToolUse lsp checking OMO LSP diagnostics
PostCompact rules resetting OMO project rule cache

--no-tui in README applies to OmO install wizard, not Codex session TUI (README.md:38-41).

18

축 01: TUI 설계

AXIS

001_tui-design/01_lc_tui.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex is a thin distribution wrapper over oh-my-openagent (OmO). It does not ship Ratatui, Ink, blessed, or any terminal frame renderer. The user's interactive session is the Codex CLI TUI; LazyCodex contributes install/bootstrap plumbing, bundled Codex plugin hooks (with statusMessage strings during hook subprocesses), and documentation.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Distribution shim — no runtime TUI
    1. --no-tui is install-time only
    1. Codex plugin hook status messages (host-rendered)
    1. Plugin manifest — interface metadata only
    1. OmO submodule — OpenCode TUI path (reference only)
  • OpenCode install TUI (@clack/prompts)
    1. What the user actually sees on Codex
    1. Negative evidence — grep bar
    1. Hook subprocess I/O model (not a terminal app)
    1. Marketplace packaging (no UI chrome)
    1. Test contract — hooks stay component-isolated

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["User terminal"] --> LC["lazycodex-ai.js shim"]
  LC -->|"bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex"| OMO["OmO installer"]
  OMO --> CX["Codex host TUI<br/>(codex-rs/tui)"]
  CX --> Plugin["plugins/omo hooks"]
  Plugin -->|"statusMessage during hook"| CX
  LC -.->|"no renderer"| NR["❌ no LC-owned TUI loop"]
  OMO_OC["src/ OmO submodule<br/>(OpenCode path only)"] -.->|"ctx.client.tui.showToast"| OC["OpenCode TUI"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-20
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/README.md:79-81
LazyCodex is a thin distribution layer. The core engine is oh-my-openagent (OmO),
included as a submodule under `src/`.

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:38-41
bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:7-11
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/rules/dist/cli.js\" hook session-start",
  "timeout": 10,
  "statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:17-34
"interface": {
  "displayName": "OMO",
  "shortDescription": "Unified local Codex components",
  "longDescription": "OMO exposes the local Codex Rules, Comment Checker, LSP, Ultrawork, and Ultragoal components as one plugin namespace...",
  "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
    "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
    "ulw: run this change with evidence."
  ],
  "brandColor": "#7C3AED"
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/plugin/hooks/create-session-hooks.ts:165-175
toast: async ({ title, message, variant, duration }) => {
  await ctx.client.tui
    .showToast({
      body: {
        title,
        message,
        variant: variant ?? "warning",
        duration: duration ?? 5000,
      },
    })
    .catch(() => {})
},

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts:16-20
export async function runTuiInstaller(args: InstallArgs, version: string): Promise<number> {
  if (!process.stdin.isTTY || !process.stdout.isTTY) {
    console.error("Error: Interactive installer requires a TTY. Use --non-interactive or set environment variables directly.")
    return 1
  }

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:455-462
function formatAdditionalContextOutput(eventName: ContextInjectionHookEventName, additionalContext: string): string {
  if (additionalContext.trim().length === 0) return "";
  return `${JSON.stringify({
    hookSpecificOutput: {
      hookEventName: eventName,
      additionalContext,
    },
  })}\n`;
}

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:285-290
def main() -> None:
    payload = _load_payload()
    if payload is not None and _should_inject(payload):
        _ = sys.stdout.write(ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE)
        _ = sys.stdout.flush()
    sys.exit(0)

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "policy": { "installation": "AVAILABLE", "authentication": "ON_INSTALL" }
  }]
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:38-57
test("#given isolated components #when hooks are inspected #then commands stay inside component roots", async () => {
  const hooks = await readJson("hooks/hooks.json");
  const text = JSON.stringify(hooks);
  const componentMarkers = [
    "components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js",
    "components/lsp/dist/cli.js",
    "components/rules/dist/cli.js",
    // ...
  ];
  for (const marker of componentMarkers) {
    assert.match(text, new RegExp(marker.replaceAll("/", "\\/")));
  }
});

코퍼스 — 001_tui-design/01_lc_tui.md (심화)

LazyCodex (lc) — TUI Design

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ (source checkout). 런타임 TUI 없음 — interactive rendering is entirely delegated to the Codex host (121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tui).

LazyCodex is a thin distribution wrapper over oh-my-openagent (OmO). It does not ship Ratatui, Ink, blessed, or any terminal frame renderer. The user's interactive session is the Codex CLI TUI; LazyCodex contributes install/bootstrap plumbing, bundled Codex plugin hooks (with statusMessage strings during hook subprocesses), and documentation.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["User terminal"] --> LC["lazycodex-ai.js shim"]
  LC -->|"bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex"| OMO["OmO installer"]
  OMO --> CX["Codex host TUI<br/>(codex-rs/tui)"]
  CX --> Plugin["plugins/omo hooks"]
  Plugin -->|"statusMessage during hook"| CX
  LC -.->|"no renderer"| NR["❌ no LC-owned TUI loop"]
  OMO_OC["src/ OmO submodule<br/>(OpenCode path only)"] -.->|"ctx.client.tui.showToast"| OC["OpenCode TUI"]

1. Distribution shim — no runtime TUI

The npm binary is a synchronous bunx forwarder. It never opens alternate screen buffers, handles keyboard input, or draws widgets.

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-20
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})

Key behaviors:

Line range Behavior
:6-7 --dry-run prints the resolved bunx argv without executing
:9-11 install subcommand adds --platform=codex to OmO installer
:18-20 stdio: "inherit" — child owns stdin/stdout/stderr (Codex or OmO TUI if any)
:22-27 Propagates spawn error / exit status; no LC error UI

The README explicitly positions LazyCodex as a distribution layer, not a UI product:

// 161_lazycodex/README.md:79-81
LazyCodex is a thin distribution layer. The core engine is oh-my-openagent (OmO),
included as a submodule under `src/`.

2. --no-tui is install-time only

README documents a non-interactive install path:

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:38-41
bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

This flag applies to the OmO/Codex install wizard, not to suppressing Codex's runtime session TUI. After install, the user still interacts through Codex's native terminal UI when running an agent session.

Contrast: OmO's OpenCode installer uses @clack/prompts for a dedicated install TUI (src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts), which LazyCodex does not invoke on the Codex platform path.

3. Codex plugin hook status messages (host-rendered)

While LazyCodex does not render frames, the bundled plugins/omo plugin registers status messages that Codex displays during hook subprocess execution:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:7-11
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/rules/dist/cli.js\" hook session-start",
  "timeout": 10,
  "statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"
}
Hook event statusMessage (when set) Timeout (s)
SessionStart → rules loading OMO project rules 10
SessionStart → telemetry (none) 5
UserPromptSubmit → rules loading OMO project rules 10
UserPromptSubmit → ultragoal checking OMO ultragoal steering 10
PostToolUse → comment-checker checking OMO comments 30
PostToolUse → lsp checking OMO LSP diagnostics 60
PostToolUse → rules (apply_patch) matching OMO project rules 10
PostCompact → rules resetting OMO project rule cache 10

These strings are host chrome, not LC-owned widgets. Codex decides placement, duration, and styling.

4. Plugin manifest — interface metadata only

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:17-34
"interface": {
  "displayName": "OMO",
  "shortDescription": "Unified local Codex components",
  "longDescription": "OMO exposes the local Codex Rules, Comment Checker, LSP, Ultrawork, and Ultragoal components as one plugin namespace...",
  "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"],
  "defaultPrompt": [
    "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
    "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
    "ulw: run this change with evidence."
  ],
  "brandColor": "#7C3AED"
}

The interface block is marketplace/catalog metadata for Codex plugin discovery — not a TUI theme or layout definition.

5. OmO submodule — OpenCode TUI path (reference only)

LazyCodex vendors OmO under 161_lazycodex/src/ (git submodule). On OpenCode, OmO hooks call host TUI APIs. This path is not active when --platform=codex is used, but documents the delegation pattern LC inherits upstream:

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/plugin/hooks/create-session-hooks.ts:165-175
toast: async ({ title, message, variant, duration }) => {
  await ctx.client.tui
    .showToast({
      body: {
        title,
        message,
        variant: variant ?? "warning",
        duration: duration ?? 5000,
      },
    })
    .catch(() => {})
},

Model-fallback and auto-update hooks use the same ctx.client.tui.showToast channel (:187-195). LazyCodex on Codex does not expose ctx.client.tui — that API belongs to OpenCode's client SDK.

OpenCode install TUI (@clack/prompts)

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts:16-20
export async function runTuiInstaller(args: InstallArgs, version: string): Promise<number> {
  if (!process.stdin.isTTY || !process.stdout.isTTY) {
    console.error("Error: Interactive installer requires a TTY. Use --non-interactive or set environment variables directly.")
    return 1
  }

OpenCode installer: spinners, intro/outro banners, config prompts. Codex install via lazycodex-ai install delegates to OmO's Codex-specific installer (no @clack in the LC shim itself).

6. What the user actually sees on Codex

Layer UI owner LC contribution
Session loop (chat, tool cards, approvals) Codex codex-rs/tui None
Plugin hook progress text Codex host (from statusMessage) hooks/hooks.json strings
MCP tool invocations Codex host LSP MCP stdio server registration
Install/bootstrap OmO via bunx lazycodex-ai.js argv forwarding
Marketing/docs site Next.js on Cloudflare packages/web/ (browser, not TUI)

Interactive agent UX depth lives in 001_tui-design/01_co_tui.md. LazyCodex adds workflow hooks and skills; it does not fork or patch Codex's renderer.

7. Negative evidence — grep bar

Searched 161_lazycodex/ for common TUI libraries and render loops:

Pattern Finding in LC-owned paths
ratatui, crossterm, ink, blessed Not in bin/, plugins/omo/
Native readline REPL in LC shim Only --dry-run echo (lazycodex-ai.js:13-15)
Frame buffer / alternate screen Absent
Keyboard event handlers in plugin hooks Hooks are stdin-JSON → stdout-JSON/text subprocesses

Hook CLIs (components/*/dist/cli.js) are one-shot subprocesses, not persistent TUI apps.

8. Hook subprocess I/O model (not a terminal app)

Rules hook emits JSON on stdout for Codex to parse:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:455-462
function formatAdditionalContextOutput(eventName: ContextInjectionHookEventName, additionalContext: string): string {
  if (additionalContext.trim().length === 0) return "";
  return `${JSON.stringify({
    hookSpecificOutput: {
      hookEventName: eventName,
      additionalContext,
    },
  })}\n`;
}

Ultrawork detector writes plain-text directive to stdout (Codex treats non-JSON stdout as additional_context):

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:285-290
def main() -> None:
    payload = _load_payload()
    if payload is not None and _should_inject(payload):
        _ = sys.stdout.write(ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE)
        _ = sys.stdout.flush()
    sys.exit(0)

No curses/ANSI layout — only structured hook output consumed by the host TUI layer.

9. Marketplace packaging (no UI chrome)

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "policy": { "installation": "AVAILABLE", "authentication": "ON_INSTALL" }
  }]
}

Local Codex marketplace snapshot; does not define terminal themes or layout.

10. Test contract — hooks stay component-isolated

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:38-57
test("#given isolated components #when hooks are inspected #then commands stay inside component roots", async () => {
  const hooks = await readJson("hooks/hooks.json");
  const text = JSON.stringify(hooks);
  const componentMarkers = [
    "components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js",
    "components/lsp/dist/cli.js",
    "components/rules/dist/cli.js",
    // ...
  ];
  for (const marker of componentMarkers) {
    assert.match(text, new RegExp(marker.replaceAll("/", "\\/")));
  }
});

Aggregate tests verify hook command paths — subprocess invocation, not UI rendering.

11. Parity matrix — TUI axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc) OmO OpenCode path
Renderer Ratatui interactive loop in codex-rs/tui None — shim only OpenCode client TUI
Install UX codex binary bunx lazycodex-ai install → OmO @clack/prompts in tui-installer.ts
Toast/status Native widgets Hook statusMessage only ctx.client.tui.showToast
Keyboard input Host handles Host handles Host handles
--no-tui N/A (Codex flags differ) Install wizard flag only OmO install flag
Evidence path 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tui/ 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:1-27 161_lazycodex/src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts

12. Design boundary

LazyCodex never draws frames, manages cursor state, or handles keyboard input. Any perceived "TUI feature" is either:

  1. Codex host — chat loop, tool rendering, approvals, compaction UI
  2. Hook side-effects — statusMessage during subprocess hooks
  3. OmO OpenCode (upstream submodule) — showToast, install wizard — not on Codex platform path

Do not document LC as having a TUI crate or render loop. Cross-reference 01_co_tui.md for interactive depth.

13. Operational workflow (user perspective)

  1. User runs bunx lazycodex-ai install (or install --no-tui --codex-autonomous)
  2. OmO installs Codex plugin marketplace entry + omo plugin under ~/.codex/
  3. User starts codex (or IDE integration) — Codex TUI renders the session
  4. On session start, OMO rules hook runs; user may briefly see loading OMO project rules
  5. User types prompt; optional ulw/ultrawork triggers ultrawork directive injection
  6. All subsequent UI — tool cards, diffs, MCP panels — is Codex-native

14. Error handling — shim layer

Failure Behavior Lines
bunx not found result.error.message to stderr, exit 1 lazycodex-ai.js:22-24
OmO install failure Child exit status propagated lazycodex-ai.js:27
Hook timeout Codex host cancels subprocess hooks.json per-hook timeout
Hook stderr Host logging; no LC overlay N/A

LC shim does not retry failed installs or present interactive error menus.

15. Evidence quality bar

  • Primary paths under 161_lazycodex/bin/ and plugins/omo/hooks/ with line numbers
  • Negative claim documented: no native TUI renderer in LC
  • OpenCode delegation pattern cited from src/ submodule for completeness
  • Hook statusMessage inventory from hooks.json
  • Parity table vs Codex host TUI

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 6-27 bunx forwarder, install platform flag
161_lazycodex/README.md 38-41, 79-81 --no-tui, thin distribution claim
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 3-97 Hook commands, timeouts, statusMessage
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 14-34 Plugin manifest, interface metadata
161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json 1-16 Local marketplace snapshot
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts 455-462 Hook stdout JSON format
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py 285-290 Plain-text hook output
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs 38-57 Hook path isolation tests
161_lazycodex/src/src/plugin/hooks/create-session-hooks.ts 165-175 OpenCode showToast (reference)
161_lazycodex/src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts 16-20 OpenCode install TUI TTY gate

Related corpus docs

  • Codex TUI depth: 001_tui-design/01_co_tui.md
  • LazyCodex overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md
  • Prompt injection (hook context): 002_prompt-context/02_lc_prompt.md
  • App surfaces (web, not TUI): 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_lc_surface.md
  • OmO upstream: 172_oh-my-openagent/

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP38: initial stub with citations
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — full TUI delegation analysis with path:line

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Runtime TUI None — Codex host owned Re-verify if LC adds native renderer
Install --no-tui OmO install flag only OmO installer flags may evolve
Hook statusMessage Listed from hooks.json Strings/timeouts may change per OmO release
OpenCode toast API Cited from submodule OpenCode SDK drift independent of LC
Codex TUI Independent crate See 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/tui for host changes
19

축 02: 프롬프트·컨텍스트

AXIS

002_prompt-context/02_lc_prompt.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex does not assemble provider messages, call models directly, or implement compaction. Its prompt/context surface is the bundled plugins/omo Codex plugin: lifecycle hooks that emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext, keyword-triggered ultrawork directives, ultragoal steering, lazy-loaded skills, and LSP MCP tool context when invoked.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Distribution boundary — no LC-owned system prompt
    1. Hook registry — context injection events
    1. Rules component — static context injection
  • SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit
  • Transcript deduplication
  • PostToolUse — dynamic context
  • PostCompact — cache reset
    1. Ultrawork keyword directive
    1. Ultragoal steering
    1. Post-edit context — block decisions with additionalContext
  • Comment checker

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["User prompt"] --> CX["Codex host prompt assembly"]
  CX --> H1["SessionStart hooks"]
  CX --> H2["UserPromptSubmit hooks"]
  CX --> H3["PostToolUse hooks"]
  CX --> H4["PostCompact hooks"]
  H1 --> Rules["rules: static context"]
  H2 --> Rules
  H2 --> ULW["ultrawork-detector.py"]
  H2 --> UG["ultragoal steering"]
  H3 --> RulesD["rules: dynamic context"]
  H3 --> CC["comment-checker block"]
  H3 --> LSP["LSP diagnostics block"]
  H4 --> RulesR["rules: cache reset"]
  Rules --> AC["additionalContext JSON"]
  ULW --> AC2["plain-text directive"]
  UG --> AC
  Skills["skills/ SKILL.md"] -.->|"lazy load on invoke"| CX
  MCP["LSP MCP tools"] -.->|"tool turn context"| CX

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14-16,23
"skills": "./skills/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
"capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:3-97
"SessionStart": [ rules session-start, telemetry session-start ],
"UserPromptSubmit": [ rules user-prompt-submit, ultrawork-detector.py, ultragoal user-prompt-submit ],
"PostToolUse": [ comment-checker + lsp (edit matchers), rules post-tool-use (apply_patch) ],
"PostCompact": [ rules post-compact (manual|auto) ]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:82-102
export async function runSessionStartHook(
  input: CodexSessionStartInput,
  options: CodexRulesHookOptions = {},
): Promise<string> {
  const cachePath = sessionCachePath(input.session_id, options.pluginDataRoot);
  if (input.source === "clear") {
    clearSessionState(cachePath);
  } else if (input.source !== "resume" && !hasPostCompactPending(cachePath)) {
    clearSessionState(cachePath);
  }
  // ...
  return runStaticInjection(input.cwd, transcriptPath, "SessionStart", cachePath, options, ...);
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:455-462
return `${JSON.stringify({
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: eventName,
    additionalContext,
  },
})}\n`;

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:276-287
function isRuleAlreadyInTranscript(rule: LoadedRule, transcriptText: string): boolean {
  const bodyNeedle = rule.body.trim().slice(0, 2_000);
  if (bodyNeedle.length === 0 || !transcriptText.includes(bodyNeedle)) {
    return false;
  }
  const markers = [
    `Instructions from: ${rule.path}`,
    `Instructions from: ${rule.realPath}`,
    rule.relativePath.length === 0 ? null : rule.relativePath,
  ].filter((marker): marker is string => marker !== null);
  return markers.some((marker) => transcriptText.includes(marker));
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:129-139
export async function runPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const config = configFromEnvironment(options.env);
  if (config.disabled || config.mode === "off" || config.mode === "static") {
    return "";
  }
  const targetPaths = extractCodexToolPaths(input, input.cwd);
  // fingerprint changed files → loadDynamicRules → formatDynamic
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:104-110
export async function runPostCompactHook(...) {
  markSessionCompacted(sessionCachePath(input.session_id, options.pluginDataRoot));
  return "";
}

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:276-282
def _should_inject(payload: dict[str, object]) -> bool:
    if payload.get("hook_event_name") != "UserPromptSubmit":
        return False
    prompt = payload.get("prompt")
    if not isinstance(prompt, str) or not prompt:
        return False
    return ULTRAWORK_PATTERN.search(prompt) is not None

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/codex-hook.ts:25-37
export async function applyUserPromptUltragoalSteering(payload: UserPromptSubmitPayload): Promise<string> {
  if (payload.hook_event_name !== "UserPromptSubmit") return "";
  const proposal = parseUltragoalSteeringDirective(payload.prompt);
  if (proposal === null) return "";
  const result = await steerUltragoal(payload.cwd, proposal);
  if (!result.accepted) return "";
  return JSON.stringify({
    status: "accepted",
    kind: result.audit.kind,
    source: result.audit.source,
    deduped: result.deduped,
  });
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:60-65
return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:55-63
const output: PostToolUseHookOutput = {
  decision: "block",
  reason,
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: "PostToolUse",
    additionalContext: reason,
  },
};

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:9-14
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:16-30
const opencodeOnlyOrchestrationPattern = /\b(?:call_omo_agent|background_output|team_[a-z_]+|task)\s*\(/;
// Maps call_omo_agent → spawn_agent, task → spawn_agent, background_output → wait_agent, etc.

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-9
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
assert.equal(manifest.name, "omo");
assert.equal(hookPath, "./hooks/hooks.json");
assert.equal(skillsPath, "./skills/");

코퍼스 — 002_prompt-context/02_lc_prompt.md (심화)

LazyCodex — Prompt / Context Assembly

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ + OmO src/ submodule — no local LLM loop; context is injected via Codex plugin hooks into the host-managed prompt.

LazyCodex does not assemble provider messages, call models directly, or implement compaction. Its prompt/context surface is the bundled plugins/omo Codex plugin: lifecycle hooks that emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext, keyword-triggered ultrawork directives, ultragoal steering, lazy-loaded skills, and LSP MCP tool context when invoked.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["User prompt"] --> CX["Codex host prompt assembly"]
  CX --> H1["SessionStart hooks"]
  CX --> H2["UserPromptSubmit hooks"]
  CX --> H3["PostToolUse hooks"]
  CX --> H4["PostCompact hooks"]
  H1 --> Rules["rules: static context"]
  H2 --> Rules
  H2 --> ULW["ultrawork-detector.py"]
  H2 --> UG["ultragoal steering"]
  H3 --> RulesD["rules: dynamic context"]
  H3 --> CC["comment-checker block"]
  H3 --> LSP["LSP diagnostics block"]
  H4 --> RulesR["rules: cache reset"]
  Rules --> AC["additionalContext JSON"]
  ULW --> AC2["plain-text directive"]
  UG --> AC
  Skills["skills/ SKILL.md"] -.->|"lazy load on invoke"| CX
  MCP["LSP MCP tools"] -.->|"tool turn context"| CX

1. Distribution boundary — no LC-owned system prompt

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

The npm package forwards to OmO; it never reads AGENTS.md, never tokenizes, never merges chat history. All prompt assembly is Codex host + plugin hooks.

Plugin manifest declares context capabilities:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14-16,23
"skills": "./skills/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
"capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"]

2. Hook registry — context injection events

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:3-97
"SessionStart": [ rules session-start, telemetry session-start ],
"UserPromptSubmit": [ rules user-prompt-submit, ultrawork-detector.py, ultragoal user-prompt-submit ],
"PostToolUse": [ comment-checker + lsp (edit matchers), rules post-tool-use (apply_patch) ],
"PostCompact": [ rules post-compact (manual|auto) ]
Event Components Context type
SessionStart rules, telemetry Static rules → additionalContext
UserPromptSubmit rules, ultrawork, ultragoal Static rules + keyword directive + goal steering
PostToolUse comment-checker, lsp, rules Block decisions + dynamic rules
PostCompact rules Session cache reset (no context emit)

Telemetry hook on SessionStart tracks activity (PostHog) — not prompt text (hooks.json:14-22).

3. Rules component — static context injection

SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:82-102
export async function runSessionStartHook(
  input: CodexSessionStartInput,
  options: CodexRulesHookOptions = {},
): Promise<string> {
  const cachePath = sessionCachePath(input.session_id, options.pluginDataRoot);
  if (input.source === "clear") {
    clearSessionState(cachePath);
  } else if (input.source !== "resume" && !hasPostCompactPending(cachePath)) {
    clearSessionState(cachePath);
  }
  // ...
  return runStaticInjection(input.cwd, transcriptPath, "SessionStart", cachePath, options, ...);
}

Static injection pipeline (:212-247):

  1. Read config from environment — skip if disabled, mode === "off", or mode === "dynamic" (:220-223)
  2. engine.loadStaticRules(cwd) discovers project/user/bundled instruction files
  3. Filter rules already present in transcript (filterRulesAlreadyInTranscript, :250-274)
  4. Format block via engine.formatStatic(rules) (:242)
  5. Mark injected rules in session cache to prevent duplicates
  6. Emit JSON:
// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:455-462
return `${JSON.stringify({
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: eventName,
    additionalContext,
  },
})}\n`;

Transcript deduplication

Rules avoid re-injecting content already in the session transcript:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:276-287
function isRuleAlreadyInTranscript(rule: LoadedRule, transcriptText: string): boolean {
  const bodyNeedle = rule.body.trim().slice(0, 2_000);
  if (bodyNeedle.length === 0 || !transcriptText.includes(bodyNeedle)) {
    return false;
  }
  const markers = [
    `Instructions from: ${rule.path}`,
    `Instructions from: ${rule.realPath}`,
    rule.relativePath.length === 0 ? null : rule.relativePath,
  ].filter((marker): marker is string => marker !== null);
  return markers.some((marker) => transcriptText.includes(marker));
}

Transcript parsing also extracts JSON-line string values for nested content search (:299-314).

PostToolUse — dynamic context

After edit-like tools, rules inject file-targeted dynamic context:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:129-139
export async function runPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const config = configFromEnvironment(options.env);
  if (config.disabled || config.mode === "off" || config.mode === "static") {
    return "";
  }
  const targetPaths = extractCodexToolPaths(input, input.cwd);
  // fingerprint changed files → loadDynamicRules → formatDynamic
}

Dynamic mode skips static-only configs; static mode skips dynamic post-edit injection — modes are mutually exclusive at runtime.

PostCompact — cache reset

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts:104-110
export async function runPostCompactHook(...) {
  markSessionCompacted(sessionCachePath(input.session_id, options.pluginDataRoot));
  return "";
}

After compaction, pending re-injection channels are flagged so static rules can re-apply on next prompt (isPostCompactPending, used at :117, :154).

4. Ultrawork keyword directive

Python hook on UserPromptSubmit — word-bounded keyword match:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

Contract documented in module docstring (:4-10):

  • stdin: JSON with prompt, hook_event_name, session fields
  • stdout: Full ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE text when matched; empty otherwise
  • exit: Always 0 — never blocks the turn (:10, :290)

Injection gate:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:276-282
def _should_inject(payload: dict[str, object]) -> bool:
    if payload.get("hook_event_name") != "UserPromptSubmit":
        return False
    prompt = payload.get("prompt")
    if not isinstance(prompt, str) or not prompt:
        return False
    return ULTRAWORK_PATTERN.search(prompt) is not None

The directive (ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE, :25-256) is a large structured block covering:

  • Mandatory first-line banner: ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLED!
  • Role/goal constraints (evidence-driven, test-first + manual QA channels)
  • Manual-QA channels: HTTP, tmux, browser use, computer use
  • Bootstrap requirements: goal creation, parallel subagents, reviewer gate
  • Stop/retry rules

Codex treats non-JSON stdout as raw additional_context — no hookSpecificOutput wrapper for this hook.

5. Ultragoal steering

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/codex-hook.ts:25-37
export async function applyUserPromptUltragoalSteering(payload: UserPromptSubmitPayload): Promise<string> {
  if (payload.hook_event_name !== "UserPromptSubmit") return "";
  const proposal = parseUltragoalSteeringDirective(payload.prompt);
  if (proposal === null) return "";
  const result = await steerUltragoal(payload.cwd, proposal);
  if (!result.accepted) return "";
  return JSON.stringify({
    status: "accepted",
    kind: result.audit.kind,
    source: result.audit.source,
    deduped: result.deduped,
  });
}

Ultragoal mutates durable .omo/ultragoal plan/ledger state in the workspace when steering directives are accepted. Hook registered at hooks.json:44-52 with statusMessage: "checking OMO ultragoal steering".

Unlike ultrawork, ultragoal output is structured JSON status — not a large prompt block — but accepted steering affects subsequent turns via persisted goal files.

6. Post-edit context — block decisions with additionalContext

Comment checker

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:60-65
return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

Runs on mutation tools matched in hooks.json:57 — injects block reason when AI-slop comments detected.

LSP diagnostics

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:55-63
const output: PostToolUseHookOutput = {
  decision: "block",
  reason,
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: "PostToolUse",
    additionalContext: reason,
  },
};

Blocks turn when LSP reports errors after edits (MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES, :27; executeLspDiagnostics with severity: "error", :31-33).

These are prompt-adjacent — they can halt the tool loop and surface diagnostic text as context for the model's next turn.

7. Skills — lazy prompt bodies

Skills directory is synced from components + shared skills:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:9-14
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

Aggregate inventory (checked-in plugins/omo/skills/):

Skill Source
comment-checker, lsp, rules, ultragoal Component-local
programming, debugging, frontend-ui-ux, refactor, review-work Shared (when present)
remove-ai-slops, ai-slop-remover Shared cleanup skills

Skills are exposed via manifest "skills": "./skills/" — Codex lazy-loads SKILL.md bodies when the model or user invokes a skill; they are not prepended to every system prompt.

Codex compatibility rewrite

OpenCode-only orchestration examples get a compatibility preamble:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:16-30
const opencodeOnlyOrchestrationPattern = /\b(?:call_omo_agent|background_output|team_[a-z_]+|task)\s*\(/;
// Maps call_omo_agent → spawn_agent, task → spawn_agent, background_output → wait_agent, etc.

Inserted after YAML frontmatter when pattern matches (:36-45).

8. LSP MCP — tool context (not base prompt)

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-9
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

LSP tools (diagnostics, goto_definition, rename, etc.) contribute context when the model invokes them — not at session start. See 003_tool-runtime/03_lc_tool.md.

9. Context channels summary

Channel Trigger Output shape Blocks turn?
Rules static SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext No
Rules dynamic PostToolUse (edits) hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext No
Ultrawork UserPromptSubmit (ulw/ultrawork) Plain-text directive No
Ultragoal UserPromptSubmit (steering syntax) JSON status No
Comment checker PostToolUse (edits) decision: block Yes
LSP hook PostToolUse (edits) decision: block + additionalContext Yes
Skills On invoke SKILL.md body No
LSP MCP On tool call Tool result messages No

10. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Feature Owner
System prompt template Codex host
Message list / roles Codex host
Compaction algorithm Codex host (PostCompact hook notifies rules only)
Token counting Codex host
Model API request shape Codex host
Provider routing Codex host (+ OmO upstream for OpenCode)

OmO submodule (161_lazycodex/src/) contains OpenCode-specific transforms (messages-transform.ts, system-transform.ts, session-compacting.ts) — not exercised on Codex platform install path.

11. Parity matrix — prompt/context axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
Base system prompt core prompt assembly Host-owned
Project rules AGENTS.md, skills in codex-rs OMO rules hook + skills dir
Keyword modes Custom instructions / config ultrawork/ulw detector
Goal steering Built-in goals (if enabled) ultragoal .omo/ files
Post-edit gates Policy / guardian comment-checker + LSP blocks
Compaction hook Host compaction rules cache reset on PostCompact
Lazy skills Codex skills API plugins/omo/skills/

12. Security / privacy notes

  • Rules content: Arbitrary project instruction files merged into model context — trust model boundary
  • Ultrawork directive: Large injected block changes agent behavior significantly — keyword-gated only
  • Telemetry: PostHog on SessionStart — not prompt text (components/telemetry/)
  • Transcript reads: Rules hook reads transcript JSONL for dedup — local filesystem only

13. Test coverage

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
assert.equal(manifest.name, "omo");
assert.equal(hookPath, "./hooks/hooks.json");
assert.equal(skillsPath, "./skills/");

sync-skills.test.mjs verifies aggregate skill inventory and Codex compatibility block injection.

14. Operational workflow

  1. Session starts → rules static context injected (project AGENTS-style files)
  2. User submits prompt → rules re-evaluated; if ulw present, ultrawork directive appended
  3. Ultragoal steering parsed; may update .omo/ultragoal ledger
  4. Model acts with merged host + hook context
  5. After edits → LSP/comment-checker may block; rules may inject file-specific dynamic context
  6. On compaction → rules cache reset; static rules re-inject on next turn

15. Evidence quality bar

  • Rules hook static/dynamic/compact paths with line numbers
  • Ultrawork detector contract and keyword pattern
  • Ultragoal steering flow
  • Block hooks (comment-checker, LSP) with JSON shapes
  • Skills sync and compatibility rewrite
  • Negative claims: no LC-owned LLM loop

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 14-16, 23 skills/hooks/MCP manifest

Related corpus docs

  • TUI (host renders injected context): 001_tui-design/01_lc_tui.md
  • Tool runtime: 003_tool-runtime/03_lc_tool.md
  • Skills depth: 004_skill-system/04_lc_skill.md
  • Subagents (ultrawork spawns): 005_subagents-collab/05_lc_subagent.md
  • Codex prompt assembly: 002_prompt-context/02_co_prompt.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP38: initial stub
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — full hook/context path:line analysis

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Hook events hooks.json as checked in OmO may add/rename hooks per release
Ultrawork directive text ultrawork-detector.py body Directive content evolves with OmO
Rules modes static/dynamic/off via env Config keys may expand
Skills inventory 11 skills in plugins/omo/skills sync-skills.mjs regenerates on build
Compaction PostCompact rules reset only Codex compaction API independent
OpenCode transforms src/ submodule present Not on Codex platform path
20

축 03: 도구 런타임

AXIS

003_tool-runtime/03_lc_tool.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex has no LLM tool loop, permission engine, or sandbox. Runtime layers: (1) lazycodex-ai.js CLI forwarder, (2) Codex plugin hook commands, (3) LSP MCP JSON-RPC over stdio. Tool dispatch, streaming, and approval UX remain Codex-owned.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. CLI layer — not a tool runtime
    1. Plugin workspace — component isolation
    1. Hook subprocess registry
    1. LSP MCP server — the only LC-registered MCP
    1. MCP JSON-RPC implementation
    1. LSP MCP tool catalog
    1. Language server subprocess spawning
    1. Workspace edit application (rename tool)
    1. PostToolUse quality hooks — pseudo-tools
  • Comment checker
  • LSP diagnostics hook

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  Model["Codex model turn"] --> Host["Codex tool runtime"]
  Host --> Builtin["Host builtins<br/>(bash, apply_patch, spawn_agent, …)"]
  Host --> MCP["MCP client"]
  MCP --> LSP["lsp MCP stdio server"]
  Host --> Hooks["PostToolUse hooks"]
  Hooks --> CC["comment-checker"]
  Hooks --> LSPH["lsp diagnostics hook"]
  Hooks --> Rules["rules dynamic"]
  LSP --> Tools["lsp_diagnostics, lsp_rename, …"]
  LSP --> LSProc["Language server subprocess"]
  Shim["lazycodex-ai.js"] -.->|"install only"| Host

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:18-20
const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker",
  "components/rules",
  "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp",
  "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal",
  "components/ultrawork"
],
"scripts": {
  "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
  "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:57
"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$"

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-9
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
