README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70
assert.equal(server.command, "node");
assert.deepEqual(server.args, ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"]);
assert.equal(server.cwd, ".");

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:44-76
const SERVER_NAME = "lsp";
const SERVER_VERSION = "0.1.0";
const DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10 * 60_000;

export async function handleLspMcpRequest(input: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse | undefined> {
  // ...
  if (method === "initialize") {
    return successResponse(id, {
      capabilities: { tools: { listChanged: false } },
      serverInfo: { name: SERVER_NAME, version: SERVER_VERSION },
      protocolVersion,
    });
  }
  if (method === "tools/list") {
    return successResponse(id, { tools: LSP_MCP_TOOLS.map(describeTool) });
  }
  if (method === "tools/call") {
    return handleToolCall(id, input["params"]);
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:547-549
const tool = LSP_MCP_TOOLS.find((candidate) => matchesToolName(candidate, name));
if (!tool) throw new Error(`Unknown LSP tool: ${name}`);
return tool.execute(params, signal);

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:24-28,148-161
export interface PreparedSpawnCommand {
  command: string;
  args: string[];
  shell: false;  // explicit — no shell interpolation
}
// ...
if (platform !== "win32") {
  return { command: cmd, args, shell: false };
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/workspace-edit.ts:59-79
export function applyWorkspaceEdit(edit: WorkspaceEdit | null): ApplyResult {
  if (!edit) {
    return { success: false, filesModified: [], totalEdits: 0, errors: ["No edit provided"] };
  }
  if (edit.changes) {
    for (const [uri, edits] of Object.entries(edit.changes)) {
      const filePath = uriToPath(uri);
      const applyResult = applyTextEditsToFile(filePath, edits);
      // ...
    }
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:35-65
export async function runCommentCheckerPostToolUse(...) {
  const requests = extractCodexCommentCheckRequests(input);
  if (requests.length === 0) return "";
  // ...
  return JSON.stringify({
    decision: "block",
    reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
  });
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:27-33,36-48
const MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["apply_patch", "write", "edit", "multiedit", "multi_edit"]);

export async function runLspPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const filePaths = extractMutatedFilePaths(input);
  if (filePaths.length === 0) return "";
  // run diagnostics per file; block if errors remain
}

README / docs

src/src/tools/index.ts
src/src/tools/skill/tools.ts
src/src/tools/delegate-task/
src/src/tools/call-omo-agent/
src/src/plugin/tool-registry.ts

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-71
// MCP lsp server path isolation

// 161_lazycodex/src/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/test/mcp.test.ts (submodule)
// JSON-RPC request/response unit tests

코퍼스 — 003_tool-runtime/03_lc_tool.md (심화)

LazyCodex — Tool Runtime

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ — no standalone tool dispatcher; Codex host executes tools; LC packages hook subprocesses and one LSP MCP stdio server.

LazyCodex has no LLM tool loop, permission engine, or sandbox. Runtime layers: (1) lazycodex-ai.js CLI forwarder, (2) Codex plugin hook commands, (3) LSP MCP JSON-RPC over stdio. Tool dispatch, streaming, and approval UX remain Codex-owned.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  Model["Codex model turn"] --> Host["Codex tool runtime"]
  Host --> Builtin["Host builtins<br/>(bash, apply_patch, spawn_agent, …)"]
  Host --> MCP["MCP client"]
  MCP --> LSP["lsp MCP stdio server"]
  Host --> Hooks["PostToolUse hooks"]
  Hooks --> CC["comment-checker"]
  Hooks --> LSPH["lsp diagnostics hook"]
  Hooks --> Rules["rules dynamic"]
  LSP --> Tools["lsp_diagnostics, lsp_rename, …"]
  LSP --> LSProc["Language server subprocess"]
  Shim["lazycodex-ai.js"] -.->|"install only"| Host

1. CLI layer — not a tool runtime

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:18-20
const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})

The npm bin forwards argv to oh-my-openagent via bunx. No tool registry, no JSON-RPC server, no model API. Install path adds --platform=codex (:9-10).

2. Plugin workspace — component isolation

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker",
  "components/rules",
  "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp",
  "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal",
  "components/ultrawork"
],
"scripts": {
  "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
  "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
}

Each component builds a dist/cli.js hook entrypoint. Aggregate tests enforce hook commands reference components/*/dist/ paths, not external npm package names (aggregate.test.mjs:54-57).

3. Hook subprocess registry

Full map from hooks/hooks.json:

Event Matcher Command Timeout
SessionStart — rules hook session-start 10s
SessionStart — telemetry hook session-start 5s
UserPromptSubmit — rules hook user-prompt-submit 10s
UserPromptSubmit — ultrawork-detector.py 5s
UserPromptSubmit — ultragoal hook user-prompt-submit 10s
PostToolUse edit tools regex comment-checker hook post-tool-use 30s
PostToolUse edit tools regex lsp hook post-tool-use 60s
PostToolUse ^apply_patch$ rules hook post-tool-use 10s
PostCompact manual|auto rules hook post-compact 10s

PostToolUse matcher for quality hooks:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:57
"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$"

Hooks are not tools the model calls — Codex invokes them on lifecycle events. They can return decision: "block" to interrupt the tool loop.

4. LSP MCP server — the only LC-registered MCP

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-9
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

Aggregate test locks this contract:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70
assert.equal(server.command, "node");
assert.deepEqual(server.args, ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"]);
assert.equal(server.cwd, ".");

No Exa, Tavily, browser, or filesystem MCP servers in LC snapshot — LSP only.

5. MCP JSON-RPC implementation

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:44-76
const SERVER_NAME = "lsp";
const SERVER_VERSION = "0.1.0";
const DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10 * 60_000;

export async function handleLspMcpRequest(input: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse | undefined> {
  // ...
  if (method === "initialize") {
    return successResponse(id, {
      capabilities: { tools: { listChanged: false } },
      serverInfo: { name: SERVER_NAME, version: SERVER_VERSION },
      protocolVersion,
    });
  }
  if (method === "tools/list") {
    return successResponse(id, { tools: LSP_MCP_TOOLS.map(describeTool) });
  }
  if (method === "tools/call") {
    return handleToolCall(id, input["params"]);
  }
}

Stdio server loop with idle timeout (runMcpStdioServer, :78-100):

  • Default idle shutdown: 10 minutes (DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS)
  • Handles notifications/initialized, ping, tool list/call
  • Unknown methods → -32601 Method not found

6. LSP MCP tool catalog

Registered tools (tools.ts:560-669):

MCP name Aliases Mutates FS?
status lsp_status No
diagnostics lsp_diagnostics No
goto_definition lsp_goto_definition No
find_references lsp_find_references No
symbols lsp_symbols No
prepare_rename lsp_prepare_rename No
rename lsp_rename Yes — applies workspace edit

Tool dispatch:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:547-549
const tool = LSP_MCP_TOOLS.find((candidate) => matchesToolName(candidate, name));
if (!tool) throw new Error(`Unknown LSP tool: ${name}`);
return tool.execute(params, signal);

rename description explicitly states it applies workspace edits (:656-657) — filesystem mutation via LSP protocol, not Codex apply_patch.

7. Language server subprocess spawning

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:24-28,148-161
export interface PreparedSpawnCommand {
  command: string;
  args: string[];
  shell: false;  // explicit — no shell interpolation
}
// ...
if (platform !== "win32") {
  return { command: cmd, args, shell: false };
}

Spawn preparation:

  • Validates cwd exists and is directory (validateCwd, :41-57)
  • Windows: resolves .cmd/.bat shims without shell: true (:112-172)
  • Process tree kill on shutdown (killProcessTree, :89-110)
  • Not a sandbox — runs configured LSP commands from project/user LSP config

8. Workspace edit application (rename tool)

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/workspace-edit.ts:59-79
export function applyWorkspaceEdit(edit: WorkspaceEdit | null): ApplyResult {
  if (!edit) {
    return { success: false, filesModified: [], totalEdits: 0, errors: ["No edit provided"] };
  }
  if (edit.changes) {
    for (const [uri, edits] of Object.entries(edit.changes)) {
      const filePath = uriToPath(uri);
      const applyResult = applyTextEditsToFile(filePath, edits);
      // ...
    }
  }
}

applyTextEditsToFile reads/writes files directly (:19-48). Also handles documentChanges create/rename/delete (:81+). This is the highest-risk tool surface in LC — relies on Codex MCP approval + host sandbox.

9. PostToolUse quality hooks — pseudo-tools

Comment checker

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:35-65
export async function runCommentCheckerPostToolUse(...) {
  const requests = extractCodexCommentCheckRequests(input);
  if (requests.length === 0) return "";
  // ...
  return JSON.stringify({
    decision: "block",
    reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
  });
}

Extracts file paths from Codex tool input/response (extractCodexCommentCheckRequests, :31-33). Blocks when AI-slop comment patterns detected.

LSP diagnostics hook

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:27-33,36-48
const MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["apply_patch", "write", "edit", "multiedit", "multi_edit"]);

export async function runLspPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const filePaths = extractMutatedFilePaths(input);
  if (filePaths.length === 0) return "";
  // run diagnostics per file; block if errors remain
}

Uses same underlying executeLspDiagnostics as MCP tool (:31-33) but triggered automatically post-edit — model did not invoke the tool explicitly.

10. Rules hook — context not dispatch

Rules PostToolUse emits additionalContext for matched files — does not register as an MCP tool or model-callable function. See 002_prompt-context/02_lc_prompt.md.

11. Ultrawork / ultragoal — no tool registration

  • ultrawork-detector.py — stdout context injection only; exit 0 always
  • ultragoal — may mutate .omo/ goal files via steering CLI; not in MCP registry

Subagent spawn tools (spawn_agent, wait_agent) are Codex host builtins, not LC-registered. LC bundles TOML agent role definitions under components/ultrawork/agents/ for host consumption.

12. OmO submodule tool registry (OpenCode — reference)

Upstream OmO in 161_lazycodex/src/ implements full tool factories:

src/src/tools/index.ts
src/src/tools/skill/tools.ts
src/src/tools/delegate-task/
src/src/tools/call-omo-agent/
src/src/plugin/tool-registry.ts

These register OpenCode-native tools (call_omo_agent, task, background_output). Not active on Codex platform — LC Codex path uses host tools + LSP MCP only. Skill sync rewrites OpenCode examples to Codex equivalents (sync-skills.mjs:18-30).

13. Runtime layers table

Layer LC-owned? Mechanism
LLM tool loop No Codex host
Builtin tools (bash, patch, agents) No Codex host
MCP client No Codex host loads .mcp.json
LSP MCP server Yes (plugin snapshot) stdio JSON-RPC
Lifecycle hooks Yes subprocess CLI per event
Tool permissions / guardian No Codex host
Hook block decisions Yes JSON decision: "block"

14. Parity matrix — tool runtime

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
Tool registry core + MCP client Host + LSP MCP only
Shell execution shell tool + sandbox Host-owned
Subagent dispatch spawn_agent, task Host + bundled TOML roles
Post-edit lint Optional / policy LSP + comment-checker hooks
Web/search MCP Host / other plugins Not bundled
LSP intelligence Optional First-class LSP MCP + hook

15. Error handling

Failure Behavior Source
Unknown MCP tool Error: Unknown LSP tool tools.ts:548
Invalid JSON-RPC -32600 Invalid Request mcp.ts:51
Missing method -32601 Method not found mcp.ts:75
LSP spawn failure LspProcessSpawnError process.ts:156-178
Invalid cwd LspInvalidPathError process.ts:176-178
Hook timeout Codex cancels subprocess hooks.json timeouts
Comment check error Skipped (no block) comment-checker codex-hook.ts:53
MCP idle timeout Server closes after 10 min mcp.ts:46,96-100

16. Security notes

  • LSP config trust: User/project LSP server commands execute with shell: false but full user privileges
  • lsp.rename: Can modify multiple files via workspace edit — requires Codex tool approval
  • Hook subprocesses: Run as user; inherit environment; no seccomp/seatbelt in LC
  • No network MCP: LC snapshot registers LSP only — reduces MCP SSRF surface vs OmO OpenCode full stack
  • Telemetry: Separate outbound PostHog — not a model tool (components/telemetry/)

17. Test coverage

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-71
// MCP lsp server path isolation

// 161_lazycodex/src/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/test/mcp.test.ts (submodule)
// JSON-RPC request/response unit tests

Component-level tests exist under each components/*/test/ directory.

18. Operational workflow

  1. Codex session loads omo plugin → reads .mcp.json → spawns LSP MCP on first tool need
  2. Model calls lsp_diagnostics / lsp_goto_definition via MCP
  3. MCP server spawns language server for file extension (from LSP config)
  4. User edits via apply_patch → PostToolUse hooks fire automatically
  5. LSP hook may block with error diagnostics; comment-checker may block on slop comments
  6. Rules hook may inject dynamic context for edited files
  7. MCP server idles out after 10 minutes if unused

19. Evidence quality bar

  • MCP server JSON-RPC paths with line numbers
  • Full LSP tool catalog from tools.ts
  • Hook registry with matchers and timeouts
  • PostToolUse block hooks documented
  • Process spawn policy (shell: false)
  • Negative: no LC-owned LLM tool loop

20. Source citation index

Path Lines Role

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json 8-21 Workspace layout

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json 1-9 LSP MCP registration

변경 기록

날짜 변경

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current

21

축 04: 스킬 시스템

AXIS

004_skill-system/04_lc_skill.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex exposes skills through the bundled omo Codex plugin: manifest path ./skills/, build-time sync from component and shared sources, Codex compatibility rewrites for OpenCode orchestration examples, and marketplace registration under sisyphuslabs. Skill invocation and lazy loading are Codex host behaviors.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Role in the LazyCodex stack
    1. Marketplace packaging
    1. Build pipeline — sync-skills.mjs
  • Source mapping
    1. Checked-in aggregate inventory
    1. Codex harness tool compatibility rewrite
    1. Bundled Codex agent TOMLs (skill cross-references)
    1. Skill format — SKILL.md conventions
    1. OmO upstream skill runtime (OpenCode — reference)
    1. Relationship to other context channels
    1. Test coverage

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  Build["npm run build"] --> Sync["sync-skills.mjs"]
  Sync --> Comp["Component skills<br/>comment-checker, lsp, rules, ultragoal"]
  Sync --> Shared["shared-skills/skills/*<br/>(when present)"]
  Sync --> Adapt["insertCodexCompatibilityGuidance"]
  Adapt --> Out["plugins/omo/skills/"]
  Out --> Manifest["plugin.json skills: ./skills/"]
  Manifest --> CX["Codex host lazy-load"]
  Market[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"] --> CX
  Agents["ultrawork/agents/*.toml"] -.->|"referenced by skills"| Out

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:9-10
forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
  ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...]

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14,27-31
"skills": "./skills/",
"defaultPrompt": [
  "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
  "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
  "ulw: run this change with evidence."
]

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "category": "Developer Tools",
    "policy": {
      "installation": "AVAILABLE",
      "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:6-14
const root = dirname(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)));
const sharedSkillsRoot = join(root, "..", "..", "shared-skills", "skills");
const skillsRoot = join(root, "skills");
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:17-20
"build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
"sync:skills": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs",

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:16-34
const opencodeOnlyOrchestrationPattern = /\b(?:call_omo_agent|background_output|team_[a-z_]+|task)\s*\(/;

const codexHarnessToolCompatibility = `## Codex Harness Tool Compatibility
...
| \`call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)\` |
| \`call_omo_agent(subagent_type="librarian", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian", ...)\` |
| \`task(subagent_type="plan", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="plan", ...)\` |
| \`task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)\` for final verification | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)\` |
| \`background_output(task_id="...")\` | \`wait_agent(...)\` |
| \`team_*(...)\` | Codex native subagents + send_message, followup_task, wait_agent, close_agent |
`;

README / docs

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/
├── explorer.toml
├── librarian.toml
├── plan.toml
└── codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"

README / docs

src/src/tools/skill/tools.ts
src/src/tools/skill/description-formatter.ts
src/src/plugin/skill-context.ts
src/src/shared/host-skill-config.ts

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
assert.equal(manifest.skills, "./skills/");

코퍼스 — 004_skill-system/04_lc_skill.md (심화)

LazyCodex — Skill System

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ — packaging and distribution of OmO-derived skills under one Codex plugin namespace; no native skill loader or activation engine.

LazyCodex exposes skills through the bundled omo Codex plugin: manifest path ./skills/, build-time sync from component and shared sources, Codex compatibility rewrites for OpenCode orchestration examples, and marketplace registration under sisyphuslabs. Skill invocation and lazy loading are Codex host behaviors.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  Build["npm run build"] --> Sync["sync-skills.mjs"]
  Sync --> Comp["Component skills<br/>comment-checker, lsp, rules, ultragoal"]
  Sync --> Shared["shared-skills/skills/*<br/>(when present)"]
  Sync --> Adapt["insertCodexCompatibilityGuidance"]
  Adapt --> Out["plugins/omo/skills/"]
  Out --> Manifest["plugin.json skills: ./skills/"]
  Manifest --> CX["Codex host lazy-load"]
  Market[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"] --> CX
  Agents["ultrawork/agents/*.toml"] -.->|"referenced by skills"| Out

1. Role in the LazyCodex stack

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:9-10
forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
  ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...]

Install deploys the aggregate plugin (including skills) to the user's Codex config. The shim does not parse or execute skills at runtime.

Plugin manifest:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14,27-31
"skills": "./skills/",
"defaultPrompt": [
  "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
  "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
  "ulw: run this change with evidence."
]

defaultPrompt entries are marketplace starter prompts — not automatic skill injection.

2. Marketplace packaging

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "category": "Developer Tools",
    "policy": {
      "installation": "AVAILABLE",
      "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
    }
  }]
}

Single plugin namespace omo aggregates all component skills — users install one marketplace entry, not per-component plugins.

3. Build pipeline — sync-skills.mjs

Source mapping

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:6-14
const root = dirname(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)));
const sharedSkillsRoot = join(root, "..", "..", "shared-skills", "skills");
const skillsRoot = join(root, "skills");
const skillSources = [
  ["comment-checker", "components/comment-checker/skills/comment-checker"],
  ["lsp", "components/lsp/skills/lsp"],
  ["rules", "components/rules/skills/rules"],
  ["ultragoal", "components/ultragoal/skills/ultragoal"],
];

Build steps (:57-74):

  1. rm -rf skills/ — clean regenerate
  2. Copy each component skill directory
  3. Run adaptSkillForCodex (compatibility injection)
  4. Copy all directories from shared-skills/skills/ when present
  5. Adapt shared skills too

Triggered by package build:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:17-20
"build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
"sync:skills": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs",

4. Checked-in aggregate inventory

Skills present in plugins/omo/skills/ at snapshot time:

Skill directory Typical role
comment-checker Post-edit AI-slop comment detection guidance
lsp LSP MCP tool usage patterns
rules Project rules discovery and matching
ultragoal Goal/plan steering with .omo/ultragoal
programming General implementation discipline
debugging Systematic debug workflow
frontend-ui-ux UI/UX implementation patterns
refactor Safe refactoring guidance
review-work Code review workflow
remove-ai-slops / ai-slop-remover Cleanup AI-generated noise

Caveat: shared-skills/ is absent in some checkouts — shared skill provenance is inferred from sync-skills.mjs paths and generated output, not live shared tree.

5. Codex harness tool compatibility rewrite

OpenCode-only tool names in skill examples trigger automatic preamble injection:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:16-34
const opencodeOnlyOrchestrationPattern = /\b(?:call_omo_agent|background_output|team_[a-z_]+|task)\s*\(/;

const codexHarnessToolCompatibility = `## Codex Harness Tool Compatibility
...
| \`call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)\` |
| \`call_omo_agent(subagent_type="librarian", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian", ...)\` |
| \`task(subagent_type="plan", ...)\` | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="plan", ...)\` |
| \`task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)\` for final verification | \`spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)\` |
| \`background_output(task_id="...")\` | \`wait_agent(...)\` |
| \`team_*(...)\` | Codex native subagents + send_message, followup_task, wait_agent, close_agent |
`;

Insertion logic (:36-45):

  • Skip if pattern not found or block already present
  • Insert after YAML frontmatter if present; else prepend to file

Rule: When skill body conflicts with compatibility section, compatibility section wins (sync-skills.mjs:32).

6. Bundled Codex agent TOMLs (skill cross-references)

Skills referencing subagent types must match bundled TOML definitions:

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/
├── explorer.toml
├── librarian.toml
├── plan.toml
└── codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml

aggregate.test.mjs verifies:

  • All four TOML files exist
  • Skills with spawn_agent(agent_type="...") references have matching TOML name field
  • findSpawnAgentTypes regex extracts agent types from skill content (:13-19)

Example agent definition consumed by skills:

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"

Skills teach when to spawn these roles; Codex host executes spawn_agent.

7. Skill format — SKILL.md conventions

Component skills follow standard frontmatter + markdown body pattern. After sync, Codex-compatible skills may include:

  1. YAML frontmatter (name, description, triggers)
  2. ## Codex Harness Tool Compatibility block (if OpenCode examples detected)
  3. Procedure body with tool examples translated to Codex names

Lazy loading: Codex reads skill metadata from plugin manifest; full SKILL.md body loads when model/user activates skill — not merged into every system prompt.

8. OmO upstream skill runtime (OpenCode — reference)

Submodule 161_lazycodex/src/ implements OpenCode-native skill tools:

src/src/tools/skill/tools.ts
src/src/tools/skill/description-formatter.ts
src/src/plugin/skill-context.ts
src/src/shared/host-skill-config.ts

OpenCode path registers skill tools in plugin tool registry (src/src/plugin/tool-registry.ts). Codex platform install does not use this registry — skills are static files in ./skills/ consumed by Codex host API.

9. Relationship to other context channels

Mechanism Always-on? Skill overlap
Rules hook SessionStart + prompts rules skill documents rule files
Ultrawork directive Keyword ulw/ultrawork Ultrawork workflow in detector + agent TOMLs
Ultragoal Steering syntax ultragoal skill
LSP MCP On tool invoke lsp skill
Skills On invoke Primary extensibility surface

Skills complement hooks — hooks inject automatically; skills are opt-in playbooks.

10. Test coverage

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
assert.equal(manifest.skills, "./skills/");

sync-skills.test.mjs (referenced in prior corpus):

  • Expected aggregate skill directory names after sync
  • Skills with non-generic spawn_agent references have matching TOML
  • Codex compatibility block present when OpenCode patterns exist

aggregate.test.mjs:73+ verifies build script runs telemetry sync before workspace builds — skills sync is first step in build chain.

11. Owned vs delegated surfaces

Surface LC-owned? Notes
Skill file content (generated) Yes sync-skills.mjs output
Skill discovery/loader No Codex host
Skill invocation API No Codex host
Agent TOML role defs Yes ultrawork/agents/
Marketplace metadata Yes marketplace.json + plugin.json
Shared skill source tree Partial May be absent in checkout
OpenCode skill tools No (submodule) Not on Codex path

12. Parity matrix — skill axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc) OmO OpenCode
Skill storage Host skills dirs + plugin plugins/omo/skills/ Dynamic skill tools
Loader codex-rs skills module Host reads plugin manifest skill-context.ts
Orchestration examples Native spawn_agent Rewritten from OpenCode call_omo_agent, task
Agent roles agents/*.md ultrawork/*.toml Full agent registry
Marketplace Codex plugin market sisyphuslabs/omo N/A

13. Maintainer workflow

  1. Edit skill source in components/*/skills/ or upstream shared-skills/
  2. Run npm run sync:skills in plugins/omo/
  3. Review generated skills/ for compatibility block correctness
  4. Run npm test — aggregate + sync-skills tests
  5. Release via LC/OmO publish pipeline → user lazycodex-ai install updates plugin

14. Design boundary

Do not document LazyCodex as implementing:

  • Skill embedding / vector search
  • Skill activation classifiers (model-side)
  • Skill permission policies (beyond Codex host)
  • Runtime skill hot-reload (requires plugin reinstall / host refresh)

LC is a skill packager for Codex plugin distribution.

15. Error handling

Failure Behavior
Missing shared-skills dir Component skills still sync; shared loop skipped if readdir fails
Skill without frontmatter Compatibility block prepended to entire file
Unknown spawn_agent type in skill Test failure in aggregate.test.mjs
Empty skills/ after failed sync Build/test failure

16. Evidence quality bar

  • sync-skills.mjs full pipeline with line numbers
  • Marketplace + manifest skill path
  • Codex compatibility mapping table
  • Inventory of checked-in skills
  • Agent TOML cross-reference contract
  • Negative: no LC skill loader/runtime

17. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 9-10 Install deploys plugin+skills
161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json 1-16 sisyphuslabs marketplace
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 14, 27-31 skills path, defaultPrompt
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs 6-74 Sync + Codex rewrite
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json 17-20 build/sync:skills scripts

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current

축 05: 서브에이전트

AXIS

005_subagents-collab/05_lc_subagent.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex packages four Codex agent role definitions (explorer, librarian, plan, codex-ultrawork-reviewer), ultrawork keyword workflow injection, and skill-level Codex compatibility mappings. Process isolation, async scheduling, and inter-agent messaging are Codex runtime concerns.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Boundary — LC vs Codex vs OmO
    1. Bundled Codex agent roles
  • explorer — read-only codebase search
  • librarian — external OSS / docs research
  • plan — strategic planner (no product code)
  • codex-ultrawork-reviewer — verification gate
    1. Ultrawork keyword trigger
    1. Codex-native spawn mapping (from skills sync)
    1. Collaboration workflow (ultrawork mode)
    1. Test contract — TOML + skill alignment
    1. OmO upstream subagent runtime (OpenCode — reference)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["User prompt with ulw/ultrawork"] --> Det["ultrawork-detector.py"]
  Det --> Ctx["additional_context directive"]
  Ctx --> Orch["Orchestrator agent"]
  Orch --> SA["Codex spawn_agent"]
  SA --> E["explorer — read-only search"]
  SA --> L["librarian — external OSS research"]
  SA --> P["plan — plan-only markdown"]
  SA --> W["worker — implementation"]
  SA --> R["codex-ultrawork-reviewer — gate"]
  R -->|REJECTION| Orch
  R -->|APPROVAL| Done["Done"]
  TOML["agents/*.toml"] -.-> SA
  Skills["skills/ spawn_agent examples"] -.-> Orch

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:9-10
// install --platform=codex — deploys TOML agents to Codex config, not OmO task runtime

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6,65-67
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
# ...
# READ-ONLY. Tools I will NEVER call: edit, write, apply_patch, spawn_agent, call_*_agent

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:1-6,11-17
name = "librarian"
description = "External open-source codebase and documentation researcher..."
# USE me when: unfamiliar package, upstream API, OSS implementation search
# AVOID me when: answer is in local working tree (explorer's job)

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/plan.toml:1-6,11-17
name = "plan"
# You ARE the planner. You ARE NOT an implementer.
# When caller demands implementation, REFUSE.

README / docs

Spawn parallel read-only subagents for internal-source aspects...
Spawn parallel read-only subagents for external-source aspects...

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17
name = "codex-ultrawork-reviewer"
description = "Strict ultrawork verification reviewer..."
# Return UNCONDITIONAL APPROVAL only when diff satisfies every success criterion
# Return REJECTION if any criterion lacks evidence

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22,276-290
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

def main() -> None:
    payload = _load_payload()
    if payload is not None and _should_inject(payload):
        _ = sys.stdout.write(ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE)
    sys.exit(0)

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:24-30
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)` |
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="librarian", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="plan", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="plan", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)` for final verification | `spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)` |
| `task(category="...", ...)` for implementation | `spawn_agent(agent_type="worker", ...)` |
| `background_output(task_id="...")` | `wait_agent(...)` |
| `team_*(...)` | send_message, followup_task, wait_agent, close_agent |

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:13-19
function findSpawnAgentTypes(content) {
  const agentTypes = new Set();
  const regex = /spawn_agent\(agent_type="([^"]+)"/g;
  for (const match of content.matchAll(regex)) {
    agentTypes.add(match[1]);
  }
  return [...agentTypes].sort();
}

README / docs

161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/delegate-task/
161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/call-omo-agent/
161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/background-task/
161_lazycodex/src/src/shared/tmux/

코퍼스 — 005_subagents-collab/05_lc_subagent.md (심화)

LazyCodex — Subagents / Collaboration

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ — no standalone subagent scheduler; collaboration via Codex host spawn APIs + bundled OmO ultrawork TOML roles and prompt hooks.

LazyCodex packages four Codex agent role definitions (explorer, librarian, plan, codex-ultrawork-reviewer), ultrawork keyword workflow injection, and skill-level Codex compatibility mappings. Process isolation, async scheduling, and inter-agent messaging are Codex runtime concerns.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["User prompt with ulw/ultrawork"] --> Det["ultrawork-detector.py"]
  Det --> Ctx["additional_context directive"]
  Ctx --> Orch["Orchestrator agent"]
  Orch --> SA["Codex spawn_agent"]
  SA --> E["explorer — read-only search"]
  SA --> L["librarian — external OSS research"]
  SA --> P["plan — plan-only markdown"]
  SA --> W["worker — implementation"]
  SA --> R["codex-ultrawork-reviewer — gate"]
  R -->|REJECTION| Orch
  R -->|APPROVAL| Done["Done"]
  TOML["agents/*.toml"] -.-> SA
  Skills["skills/ spawn_agent examples"] -.-> Orch

1. Boundary — LC vs Codex vs OmO

Responsibility Owner
spawn_agent, wait_agent, send_message Codex host
Agent TOML role definitions LC plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/
Ultrawork workflow directive LC ultrawork-detector.py
OpenCode call_omo_agent, task, background_output OmO submodule (src/) — not Codex path
Parallel process / tmux orchestration OmO OpenCode hooks — reference only 
// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:9-10
// install --platform=codex — deploys TOML agents to Codex config, not OmO task runtime

2. Bundled Codex agent roles

Four TOML files under plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/:

explorer — read-only codebase search

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:1-6,65-67
name = "explorer"
description = "Codebase search specialist for Codex sessions..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
# ...
# READ-ONLY. Tools I will NEVER call: edit, write, apply_patch, spawn_agent, call_*_agent

Output contract requires <analysis> and <results> blocks with absolute paths (:24-46). Tool strategy: parallel LSP + ripgrep + ast_grep (:48-56).

librarian — external OSS / docs research

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:1-6,11-17
name = "librarian"
description = "External open-source codebase and documentation researcher..."
# USE me when: unfamiliar package, upstream API, OSS implementation search
# AVOID me when: answer is in local working tree (explorer's job)

Uses host web_search, webfetch, gh CLI per instructions (:42+). SHA-pinned GitHub permalink citations required.

plan — strategic planner (no product code)

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/plan.toml:1-6,11-17
name = "plan"
# You ARE the planner. You ARE NOT an implementer.
# When caller demands implementation, REFUSE.

Phase 1 mandates parallel read-only subagents before drafting (:26-34):

Spawn parallel read-only subagents for internal-source aspects...
Spawn parallel read-only subagents for external-source aspects...

Plan output: single plans/<slug>.md with waves, dependency matrix, QA scenarios (:36-141).

codex-ultrawork-reviewer — verification gate

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17
name = "codex-ultrawork-reviewer"
description = "Strict ultrawork verification reviewer..."
# Return UNCONDITIONAL APPROVAL only when diff satisfies every success criterion
# Return REJECTION if any criterion lacks evidence

Strict binary verdict — "looks good but..." counts as rejection (:14-16).

3. Ultrawork keyword trigger

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:21-22,276-290
ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

def main() -> None:
    payload = _load_payload()
    if payload is not None and _should_inject(payload):
        _ = sys.stdout.write(ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE)
    sys.exit(0)

Registered on UserPromptSubmit (hooks.json:35-42), timeout 5s.

Directive highlights (ULTRAWORK_DIRECTIVE, :25-256):

  • First visible line: ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLED!
  • Requires test-first RED→GREEN and manual QA via HTTP/tmux/browser/computer-use channels
  • Mandates create_goal or binding # Goal block with success criteria
  • Requires spawning reviewer before declaring done
  • Parallel exploration stop rules after 2 unproductive waves

Hook never blocks (exit 0 always) — collaboration mode is advisory injection, not permission gate.

4. Codex-native spawn mapping (from skills sync)

OpenCode examples in skills are rewritten for Codex:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/scripts/sync-skills.mjs:24-30
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="explore", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer", ...)` |
| `call_omo_agent(subagent_type="librarian", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="plan", ...)` | `spawn_agent(agent_type="plan", ...)` |
| `task(subagent_type="oracle", ...)` for final verification | `spawn_agent(agent_type="codex-ultrawork-reviewer", ...)` |
| `task(category="...", ...)` for implementation | `spawn_agent(agent_type="worker", ...)` |
| `background_output(task_id="...")` | `wait_agent(...)` |
| `team_*(...)` | send_message, followup_task, wait_agent, close_agent |

worker agent type referenced for implementation tasks — may be Codex default worker, not a bundled TOML in ultrawork/agents/ (only four TOMLs checked in).

5. Collaboration workflow (ultrawork mode)

  1. User includes ulw or ultrawork in prompt
  2. Detector injects full workflow directive into turn context
  3. Orchestrator creates goal with success criteria
  4. Parallel phase: spawn explorer (internal) + librarian (external) read-only agents
  5. Optional: spawn plan for large/ambiguous work → plans/<slug>.md
  6. Implementation via worker spawn(s) or orchestrator direct edits
  7. Manual QA scenarios per criterion (HTTP/tmux/browser/CU)
  8. Spawn codex-ultrawork-reviewer with diff + evidence
  9. Reviewer returns APPROVAL or REJECTION with blocking issues
  10. Loop until approval or user intervention

LC defines roles and prompt discipline; Codex executes spawns and carries messages.

6. Test contract — TOML + skill alignment

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:13-19
function findSpawnAgentTypes(content) {
  const agentTypes = new Set();
  const regex = /spawn_agent\(agent_type="([^"]+)"/g;
  for (const match of content.matchAll(regex)) {
    agentTypes.add(match[1]);
  }
  return [...agentTypes].sort();
}

Tests verify:

  • Four agent TOML files exist with expected name fields
  • Skills referencing custom agent types have matching TOML definitions
  • Hook commands stay under components/ paths (:38-57)

7. OmO upstream subagent runtime (OpenCode — reference)

Submodule implements full delegation stack:

161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/delegate-task/
161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/call-omo-agent/
161_lazycodex/src/src/tools/background-task/
161_lazycodex/src/src/shared/tmux/

OpenCode hooks include delegate-task-retry, ralph-loop, start-work (create-session-hooks.ts:211-229). README documents OpenCode commands $ulw-loop, $ulw-plan, $start-work (README.md:46-52) — OpenCode session features, not Codex plugin hooks.

Codex users get ultrawork via keyword + TOML roles, not $ulw-loop slash commands (unless Codex adds equivalent).

8. Read-only vs mutating role separation

Agent Mutations Spawns subagents?
explorer No No (explicit ban, explorer.toml:66)
librarian No (read-only external) No
plan Plan file only (plans/*.md) Yes — parallel read-only research spawns (:29-30)
codex-ultrawork-reviewer No No
worker (referenced) Yes — implementation Per Codex policy

Enforcement is prompt-level in TOML developer_instructions, not OS sandbox.

9. Parallelism model

Plan agent specifies wave-based parallel execution (plan.toml:64-80):

  • Target 5-8 tasks per wave
  • Wave 1 shared dependencies extracted first
  • Dependency matrix required in plan output

Ultrawork directive mandates parallel subagent research before implementation. Actual concurrency limits are Codex host scheduler policies.

10. Review gate semantics

Reviewer input expectation (codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:10-11):

Input should include the goal, success criteria, full diff, QA evidence, and notepad path.

Verdict strings:

  • UNCONDITIONAL APPROVAL — all criteria proven
  • REJECTION — missing evidence, drift, or risk

Ultrawork directive ties declaration-of-done to reviewer approval — process contract, not automated CI.

11. Parity matrix — subagent axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc) OmO OpenCode
Spawn API spawn_agent, task Host-owned call_omo_agent, task
Role definitions agents/*.md 4× ultrawork TOML Full agent registry
Workflow trigger Config / user ulw/ultrawork keyword Hooks + slash commands
Review gate Policy / custom codex-ultrawork-reviewer Oracle / reviewer agents
Background tasks wait_agent Host API background_output
tmux parallel Host-dependent Referenced in ultrawork QA interactive-bash-session hook

12. What LC does NOT implement

  • Subagent process isolation / cgroup
  • Cross-agent mailbox persistence (Codex host)
  • Automatic spawn scheduling / queue
  • Team topology (team_* — OpenCode only; mapped to Codex messages in skill sync)
  • Ralph loop / $ulw-loop on Codex (OpenCode feature in README)

13. Security / trust notes

  • Spawned agents inherit orchestrator permission mode from Codex session
  • Read-only roles rely on prompt constraints — not hardware enforcement
  • Librarian agent may fetch arbitrary URLs via host web tools — SSRF boundary is Codex policy
  • Reviewer cannot block at OS level — only influences orchestrator via message

14. Evidence quality bar

  • All four TOML agents cited with line ranges
  • Ultrawork detector keyword + directive contract
  • Codex spawn mapping from sync-skills.mjs
  • aggregate.test.mjs spawn_agent alignment
  • Negative: no LC scheduler implementation

15. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml 1-6, 24-67 Read-only search agent
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml 1-17 External research agent
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/plan.toml 1-17, 26-34, 64-80 Planner + parallel gather
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml 1-17 Review gate
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py 21-22, 25-256, 276-290 Keyword workflow injection
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 35-42 UserPromptSubmit registration

161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs 13-19 spawn_agent type extraction

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP38: initial stub
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — TOML roles, ultrawork workflow, spawn mapping

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Agent TOMLs 4 roles in ultrawork/agents Model IDs and instructions may change
Ultrawork directive Full body in detector.py OmO iteration on QA requirements

23

축 06: 앱·서버 표면

AXIS

006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_lc_surface.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex does not ship JSON-RPC session daemons, WebSocket streaming endpoints, REST APIs, or client SDKs for agent control. Agent session transport, tool streaming, and approvals are Codex/OmO host-owned. LC packages entrypoints and plugin components.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Surface inventory
    1. CLI surface — synchronous forwarder
    1. Codex plugin surface
  • Manifest
  • Hook transport model
    1. MCP surface — stdio JSON-RPC only
    1. Web surface — Next.js on Cloudflare Workers
    1. CI/CD surfaces
    1. Marketplace discovery surface
    1. Absent surfaces (negative evidence)
    1. Client interaction model (Codex user)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart LR
  subgraph LC["LazyCodex-owned surfaces"]
    CLI["lazycodex-ai.js<br/>npm bin"]
    Plugin["plugins/omo<br/>Codex plugin"]
    MCP["LSP MCP stdio"]
    Web["packages/web<br/>Next.js docs"]
  end
  subgraph Host["Not LC-owned"]
    CX["Codex CLI / IDE"]
    AS["codex-rs/app-server"]
  end
  User["User"] --> CLI
  CLI -->|install| Plugin
  User --> CX
  CX --> Plugin
  CX --> MCP
  User --> Web
  IDE["IDE clients"] --> AS
  AS -.->|"LC absent"| X["❌"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:1-27
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"

const args = process.argv.slice(2)
const dryRun = args[0] === "--dry-run"
const forwardedArgs = dryRun ? args.slice(1) : args
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

if (dryRun) {
  console.log(["bunx", ...commandArgs].join(" "))
  process.exit(0)
}

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, { stdio: "inherit" })
process.exit(result.status ?? 1)

bunx lazycodex-ai install
# shorthand for bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex

  output: NodeJS.WritableStream = process.stdout,
  options: McpStdioServerOptions = {},

코퍼스 — 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_lc_surface.md (심화)

LazyCodex — App Server / Client Surfaces

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ — not an agent app server; three concrete surfaces: npm CLI shim, bundled Codex plugin (hooks + LSP MCP stdio), and public Next.js docs on Cloudflare Workers.

LazyCodex does not ship JSON-RPC session daemons, WebSocket streaming endpoints, REST APIs, or client SDKs for agent control. Agent session transport, tool streaming, and approvals are Codex/OmO host-owned. LC packages entrypoints and plugin components.

Architecture

flowchart LR
  subgraph LC["LazyCodex-owned surfaces"]
    CLI["lazycodex-ai.js<br/>npm bin"]
    Plugin["plugins/omo<br/>Codex plugin"]
    MCP["LSP MCP stdio"]
    Web["packages/web<br/>Next.js docs"]
  end
  subgraph Host["Not LC-owned"]
    CX["Codex CLI / IDE"]
    AS["codex-rs/app-server"]
  end
  User["User"] --> CLI
  CLI -->|install| Plugin
  User --> CX
  CX --> Plugin
  CX --> MCP
  User --> Web
  IDE["IDE clients"] --> AS
  AS -.->|"LC absent"| X["❌"]

1. Surface inventory

Surface Protocol LC-owned? Role
lazycodex-ai npm bin Process spawn (bunx) Yes Install/command alias to OmO
Codex plugin omo Codex plugin API Yes (snapshot) hooks, skills, MCP manifest
LSP MCP stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 Yes Language intelligence tools
Docs web HTTPS (Cloudflare Worker) Yes Marketing + documentation
Agent app-server JSON-RPC / WS No Codex codex-rs/app-server
OpenCode server OpenCode protocol No OmO submodule target

2. CLI surface — synchronous forwarder

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:1-27
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process"

const args = process.argv.slice(2)
const dryRun = args[0] === "--dry-run"
const forwardedArgs = dryRun ? args.slice(1) : args
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

if (dryRun) {
  console.log(["bunx", ...commandArgs].join(" "))
  process.exit(0)
}

const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, { stdio: "inherit" })
process.exit(result.status ?? 1)

Characteristics:

  • No persistent server — one-shot process per invocation
  • --dry-run prints resolved argv (:13-15) for CI/debug
  • stdio: inherit — child owns terminal for install wizards
  • Install vs runtime distinguished by first arg (:9-11)

README install entry:

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:34-36
bunx lazycodex-ai install
# shorthand for bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex

3. Codex plugin surface

Manifest

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-16
{
  "name": "omo",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "OMO",
    "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"]
  }
}

Codex host loads this at plugin install time — hooks register subprocess commands; MCP spawns on demand; skills expose directory to host skill API.

Hook transport model

Hooks are not network services — Codex spawns local subprocess per event:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json:7-9
"command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/rules/dist/cli.js\" hook session-start",
"timeout": 10,
"statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"

${PLUGIN_ROOT} resolved by Codex plugin loader. stdin JSON → stdout JSON/text. No HTTP, no WebSocket.

Aggregate test enforces component isolation:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:38-57
// All hook paths must reference components/*/dist/cli.js — no external @scope packages

4. MCP surface — stdio JSON-RPC only

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-9
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

Server implementation:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:78-82
export async function runMcpStdioServer(
  input: NodeJS.ReadableStream = process.stdin,
  output: NodeJS.WritableStream = process.stdout,
  options: McpStdioServerOptions = {},
): Promise<void> {

Supported JSON-RPC methods (:49-75):

  • initialize — protocol handshake, serverInfo: { name: "lsp", version: "0.1.0" }
  • tools/list — seven LSP tools
  • tools/call — execute tool
  • ping — health check
  • notifications/initialized — no response

No SSE MCP, no HTTP MCP, no websocket transport in LC snapshot.

Idle lifecycle: 10-minute default timeout closes server (DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10 * 60_000, :46).

5. Web surface — Next.js on Cloudflare Workers

Repository layout from README:

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:84-91
lazycodex/
├── src/                     → oh-my-openagent (submodule)
├── packages/
│   └── web/                 → Next.js 15 + Tailwind v4 + opennextjs-cloudflare
│                              (deployed to lazycodex.ai via Cloudflare Workers)
├── .github/workflows/       → web-ci.yml + web-deploy.yml

Wrangler config:

# 161_lazycodex/packages/web/wrangler.toml:1-18
main = ".open-next/worker.js"
name = "lazycodex-web"
compatibility_date = "2026-05-25"
compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]
workers_dev = false

[[routes]]
pattern = "lazycodex.ai"
custom_domain = true

Web surface is static/docs/marketing — not an agent control plane. No session API, no chat WebSocket, no tool streaming endpoint.

DNS note in wrangler: www.lazycodex.ai, lazycodex.dev redirect at Cloudflare edge — never hit Worker (:12-15).

Documentation URL referenced in README: lazycodex.ai/docs (README.md:54).

6. CI/CD surfaces

.github/workflows/ (cited in README :89-90):

Workflow Purpose
web-ci.yml Build/test docs site
web-deploy.yml Deploy to Cloudflare
npm publish workflows lazycodex-ai package to registry

These are maintainer pipelines — not runtime agent surfaces.

7. Marketplace discovery surface

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:7-15
"plugins": [{
  "name": "omo",
  "source": "./plugins/omo",
  "policy": { "installation": "AVAILABLE", "authentication": "ON_INSTALL" }
}]

Local Codex marketplace snapshot for development/install testing — consumed by Codex plugin installer, not a public HTTP API hosted by LC.

8. Absent surfaces (negative evidence)

Searched 161_lazycodex/ for common app-server patterns:

Pattern Finding
express, fastify, hono server listen Not in LC-owned runtime paths
WebSocket /ws session Absent
JSON-RPC app-server crate Absent — lives in 121_openai-codex/codex-rs/app-server
gRPC / Connect Absent
OpenCode opencode serve integration in LC shim Not in lazycodex-ai.js
Agent SDK npm export LC package is install shim only

OmO submodule may implement OpenCode server integration under src/ — not activated by lazycodex-ai Codex path.

9. Client interaction model (Codex user)

  1. Install: bunx lazycodex-ai install → OmO writes Codex config + plugin files
  2. Session: User runs codex CLI or IDE — Codex is the client
  3. Plugin hooks: Codex invokes subprocess hooks on lifecycle events
  4. MCP: Codex spawns LSP MCP stdio child when model uses LSP tools
  5. Docs: Browser → https://lazycodex.ai for human documentation only

LC never sits in the hot path of model streaming.

10. OmO OpenCode surfaces (submodule reference)

OpenCode path in 161_lazycodex/src/ includes:

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/cli/tui-installer.ts:16-20
// Interactive @clack/prompts install TUI — TTY required

// 161_lazycodex/src/src/plugin/hooks/create-session-hooks.ts:165-175
// ctx.client.tui.showToast — OpenCode client SDK

These are different product surfaces from LazyCodex-on-Codex. README still mentions OpenCode commands ($ulw-loop, etc.) for OmO users (README.md:46-52).

11. Plugin component CLI entrypoints

Each component exposes dist/cli.js subcommands invoked by hooks:

Component Hook commands
rules hook session-start, user-prompt-submit, post-tool-use, post-compact
telemetry hook session-start
comment-checker hook post-tool-use
lsp hook post-tool-use
ultragoal hook user-prompt-submit
ultrawork Python script (no cli.js)

Component workspace list (plugins/omo/package.json:8-16). These CLIs are hook adapters, not user-facing REPLs.

12. Parity matrix — surfaces

Surface Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
JSON-RPC app-server codex-rs/app-server Absent
CLI entry codex binary lazycodex-ai → bunx omo
Plugin transport Codex plugin API .codex-plugin/plugin.json
MCP transport Host MCP client + stdio/SSE LSP stdio only in plugin
Web UI for agent Codex TUI / IDE None — docs site only
Install TUI Codex installer OmO via bunx (optional --no-tui)

13. Protocol details — LSP MCP stdio framing

Request handling (mcp.ts:49-76):

  • Malformed input → -32600 Invalid Request
  • Unknown method → -32601 Method not found
  • Tool errors returned in result with isError: true (handleToolCall path)

Readline-based message loop on stdin — standard MCP stdio convention. Codex host manages process lifetime.

14. Security / exposure

  • CLI: Runs bunx with user privileges — trusts npm registry for oh-my-openagent
  • Hooks: Subprocess with inherited env — no network bind
  • MCP: Local stdio only — not exposed on TCP port by default
  • Web: Public static docs — no agent credentials
  • Telemetry: Outbound PostHog from telemetry hook — separate from user-facing surface

15. Evidence quality bar

  • CLI shim fully cited
  • Plugin manifest + hook transport model
  • MCP stdio server entry + methods
  • Web wrangler.toml + README layout
  • Negative: no app-server in LC tree
  • Parity vs Codex app-server

변경 기록

날짜 변경

2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — CLI/plugin/MCP/web surface path:line

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Web deploy wrangler.toml lazycodex.ai Cloudflare config may change
MCP transport stdio only Codex may add SSE MCP support
CLI forwarder bunx oh-my-openagent Package name/version drift

24

축 07: 샌드박스·정책

AXIS

007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_lc_sandbox.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex does not implement seatbelt, bwrap, seccomp, landlock, chroot, Docker sandboxes, network allowlists, or command classifiers. Policy surfaces are limited to: hook subprocess timeouts, post-edit block decisions (comment-checker, LSP), LSP language-server spawn with shell: false, and outbound telemetry opt-out flags.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Distribution shim — no policy
    1. Hook subprocess policy
  • Timeouts (Codex-enforced cancellation)
  • Ultrawork hook — never blocks
    1. Post-edit block policy
  • Comment checker
  • LSP diagnostics hook
    1. LSP MCP — primary execution surface
  • Spawn policy — no shell
  • Cwd validation
  • Process tree termination

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  subgraph LC["LC policy surfaces"]
    HookT["Hook timeouts<br/>hooks.json"]
    Block["PostToolUse blocks<br/>comment-checker, LSP"]
    LSPSpawn["LSP spawn shell:false"]
    Tel["Telemetry opt-out<br/>env flags"]
  end
  subgraph Host["Codex host (not LC)"]
    SB["OS sandbox / seatbelt"]
    Guard["Tool approval / guardian"]
    Perm["permission_mode"]
  end
  User["User session"] --> Host
  Host --> LC
  LSPSpawn --> LS["User-configured<br/>language servers"]
  LS --> FS["Filesystem mutations<br/>via lsp.rename"]
  Guard --> FS
  SB --> Shell["bash/shell tool"]

  stdio: "inherit",
})

PostToolUse rules:       timeout 10
PostCompact rules:       timeout 10

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:10,290
# exit: 0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).
sys.exit(0)

return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

// ...
decision: "block",
reason,  // LSP error diagnostics after edit

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:24-28
export interface PreparedSpawnCommand {
  command: string;
  args: string[];
  shell: false;
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:159-161
if (platform !== "win32") {
  return { command: cmd, args, shell: false };
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:41-57
export function validateCwd(cwd: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  if (!existsSync(cwd)) {
    return { valid: false, error: `Working directory does not exist: ${cwd}` };
  }
  const stats = statSync(cwd);
  if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
    return { valid: false, error: `Path is not a directory: ${cwd}` };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:89-110
function killProcessTree(proc: ChildProcess, signal: NodeJS.Signals): void {
  if (process.platform === "win32" && proc.pid) {
    spawnSync("taskkill", ["/pid", String(proc.pid), "/f", "/t"], { stdio: "ignore" });
  }
  if (process.platform !== "win32" && proc.pid) {
    process.kill(-proc.pid, signal);  // process group
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:654-667
{

  aliases: ["lsp_rename"],
  description: "Rename a symbol across the workspace and apply the returned workspace edit.",
  // ...
}

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/workspace-edit.ts:19-48,59-79
function applyTextEditsToFile(filePath: string, edits: TextEdit[]): FileApplyResult {
  const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
  // ... apply edits ...
  writeFileSync(filePath, lines.join("\n"), "utf-8");
}

export function applyWorkspaceEdit(edit: WorkspaceEdit | null): ApplyResult {
  // iterates edit.changes and edit.documentChanges — create/rename/delete supported
}

idleTimer = setTimeout(() => {
  closed = true;
  log("idle_timeout", { idle_timeout_ms: idleTimeoutMs });
  void options.onIdleTimeout?.();
}, idleTimeoutMs);
