코퍼스 — 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_lc_sandbox.md (심화)

LazyCodex — Sandbox / Exec Policy

분석 스냅샷: /Users/jun/Developer/codex/161_lazycodex/ — no OS-level sandbox or execution policy engine; thin npm wrapper + Codex plugin bundle delegating isolation to Codex host and tool approval flows.

LazyCodex does not implement seatbelt, bwrap, seccomp, landlock, chroot, Docker sandboxes, network allowlists, or command classifiers. Policy surfaces are limited to: hook subprocess timeouts, post-edit block decisions (comment-checker, LSP), LSP language-server spawn with shell: false, and outbound telemetry opt-out flags.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph LC["LC policy surfaces"]
    HookT["Hook timeouts<br/>hooks.json"]
    Block["PostToolUse blocks<br/>comment-checker, LSP"]
    LSPSpawn["LSP spawn shell:false"]
    Tel["Telemetry opt-out<br/>env flags"]
  end
  subgraph Host["Codex host (not LC)"]
    SB["OS sandbox / seatbelt"]
    Guard["Tool approval / guardian"]
    Perm["permission_mode"]
  end
  User["User session"] --> Host
  Host --> LC
  LSPSpawn --> LS["User-configured<br/>language servers"]
  LS --> FS["Filesystem mutations<br/>via lsp.rename"]
  Guard --> FS
  SB --> Shell["bash/shell tool"]

1. Distribution shim — no policy

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:18-20
const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, {
  stdio: "inherit",
})
  • No argv filtering, no allowlist, no capability dropping
  • Inherits user UID/GID, full environment, network stack
  • Trust boundary: npm package oh-my-openagent from registry

2. Hook subprocess policy

Timeouts (Codex-enforced cancellation)

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json (selected)
SessionStart rules:     timeout 10
SessionStart telemetry: timeout 5
UserPromptSubmit rules: timeout 10
UserPromptSubmit ultrawork: timeout 5
UserPromptSubmit ultragoal: timeout 10
PostToolUse comment-checker: timeout 30
PostToolUse lsp:         timeout 60
PostToolUse rules:       timeout 10
PostCompact rules:       timeout 10

Hooks run as user subprocesses — no privilege separation from Codex process. Timeout is the primary resource bound LC configures.

Ultrawork hook — never blocks

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:10,290
# exit: 0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).
sys.exit(0)

Context injection only — not an execution gate.

3. Post-edit block policy

Comment checker

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:60-65
return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

Triggers on mutation tools matched in hooks.json (apply_patch|write|edit|...). Policy: block turn when AI-slop comment patterns detected in edited files. Skips on missing, pass, error statuses (:52-53).

This is quality policy, not OS sandbox — prevents proceeding with bad comments until addressed.

LSP diagnostics hook

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:27-28,55-57
const MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["apply_patch", "write", "edit", "multiedit", "multi_edit"]);
// ...
decision: "block",
reason,  // LSP error diagnostics after edit

Policy: block when executeLspDiagnostics reports errors (severity: "error", :31-33) after successful mutation. Clean diagnostics or unsupported extension → no block (CLEAN_DIAGNOSTICS_TEXT, UNSUPPORTED_EXTENSION_TEXT, :28-29).

Both hooks return JSON consumed by Codex hook runtime — host decides how block affects tool loop.

4. LSP MCP — primary execution surface

Spawn policy — no shell

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:24-28
export interface PreparedSpawnCommand {
  command: string;
  args: string[];
  shell: false;
}

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:159-161
if (platform !== "win32") {
  return { command: cmd, args, shell: false };
}

Windows resolves .cmd/.bat without shell: true (:112-172) — reduces shell injection risk but does not sandbox the LSP binary.

Cwd validation

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:41-57
export function validateCwd(cwd: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  if (!existsSync(cwd)) {
    return { valid: false, error: `Working directory does not exist: ${cwd}` };
  }
  const stats = statSync(cwd);
  if (!stats.isDirectory()) {
    return { valid: false, error: `Path is not a directory: ${cwd}` };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}

Invalid cwd → LspInvalidPathError at spawn (:175-178).

Process tree termination

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts:89-110
function killProcessTree(proc: ChildProcess, signal: NodeJS.Signals): void {
  if (process.platform === "win32" && proc.pid) {
    spawnSync("taskkill", ["/pid", String(proc.pid), "/f", "/t"], { stdio: "ignore" });
  }
  if (process.platform !== "win32" && proc.pid) {
    process.kill(-proc.pid, signal);  // process group
  }
}

Cleanup policy on MCP idle timeout (10 min) or server shutdown — not a security boundary.

5. Filesystem mutation via LSP rename

Highest-risk LC tool path:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:654-667
{
  name: "rename",
  aliases: ["lsp_rename"],
  description: "Rename a symbol across the workspace and apply the returned workspace edit.",
  // ...
}

Workspace edit application:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/workspace-edit.ts:19-48,59-79
function applyTextEditsToFile(filePath: string, edits: TextEdit[]): FileApplyResult {
  const content = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
  // ... apply edits ...
  writeFileSync(filePath, lines.join("\n"), "utf-8");
}

export function applyWorkspaceEdit(edit: WorkspaceEdit | null): ApplyResult {
  // iterates edit.changes and edit.documentChanges — create/rename/delete supported
}

Policy implication: Any file path LSP server returns in workspace edit can be modified. LC does not:

  • Restrict paths to workspace root beyond LSP server behavior
  • Require secondary approval (relies on Codex MCP tool approval)
  • Snapshot/rollback filesystem state

Language server commands come from user/project LSP config — untrusted config can specify arbitrary executables.

6. MCP server lifecycle bounds

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:46,96-100
const DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10 * 60_000;
idleTimer = setTimeout(() => {
  closed = true;
  log("idle_timeout", { idle_timeout_ms: idleTimeoutMs });
  void options.onIdleTimeout?.();
}, idleTimeoutMs);

Resource policy: terminate idle MCP after 10 minutes. Not network egress control.

7. Telemetry — outbound network surface

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog.ts:6,32-35
import { shouldDisablePostHog } from "./env-flags.js";
const NO_OP_POSTHOG: PostHogClient = {
  trackActive: () => undefined,
  shutdown: async () => undefined,
};

Opt-out via environment flags (shouldDisablePostHog). When enabled, SessionStart hook sends anonymized activity to PostHog — not prompt content, but OS metadata (getSharedProperties, :62-80: platform, cpu, memory).

Telemetry is outbound network — separate from model tool policy. Users disable via env documented in component README/env-flags.

8. Rules hook — context only, no block

Rules emit additionalContext — do not return decision: block in normal operation (codex-hook.ts:455-462). PostCompact clears cache only (:104-110).

Rules mode env config can disable injection (mode === "off", :136, :221) — behavioral policy, not exec sandbox.

9. Ultragoal — filesystem writes to .omo/

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/codex-hook.ts:25-31
const result = await steerUltragoal(payload.cwd, proposal);

Steering may write plan/ledger files under workspace .omo/ultragoal — user-project scoped, not system-wide. No path traversal audit documented in hook — trusts parsed steering syntax.

10. Codex host delegation (primary sandbox)

LC explicitly relies on Codex for:

Concern LC implementation
Shell command sandbox None — Codex shell tool + seatbelt/sandbox
Network egress for bash None — Codex policy
MCP tool approval None — Codex guardian
permission_mode Passed to hooks in stdin JSON — host-owned
Subagent isolation None — Codex scheduler

Hook inputs include permission_mode field (codex-hook.ts:32, :43, :54) — LC components can read but snapshot does not branch on it for sandbox behavior.

11. Absent mechanisms (negative evidence)

Not found in 161_lazycodex/:

Mechanism Status
seatbelt / sandbox-exec Absent
bubblewrap / firejail Absent
seccomp / landlock Absent
Docker / container runtime Absent
Network allow/deny lists Absent
Command argv classifier Absent
LC-owned approval protocol UI Absent
Capability dropping (setuid/setgid) Absent
Read-only root filesystem Absent

OmO submodule may implement additional OpenCode policies under src/ — not bundled into Codex plugin exec path.

12. Threat model summary

Threat LC mitigation Residual risk
Malicious LSP server command in config shell: false spawn Full user privileges
lsp.rename path traversal None in LC Codex approval + LSP server trust
Hook subprocess escape Same user as Codex Standard process trust model
Telemetry exfil Opt-out flags PostHog when enabled
bunx supply chain None in LC Trust npm + OmO package
AI editing bad comments comment-checker block Pattern-based, not exhaustive

13. Parity matrix — sandbox/exec

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
OS sandbox codex-rs sandbox / seatbelt Delegated
Shell tool policy Guardian + policy files Host-owned
MCP approval Host MCP gate Host gate for LSP MCP
Post-edit quality gate Optional LSP + comment-checker hooks
LSP subprocess N/A unless plugin shell: false, user LSP config
Network policy Host/config Telemetry opt-out only
Hook timeouts Host + hooks.json Configured in hooks.json

14. Operational guidance

  1. Treat LSP config as trusted — it specifies executables run as your user
  2. Use Codex permission modes and MCP approval for lsp.rename
  3. Disable telemetry if outbound analytics unacceptable (shouldDisablePostHog)
  4. Rely on Codex sandbox for shell/bash — LC does not add layers
  5. Ultrawork QA channels (HTTP/tmux/browser) in directive are orchestration policy — execution still under Codex tool policy

15. Test coverage

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70
// MCP server path isolation — component-local binary

LSP process spawn tests in components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/test/ and submodule src/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/test/mcp.test.ts.

16. Evidence quality bar

  • LSP spawn shell: false with line numbers
  • workspace-edit filesystem mutations documented
  • Block hook JSON shapes
  • Hook timeout inventory
  • Telemetry opt-out path
  • Negative: no OS sandbox in LC tree
  • Host delegation explicit

17. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 18-20 No policy in shim
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 3-97 Hook timeouts
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts 52-65 Block on slop comments
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts 27-63 Block on LSP errors
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/process.ts 24-28, 41-57, 89-178 Spawn policy
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/lsp/workspace-edit.ts 19-79 FS mutations
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts 654-667 rename tool
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts 46, 96-100 Idle timeout
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog.ts 6, 32-35, 62-80 Outbound telemetry
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py 10, 290 Non-blocking hook
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/codex-hook.ts 25-31 Goal file writes
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts 32, 43, 54 permission_mode in hook input

Related corpus docs

  • Tool runtime (LSP MCP): 003_tool-runtime/03_lc_tool.md
  • Prompt (hook context): 002_prompt-context/02_lc_prompt.md
  • Codex sandbox: 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_co_sandbox.md
  • Guardian/approval: 012_guardian-approval/12_co_guardian.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-06-27 WP38: initial stub
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — exec surfaces, LSP spawn, block hooks path:line

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
OS sandbox Codex host only LC unlikely to add native sandbox
LSP spawn shell:false Windows shim logic may evolve
Hook timeouts hooks.json values OmO may retune per hook
Block policies comment-checker + LSP New quality hooks possible
Telemetry PostHog opt-out Provider/flags may change
Codex permission_mode Passed to hooks Host semantics independent
25

축 08: 설정 시스템

AXIS

008_config-system/08_lc_config.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex configuration is three-layer: (1) a zero-parser CLI shim, (2) a frozen marketplace snapshot under .agents/plugins/, and (3) component-local env/path knobs inside the bundled plugins/omo aggregate plugin. There is no .lazycodexrc, no layered settings merge, and no model-router config in this repository.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. CLI wrapper — argument forwarding only
    1. Marketplace snapshot — sisyphuslabs
    1. Aggregate plugin manifest — config surface pointers
    1. Delegated install effects (OmO-owned)
    1. Rules component — env-driven config
    1. LSP component — client config paths
    1. Telemetry component — opt-out and state dir
    1. Ultragoal — repo-local state paths
    1. Hooks manifest — lifecycle wiring (not policy config)
    1. Build / workspace config
    1. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["bunx lazycodex-ai install|run"] --> Shim["bin/lazycodex-ai.js"]
  Shim --> Bunx["bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo ..."]
  Bunx --> OmOInstall["omo install --platform=codex"]
  OmOInstall --> CodexHome["~/.codex/config.toml + marketplace cache"]
  CodexHome --> Plugin["omo@sisyphuslabs plugin activation"]
  Plugin --> CompEnv["components/* env + path config"]
  CompEnv --> Rules["CODEX_RULES_* / PI_RULES_*"]
  CompEnv --> LSP[".codex/lsp-client.json"]
  CompEnv --> Telemetry["POSTHOG opt-out + activity state dir"]
  CompEnv --> Ultragoal[".omo/ultragoal/*.json"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:5-11
const args = process.argv.slice(2)
const dryRun = args[0] === "--dry-run"
const forwardedArgs = dryRun ? args.slice(1) : args
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "category": "Developer Tools",
    "policy": {
      "installation": "AVAILABLE",
      "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
    }
  }]
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14-16
"skills": "./skills/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:34-41
bunx lazycodex-ai install
# shorthand: bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex
bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/config.ts:5-20
export function configFromEnvironment(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): PiRulesConfig {
  const config = defaultConfig();
  const disableBundledRules = isTruthy(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_DISABLE_BUNDLED", "PI_RULES_DISABLE_BUNDLED"));
  config.disabled = isTruthy(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_DISABLED", "PI_RULES_DISABLED"));
  config.mode = parseMode(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MODE", "PI_RULES_MODE")) ?? config.mode;
  config.maxRuleChars = parsePositiveInteger(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MAX_RULE_CHARS", "PI_RULES_MAX_RULE_CHARS")) ?? config.maxRuleChars;
  config.maxResultChars = parsePositiveInteger(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MAX_RESULT_CHARS", "PI_RULES_MAX_RESULT_CHARS")) ?? config.maxResultChars;
  config.enabledSources = parseEnabledSources(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_ENABLED_SOURCES", "PI_RULES_ENABLED_SOURCES"), disableBundledRules);
  return config;
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot: string | undefined): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts:16-19
const POSTHOG_ACTIVITY_STATE_FILE = "posthog-activity.json"
function getPostHogActivityStateFilePath(): string {
  return join(getActivityStateDir(), POSTHOG_ACTIVITY_STATE_FILE)
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/paths.ts:4-17
export function ultragoalDir(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(repoRoot, ULTRAGOAL_DIR);
}
export function ultragoalGoalsPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_GOALS);
}
export function ultragoalLedgerPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_LEDGER);
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker", "components/rules", "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp", "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal", "components/ultrawork"
],
"scripts": {
  "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
  "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
}

코퍼스 — 008_config-system/08_lc_config.md (심화)

LazyCodex Config System

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex has no standalone config engine. It is a Codex Light distribution overlay: lazycodex-ai delegates to oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex, and real Codex TOML mutations are owned by the upstream OmO Codex installer.

LazyCodex configuration is three-layer: (1) a zero-parser CLI shim, (2) a frozen marketplace snapshot under .agents/plugins/, and (3) component-local env/path knobs inside the bundled plugins/omo aggregate plugin. There is no .lazycodexrc, no layered settings merge, and no model-router config in this repository.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["bunx lazycodex-ai install|run"] --> Shim["bin/lazycodex-ai.js"]
  Shim --> Bunx["bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo ..."]
  Bunx --> OmOInstall["omo install --platform=codex"]
  OmOInstall --> CodexHome["~/.codex/config.toml + marketplace cache"]
  CodexHome --> Plugin["omo@sisyphuslabs plugin activation"]
  Plugin --> CompEnv["components/* env + path config"]
  CompEnv --> Rules["CODEX_RULES_* / PI_RULES_*"]
  CompEnv --> LSP[".codex/lsp-client.json"]
  CompEnv --> Telemetry["POSTHOG opt-out + activity state dir"]
  CompEnv --> Ultragoal[".omo/ultragoal/*.json"]

1. CLI wrapper — argument forwarding only

LazyCodex's only first-party executable parses argv and spawns bunx; it never reads TOML, JSON config, or env-based routing tables.

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:5-11
const args = process.argv.slice(2)
const dryRun = args[0] === "--dry-run"
const forwardedArgs = dryRun ? args.slice(1) : args
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

Key behaviors:

Behavior Lines Notes
Install path injects --platform=codex :9-10 All Codex-specific config mutations happen inside OmO installer
Non-install forwards args verbatim :11 bunx ... omo <subcommand>
--dry-run prints command only :13-15 No side effects; useful for CI/docs
stdio: "inherit" :18-19 User sees OmO TUI/output directly
Exit code passthrough :27 result.status ?? 1

Boundary: Grep of 161_lazycodex/bin/ shows no config, toml, readFile, or settings parser — only spawnSync (:18).

2. Marketplace snapshot — sisyphuslabs

The repo ships a Codex marketplace manifest that pins the single omo plugin source path:

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:1-16
{
  "name": "sisyphuslabs",
  "interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" },
  "plugins": [{
    "name": "omo",
    "source": "./plugins/omo",
    "category": "Developer Tools",
    "policy": {
      "installation": "AVAILABLE",
      "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
    }
  }]
}
Field Role
name: sisyphuslabs Marketplace identity registered with Codex host
source: ./plugins/omo Relative path to aggregate plugin root in this checkout
policy.installation: AVAILABLE Plugin listed for install without extra gating in manifest
policy.authentication: ON_INSTALL Auth handshake deferred to install-time (host/OmO)

LazyCodex does not implement marketplace CRUD APIs — it vends this JSON for OmO's --platform=codex installer to copy/register.

3. Aggregate plugin manifest — config surface pointers

The omo plugin's .codex-plugin/plugin.json is the local config index for skills, hooks, and MCP:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:14-16
"skills": "./skills/",
"hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",

Additional interface metadata (:17-33) documents capabilities and default prompts but does not define runtime policy engines:

Capability (manifest) Component backing
Hooks hooks/hooks.json → per-component CLI subprocesses
MCP Tools .mcp.json → lsp-tools-mcp stdio server
Code Intelligence components/lsp/
Workflow components/ultrawork/, components/ultragoal/
Context Injection components/rules/

4. Delegated install effects (OmO-owned)

README documents the user-facing install contract:

# 161_lazycodex/README.md:34-41
bunx lazycodex-ai install
# shorthand: bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex
bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

What LazyCodex-local source does NOT implement (delegated to 172_oh-my-openagent installer):

Config mutation Owner LC evidence
~/.codex/config.toml merge OmO install-codex/codex-config-toml.ts Referenced in prior corpus pass; not in LC tree
CODEX_HOME resolution OmO + Codex host Wrapper never touches
Plugin cache paths Codex host after install LC ships source snapshot only
Autonomous permissions flag --codex-autonomous forwarded via install args README :41
Bundled agent TOML registration OmO installer LC bundles TOMLs under components/ultrawork/agents/

5. Rules component — env-driven config

Rules are the richest in-repo config surface. configFromEnvironment maps env vars to engine knobs:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/config.ts:5-20
export function configFromEnvironment(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): PiRulesConfig {
  const config = defaultConfig();
  const disableBundledRules = isTruthy(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_DISABLE_BUNDLED", "PI_RULES_DISABLE_BUNDLED"));
  config.disabled = isTruthy(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_DISABLED", "PI_RULES_DISABLED"));
  config.mode = parseMode(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MODE", "PI_RULES_MODE")) ?? config.mode;
  config.maxRuleChars = parsePositiveInteger(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MAX_RULE_CHARS", "PI_RULES_MAX_RULE_CHARS")) ?? config.maxRuleChars;
  config.maxResultChars = parsePositiveInteger(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_MAX_RESULT_CHARS", "PI_RULES_MAX_RESULT_CHARS")) ?? config.maxResultChars;
  config.enabledSources = parseEnabledSources(firstEnv(env, "CODEX_RULES_ENABLED_SOURCES", "PI_RULES_ENABLED_SOURCES"), disableBundledRules);
  return config;
}
Env var(s) Effect Parser lines
CODEX_RULES_DISABLED / PI_RULES_DISABLED Master off switch :8-9
CODEX_RULES_MODE / PI_RULES_MODE static | dynamic | both | off :38-49
CODEX_RULES_MAX_RULE_CHARS Per-rule char budget :52-55
CODEX_RULES_MAX_RESULT_CHARS Injection size cap :13-15
CODEX_RULES_ENABLED_SOURCES Comma-separated source list or auto :58-72
CODEX_RULES_DISABLE_BUNDLED Strips plugin-bundled from sources :7, 75-76

Session cache path (persistent dedupe state, not "memory extraction"):

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot: string | undefined): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

Default on-disk layout: ~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/<sessionId>.json unless PLUGIN_DATA overrides.

6. LSP component — client config paths

LSP MCP and post-edit hooks consume LSP client configuration from Codex-standard paths (component reads .codex/lsp-client.json and ~/.codex/lsp-client.json — see components/lsp/ README and server-resolution modules). LazyCodex does not ship a top-level lsp.toml; language server mappings live in those JSON files plus env path overrides inside the LSP package.

The MCP registration is minimal:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

7. Telemetry component — opt-out and state dir

Telemetry stores daily dedupe state only (posthog-activity.json), not user content:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts:16-19
const POSTHOG_ACTIVITY_STATE_FILE = "posthog-activity.json"
function getPostHogActivityStateFilePath(): string {
  return join(getActivityStateDir(), POSTHOG_ACTIVITY_STATE_FILE)
}

Capture logic fires at most one "active day" event per UTC day (:63-80). Config knobs are env-driven in data-path.ts (typically $XDG_DATA_HOME / ~/.local/share activity dir) — operational telemetry, not product settings.

8. Ultragoal — repo-local state paths

Ultragoal config is filesystem-native under .omo/ultragoal/:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/paths.ts:4-17
export function ultragoalDir(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(repoRoot, ULTRAGOAL_DIR);
}
export function ultragoalGoalsPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_GOALS);
}
export function ultragoalLedgerPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_LEDGER);
}

These paths are relative to the git repo root, not ~/.codex — important when classifying "config" vs "memory".

9. Hooks manifest — lifecycle wiring (not policy config)

hooks/hooks.json binds hook events to component CLIs with timeouts:

Hook event Component commands Timeout
SessionStart rules + telemetry 10s / 5s (:3-22)
UserPromptSubmit rules + ultrawork detector + ultragoal steering 10s / 5s / 10s (:24-53)
PostToolUse comment-checker + lsp (+ rules on apply_patch) 30s / 60s / 10s (:55-83)
PostCompact rules cache reset 10s (:85-97)

Not present: PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, or sandbox policy hooks — execution approval remains Codex host-owned.

10. Build / workspace config

Aggregate plugin package.json defines npm workspaces for all components:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:8-21
"workspaces": [
  "components/comment-checker", "components/rules", "components/lsp",
  "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp", "components/telemetry",
  "components/ultragoal", "components/ultrawork"
],
"scripts": {
  "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
  "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
}

aggregate.test.mjs verifies build script ordering (:73-84) — config for release integrity, not runtime user prefs.

11. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Missing surface Where it lives instead
.lazycodexrc / layered merge engine Absent — no parser in LC tree
Model routing tables OmO + Codex host
Provider auth / API keys Codex credentials store
MDM / enterprise policy Codex host (if enabled)
MCP server registry beyond lsp OmO OpenCode path; LC bundles one server
Web search config Host config.toml / librarian agent prompts (see 14_lc_web-search.md)

12. Parity matrix — config axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
Primary config file ~/.codex/config.toml Delegated via OmO install
Local repo config .codex/ project files .omo/ultragoal/, rules in repo
Env overrides CODEX_* host vars CODEX_RULES_* / PI_RULES_* in rules component
Plugin manifest Host-resolved .codex-plugin/plugin.json + marketplace.json
CLI entry codex binary lazycodex-ai.js → bunx omo
Autonomous mode Host permissions --codex-autonomous install flag (forwarded)

13. Documentation drift (config-relevant)

README states "Currently available for OpenCode" (README.md:25) while the wrapper hardcodes --platform=codex (lazycodex-ai.js:10). Treat marketing copy as non-authoritative for config behavior; trust the shim + marketplace JSON.

14. Error handling — wrapper only

Failure Behavior Lines
spawnSync error console.error + exit 1 lazycodex-ai.js:22-24
Non-zero OmO exit Passthrough status :27
Missing bunx Node spawn error surfaced :22-24

No retry, no fallback installer path — fail fast per AGENTS.md corpus rules.

15. Evidence quality bar

  • Primary paths under 161_lazycodex/ with line numbers
  • Negative claims: no .lazycodexrc, no config parser in wrapper
  • Delegation boundary to OmO install documented
  • Component env config (rules) with full env var table
  • Marketplace + plugin manifest citations
  • Hooks manifest as wiring config, not permission engine

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role

161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json 1-16 Marketplace snapshot

날짜 변경

Area Snapshot Current

축 09: MCP 오케스트레이션

AXIS

009_mcp-orchestration/09_lc_mcp.md

기초 설명

MCP in LazyCodex means: Codex host reads plugins/omo/.mcp.json, spawns a stdio JSON-RPC process (lsp-tools-mcp), and routes model tool calls to LSP operations. LazyCodex does not implement discovery, auth, approval mediation, or remote SSE transports.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Aggregate manifest pointer
    1. .mcp.json — single server registration
    1. Aggregate test — MCP isolation contract
    1. JSON-RPC server — mcp.ts
  • Request dispatch (:49-76)
  • Initialize response constants (:44-46, 58-64)
  • Tool call path (:136-150)
    1. Stdio loop — idle timeout orchestration
    1. Tool registry — LSP_MCP_TOOLS
    1. Hook ↔ MCP integration (dual path)
    1. Hooks manifest — MCP-adjacent wiring

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  CodexHost["Codex host MCP client"] --> Manifest["plugins/omo/.mcp.json"]
  Manifest --> Spawn["node lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js mcp"]
  Spawn --> Stdio["stdio JSON-RPC loop"]
  Stdio --> Methods["initialize | tools/list | tools/call"]
  Methods --> LspMgr["LSP manager + language servers"]
  LspMgr --> Tools["diagnostics | goto_definition | rename | ..."]
  PostHook["PostToolUse LSP hook"] --> Tools

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:16
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70
test("#given aggregate MCP config #when inspected #then lsp server stays component isolated", async () => {
  const mcp = await readJson(".mcp.json");
  const server = mcp.mcpServers.lsp;
  assert.equal(server.command, "node");
  assert.deepEqual(server.args, ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"]);
  assert.equal(server.cwd, ".");
});

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:49-76
export async function handleLspMcpRequest(input: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse | undefined> {
  if (!isRecord(input)) return errorResponse(null, -32600, "Invalid Request");
  const id = jsonRpcId(input["id"]);
  const method = input["method"];
  if (method === "notifications/initialized") return undefined;
  if (method === "ping") return successResponse(id, {});
  if (method === "initialize") { /* capabilities + serverInfo */ }
  if (method === "tools/list") {
    return successResponse(id, { tools: LSP_MCP_TOOLS.map(describeTool) });
  }
  if (method === "tools/call") {
    return handleToolCall(id, input["params"]);
  }
  return errorResponse(id, -32601, `Method not found: ${String(method)}`);
}

README / docs

const SERVER_NAME = "lsp";
const SERVER_VERSION = "0.1.0";
// initialize returns:
// capabilities: { tools: { listChanged: false } }
// serverInfo: { name: SERVER_NAME, version: SERVER_VERSION }

README / docs

async function handleToolCall(id: JsonRpcId, params: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse> {
  if (!isRecord(params) || typeof params["name"] !== "string") {
    return errorResponse(id, -32602, "tools/call requires params.name");
  }
  try {
    const result = await executeLspTool(params["name"], coerceToolArguments(params["arguments"]));
    return successResponse(id, { content: result.content, isError: result.isError ?? false, details: result.details });
  } catch (error) {
    return successResponse(id, { content: [{ type: "text", text: messageFromError(error) }], isError: true });
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:78-133
export async function runMcpStdioServer(...) {
  const idleTimeoutMs = options.idleTimeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS; // 10 * 60_000
  // ...
  for await (const line of lines) {
    armIdleTimer();
    const response = await handleLspMcpRequest(parsed);
    if (response) output.write(`${JSON.stringify(response)}\n`);
  }
}

README / docs

// tools.ts:574-584
inputSchema: objectSchema({
  filePath: { type: "string", description: "File or directory path to check." },
  severity: { type: "string", enum: ["error", "warning", "information", "hint", "all"], ... },
}, ["filePath"]),

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:31-63
export async function runLspPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const filePaths = extractMutatedFilePaths(input);
  // ...
  const output: PostToolUseHookOutput = {
    decision: "block",
    reason,
    hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: "PostToolUse", additionalContext: reason },
  };
  return `${JSON.stringify(output)}\n`;
}

README / docs

"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$",
"command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/lsp/dist/cli.js\" hook post-tool-use",
"timeout": 60

코퍼스 — 009_mcp-orchestration/09_lc_mcp.md (심화)

LazyCodex MCP Orchestration

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex exposes one MCP server (lsp) via the bundled omo Codex plugin. There is no MCP manager, OAuth broker, multi-server orchestration runtime, or web-search MCP in this repository.

MCP in LazyCodex means: Codex host reads plugins/omo/.mcp.json, spawns a stdio JSON-RPC process (lsp-tools-mcp), and routes model tool calls to LSP operations. LazyCodex does not implement discovery, auth, approval mediation, or remote SSE transports.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  CodexHost["Codex host MCP client"] --> Manifest["plugins/omo/.mcp.json"]
  Manifest --> Spawn["node lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js mcp"]
  Spawn --> Stdio["stdio JSON-RPC loop"]
  Stdio --> Methods["initialize | tools/list | tools/call"]
  Methods --> LspMgr["LSP manager + language servers"]
  LspMgr --> Tools["diagnostics | goto_definition | rename | ..."]
  PostHook["PostToolUse LSP hook"] --> Tools

1. Aggregate manifest pointer

The plugin declares MCP config location:

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:16
"mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",

Capabilities list includes "MCP Tools" (:23) alongside Hooks and Code Intelligence — marketing metadata only; runtime is entirely in .mcp.json + lsp-tools-mcp.

2. .mcp.json — single server registration

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}
Field Value Implication
Server key lsp Only registered MCP id
Transport stdio (implicit) No url, no SSE
Command node Spawns Node subprocess
Args .../cli.js mcp CLI subcommand enters MCP mode
cwd . Plugin root — paths relative to plugins/omo/

Negative finding: Grep of 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json shows no exa, tavily, web_search, or browser MCP entries.

3. Aggregate test — MCP isolation contract

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70
test("#given aggregate MCP config #when inspected #then lsp server stays component isolated", async () => {
  const mcp = await readJson(".mcp.json");
  const server = mcp.mcpServers.lsp;
  assert.equal(server.command, "node");
  assert.deepEqual(server.args, ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"]);
  assert.equal(server.cwd, ".");
});

This test encodes the orchestration boundary: MCP command paths must stay under components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/, not per-component plugin ids like codex-lsp@... (see hooks isolation test :57).

4. JSON-RPC server — mcp.ts

Request dispatch (:49-76)

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:49-76
export async function handleLspMcpRequest(input: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse | undefined> {
  if (!isRecord(input)) return errorResponse(null, -32600, "Invalid Request");
  const id = jsonRpcId(input["id"]);
  const method = input["method"];
  if (method === "notifications/initialized") return undefined;
  if (method === "ping") return successResponse(id, {});
  if (method === "initialize") { /* capabilities + serverInfo */ }
  if (method === "tools/list") {
    return successResponse(id, { tools: LSP_MCP_TOOLS.map(describeTool) });
  }
  if (method === "tools/call") {
    return handleToolCall(id, input["params"]);
  }
  return errorResponse(id, -32601, `Method not found: ${String(method)}`);
}

Supported methods:

Method Handler Notes
initialize :58-64 protocolVersion, serverInfo.name=lsp, capabilities.tools
tools/list :67-68 Exposes full LSP_MCP_TOOLS registry
tools/call :71-72 Delegates to executeLspTool
ping :57 Health check
notifications/initialized :56 No response (notification)

Not implemented: resources/*, prompts/*, OAuth, sampling/* — LSP-only subset.

Initialize response constants (:44-46, 58-64)

const SERVER_NAME = "lsp";
const SERVER_VERSION = "0.1.0";
// initialize returns:
// capabilities: { tools: { listChanged: false } }
// serverInfo: { name: SERVER_NAME, version: SERVER_VERSION }

Tool call path (:136-150)

async function handleToolCall(id: JsonRpcId, params: unknown): Promise<JsonRpcResponse> {
  if (!isRecord(params) || typeof params["name"] !== "string") {
    return errorResponse(id, -32602, "tools/call requires params.name");
  }
  try {
    const result = await executeLspTool(params["name"], coerceToolArguments(params["arguments"]));
    return successResponse(id, { content: result.content, isError: result.isError ?? false, details: result.details });
  } catch (error) {
    return successResponse(id, { content: [{ type: "text", text: messageFromError(error) }], isError: true });
  }
}

Tool execution errors return MCP success with isError: true — host sees text error, not JSON-RPC fault (except malformed params).

5. Stdio loop — idle timeout orchestration

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts:78-133
export async function runMcpStdioServer(...) {
  const idleTimeoutMs = options.idleTimeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS; // 10 * 60_000
  // ...
  for await (const line of lines) {
    armIdleTimer();
    const response = await handleLspMcpRequest(parsed);
    if (response) output.write(`${JSON.stringify(response)}\n`);
  }
}
Parameter Default Role
DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS 600_000 (10 min) :46
idleTimer.unref() — :101 — won't keep process alive alone
Parse errors -32700 Parse error :117
Lifecycle logging stdio_started, request, response :104, 123, 127

Orchestration note: Codex host owns process spawn; lsp-tools-mcp owns idle shutdown — not LazyCodex CLI.

6. Tool registry — LSP_MCP_TOOLS

Eight tools exported (tools.ts:560-668):

Tool name Aliases Purpose
status lsp_status List configured/active LSP servers (:562-567)
diagnostics lsp_diagnostics File/directory diagnostics (:570-585)
goto_definition lsp_goto_definition Symbol definition (:588-600)
find_references lsp_find_references Cross-ref search (:603-616)
symbols lsp_symbols Document/workspace symbols (:619-636)
prepare_rename lsp_prepare_rename Rename feasibility (:639-651)
rename lsp_rename Apply workspace rename edit (:654-667)

Example schema fragment — diagnostics:

// tools.ts:574-584
inputSchema: objectSchema({
  filePath: { type: "string", description: "File or directory path to check." },
  severity: { type: "string", enum: ["error", "warning", "information", "hint", "all"], ... },
}, ["filePath"]),

Rename orchestration (:517-523): client.rename → applyWorkspaceEdit(edit) — MCP tool can mutate workspace files via LSP workspace edit, distinct from Codex apply_patch.

7. Hook ↔ MCP integration (dual path)

Same LSP stack serves two entry points:

  1. MCP tools — model-initiated via Codex MCP client
  2. PostToolUse hook — automatic diagnostics after edits
// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:31-63
export async function runLspPostToolUseHook(...) {
  const filePaths = extractMutatedFilePaths(input);
  // ...
  const output: PostToolUseHookOutput = {
    decision: "block",
    reason,
    hookSpecificOutput: { hookEventName: "PostToolUse", additionalContext: reason },
  };
  return `${JSON.stringify(output)}\n`;
}

Hook imports executeLspDiagnostics from @code-yeongyu/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/tools.js (:3) — shared tool implementation, different orchestration (hook JSON vs MCP JSON-RPC).

Mutation tools matched (:27): apply_patch, write, edit, multiedit, multi_edit.

8. Hooks manifest — MCP-adjacent wiring

hooks/hooks.json PostToolUse matcher for LSP (:55-71):

"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$",
"command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/lsp/dist/cli.js\" hook post-tool-use",
"timeout": 60

60s timeout > comment-checker's 30s — LSP subprocess spawn + diagnostics aggregation can be slow on large workspaces.

9. Orchestration boundary vs OmO OpenCode

Capability OmO (full harness) LazyCodex bundle
Multiple MCP servers Yes (Exa, docs, etc. in OmO tree) One (lsp)
MCP OAuth / remote OmO connector layer Absent
MCP marketplace OmO plugin ecosystem Frozen sisyphuslabs snapshot
Tool approval Host + OmO hooks Host only; LC hooks block post-facto
LSP MCP Present in both lsp-tools-mcp vendored under plugins/omo/components/lsp/

For full MCP breadth, read OmO corpus docs (14_omo_web-search.md, etc.). LazyCodex evidence supports aggregate Codex plugin LSP MCP only.

10. Parity matrix — MCP axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
MCP registry config.toml + merge .mcp.json single entry
Server count Multiple optional 1 (lsp)
Transport stdio / SSE per server stdio only
Tool routing codex-rs MCP client Host routes; LC defines LSP tools
OAuth Host MCP auth flows None in LC
Web search MCP Host / other plugins None — librarian uses host tools

11. Security notes

  • Subprocess spawn: MCP server runs node with plugin-relative paths — trust boundary is Codex plugin install integrity.
  • Workspace edits: rename tool applies LSP workspace edits — can modify many files in one MCP call (tools.ts:517-523).
  • No URL fetch: LSP tools operate on local paths only — no SSRF surface in MCP layer (contrast web-search axis).
  • Idle timeout: Server self-terminates after 10 min idle — limits orphaned language-server processes.

12. Test coverage

Test file Assertion
plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs:60-70 MCP command/path isolation
src/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/test/mcp.test.ts JSON-RPC request/response (upstream package in LC submodule)

Aggregate test also verifies hooks stay component-isolated (:38-57) — MCP and hooks share isolation discipline.

13. CLI entry — cli.js mcp

Spawn args from .mcp.json target ./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js mcp. The mcp subcommand invokes runMcpStdioServer() (see cli.ts in same package). Build prerequisite: npm run build in aggregate plugin workspace (package.json:18).

14. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Missing Evidence
MCP server discovery No scanner code in 161_lazycodex/
Dynamic server registration Static .mcp.json only
MCP auth / token refresh No OAuth modules
Multi-server load balancing Single lsp key
Web/document MCP .mcp.json grep negative
Tool approval queue Hooks return block post-tool, not pre-tool MCP gate

15. Evidence quality bar

  • .mcp.json + plugin.json citations with lines
  • Full JSON-RPC method table from mcp.ts
  • All 8 LSP MCP tools enumerated with line refs
  • Hook/MCP dual-path documented
  • Negative claims: no web MCP, no OAuth
  • Aggregate test as orchestration contract

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 16, 23 MCP manifest pointer
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json 1-8 LSP server spawn config
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 55-71 PostToolUse LSP hook
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/mcp.ts 44-76, 78-133, 136-150 JSON-RPC server
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts 560-668 Tool registry
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts 3, 27-63 Hook integration
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs 60-70 MCP isolation test

Related corpus docs

  • Config pointers: 008_config-system/08_lc_config.md
  • Plugin aggregate: 011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md
  • Guardian blocks (LSP hook): 012_guardian-approval/12_lc_guardian.md
  • Codex MCP depth: 009_mcp-orchestration/09_co_mcp.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC MCP axis
2026-06-27 WP38: citations + parity vs co
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — JSON-RPC + tool registry

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
MCP server count 1 (lsp) OmO may add servers; LC snapshot may resync
Tool names 8 LSP tools Aliases stable; schemas may evolve
Idle timeout 10 min default Tunable via server options if host exposes
Host MCP client Codex closed source Transport/features may expand independently
27

축 10: 메모리 파이프라인

AXIS

010_memory-pipeline/10_lc_memory.md

기초 설명

Do not classify LazyCodex as extracting facts from prior sessions, building vector indexes, or owning a replay/resume database. It packages context injection, workflow evidence, and audit ledgers — not semantic long-term memory.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Memory layer taxonomy
    1. Rules — session cache (dedupe, not semantic memory)
  • Cache path resolution
  • Serialized state shape (:14-20, 51-56)
  • Hydrate / persist cycle (:22-57)
  • Post-compact invalidation (:63-73, 118-126)
  • Rules hook events (codex-hook.ts:24-69)
    1. Ultrawork — ephemeral notepad + directive injection
  • Detector hook contract
  • Directive content — evidence requirements (:25-45)
  • Bundled agents (memory-adjacent roles)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  subgraph persistent ["Persistent (disk)"]
    RulesCache["~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/*.json"]
    Ultragoal[".omo/ultragoal/goals.json + ledger.jsonl"]
    Telemetry["posthog-activity.json (dedupe only)"]
  end
  subgraph ephemeral ["Ephemeral / session"]
    Ultrawork["/tmp/ulw-*.md notepad"]
    CodexTranscript["Codex host transcript (not LC-owned)"]
  end
  subgraph injection ["Context injection (not recall)"]
    RulesHook["rules codex-hook → additional_context"]
    UltraworkDir["ultrawork-detector.py directive"]
    UltragoalSteer["ultragoal steering hook"]
  end
  RulesCache --> RulesHook
  Ultrawork --> UltraworkDir
  Ultragoal --> UltragoalSteer

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot: string | undefined): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

README / docs

interface SerializedSessionState {
  staticDedup: string[];
  dynamicDedup: Record<string, string[]>;
  dynamicTargetFingerprints?: Record<string, string>;
  postCompactPending?: PostCompactPendingState;
  compacted?: boolean;
}

README / docs

export function hydrateEngineState(engine: Engine, cachePath: string): void {
  const state = readSessionState(cachePath);
  for (const key of state.staticDedup) engine.state.staticDedup.add(key);
  for (const [scope, keys] of Object.entries(state.dynamicDedup)) {
    engine.state.dynamicDedup.set(scope, new Set(keys));
  }
}
export function persistEngineState(engine: Engine, cachePath: string, ...): void {
  writeSessionState(cachePath, { staticDedup: [...], dynamicDedup, ... });
}

README / docs

export function markSessionCompacted(cachePath: string): void {
  writeSessionState(cachePath, { ...emptyState(), postCompactPending: { static: true, dynamic: true } });
}

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:1-10
"""Codex UserPromptSubmit hook: inject ultrawork directive on `ulw`/`ultrawork`.
  stdin:  JSON {cwd, hook_event_name="UserPromptSubmit", ... prompt ...}
  stdout: directive text or empty → treated as additional_context
  exit:   0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).
"""

README / docs

ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/paths.ts:4-17
export function ultragoalDir(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(repoRoot, ULTRAGOAL_DIR);  // .omo/ultragoal
}
export function ultragoalGoalsPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_GOALS);  // goals.json
}
export function ultragoalLedgerPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_LEDGER);  // ledger.jsonl
}

README / docs

export function validateQualityGate(input: unknown): UltragoalQualityGate {
  if (cleaner["status"] !== "passed") invalid(...);
  if (verification["status"] !== "passed") invalid(...);
  if (review["recommendation"] !== "APPROVE") invalid(...);
  if (review["architectStatus"] !== "CLEAR") invalid(...);
  // criteriaCoverage.passCount >= totalCriteria
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts:63-80
export function getPostHogActivityCaptureState(now: Date = new Date()): PostHogActivityCaptureState {
  const state = readPostHogActivityState();
  const dayUTC = getUtcDayString(now);
  const captureDaily = state.lastActiveDayUTC !== dayUTC;
  if (captureDaily) writePostHogActivityState({ ...state, lastActiveDayUTC: dayUTC });
  return { dayUTC, captureDaily };
}

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant Codex
  participant Rules
  participant Cache as codex-rules/sessions/*.json
  participant UG as .omo/ultragoal/

  User->>Codex: prompt
  Codex->>Rules: UserPromptSubmit hook
  Rules->>Cache: hydrate + persist dedupe keys
  Rules-->>Codex: additional_context (rules text)
  Note over Cache: Cleared on PostCompact pending reinject
  User->>Codex: ultragoal checkpoint
  Codex->>UG: append ledger.jsonl

코퍼스 — 010_memory-pipeline/10_lc_memory.md (심화)

LazyCodex Memory Pipeline

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex has no independent learned-memory pipeline. Memory surfaces are packaged OmO/Codex plugin components: rules session cache, ultrawork ephemeral notepad, ultragoal repo-native ledger, telemetry daily dedupe, and inherited OmO workflow docs.

Do not classify LazyCodex as extracting facts from prior sessions, building vector indexes, or owning a replay/resume database. It packages context injection, workflow evidence, and audit ledgers — not semantic long-term memory.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph persistent ["Persistent (disk)"]
    RulesCache["~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/*.json"]
    Ultragoal[".omo/ultragoal/goals.json + ledger.jsonl"]
    Telemetry["posthog-activity.json (dedupe only)"]
  end
  subgraph ephemeral ["Ephemeral / session"]
    Ultrawork["/tmp/ulw-*.md notepad"]
    CodexTranscript["Codex host transcript (not LC-owned)"]
  end
  subgraph injection ["Context injection (not recall)"]
    RulesHook["rules codex-hook → additional_context"]
    UltraworkDir["ultrawork-detector.py directive"]
    UltragoalSteer["ultragoal steering hook"]
  end
  RulesCache --> RulesHook
  Ultrawork --> UltraworkDir
  Ultragoal --> UltragoalSteer

1. Memory layer taxonomy

Layer Persistent? Path / state Role LC-owned?
CLI wrapper No lazycodex-ai Distribution entrypoint Shim only
Rules cache Yes, per session ~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/<id>.json Dedupe static/dynamic rule injections Component
Ultrawork notepad Temporary /tmp/ulw-*.md (per directive) Working evidence trail Component
Ultragoal Repo-native .omo/ultragoal/goals.json, ledger.jsonl Durable goals + audit Component
Telemetry Operational posthog-activity.json Daily event dedupe only Component
Boulder/start-work Inherited OmO .omo/boulder.json (docs) Cross-ref OmO Docs only
Vector / MEMORY.md Absent — Not implemented No

2. Rules — session cache (dedupe, not semantic memory)

Cache path resolution

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts:75-77
export function sessionCachePath(sessionId: string, pluginDataRoot: string | undefined): string {
  const root = pluginDataRoot ?? process.env["PLUGIN_DATA"] ?? join(homedir(), ".codex", "codex-rules");
  return join(root, "sessions", `${safePathSegment(sessionId)}.json`);
}

Default root: ~/.codex/codex-rules/sessions/ unless PLUGIN_DATA env overrides (:76).

Serialized state shape (:14-20, 51-56)

interface SerializedSessionState {
  staticDedup: string[];
  dynamicDedup: Record<string, string[]>;
  dynamicTargetFingerprints?: Record<string, string>;
  postCompactPending?: PostCompactPendingState;
  compacted?: boolean;
}

What is stored: rule injection keys already sent this session — prevents duplicate context spam. What is NOT stored: extracted user facts, embeddings, or cross-session recall.

Hydrate / persist cycle (:22-57)

export function hydrateEngineState(engine: Engine, cachePath: string): void {
  const state = readSessionState(cachePath);
  for (const key of state.staticDedup) engine.state.staticDedup.add(key);
  for (const [scope, keys] of Object.entries(state.dynamicDedup)) {
    engine.state.dynamicDedup.set(scope, new Set(keys));
  }
}
export function persistEngineState(engine: Engine, cachePath: string, ...): void {
  writeSessionState(cachePath, { staticDedup: [...], dynamicDedup, ... });
}

Post-compact invalidation (:63-73, 118-126)

After Codex compaction, rules hook marks pending re-injection:

export function markSessionCompacted(cachePath: string): void {
  writeSessionState(cachePath, { ...emptyState(), postCompactPending: { static: true, dynamic: true } });
}

Hook wiring: hooks/hooks.json:85-97 → post-compact CLI resets cache with status "resetting OMO project rule cache".

Rules hook events (codex-hook.ts:24-69)

Event Purpose
SessionStart Load static rules + hydrate cache
UserPromptSubmit Dynamic rule matching on prompt
PostToolUse Target fingerprint updates after apply_patch
PostCompact Mark compacted; pending reinjection

Output format: JSON with additional_context for Codex host — injection, not retrieval from a memory DB.

3. Ultrawork — ephemeral notepad + directive injection

Detector hook contract

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py:1-10
"""Codex UserPromptSubmit hook: inject ultrawork directive on `ulw`/`ultrawork`.
  stdin:  JSON {cwd, hook_event_name="UserPromptSubmit", ... prompt ...}
  stdout: directive text or empty → treated as additional_context
  exit:   0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).
"""

Keyword match (:21-22):

ULTRAWORK_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:ultrawork|ulw)\b", re.IGNORECASE)

Directive content — evidence requirements (:25-45)

Ultrawork mode mandates:

  • First line: ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLED!
  • RED→GREEN test-first gate
  • Manual QA via HTTP / tmux / browser / computer-use channels
  • Notepad path for evidence trail (typically /tmp/ulw-*.md)

This is procedural memory for one task, not durable user profile memory.

Bundled agents (memory-adjacent roles)

aggregate.test.mjs:110-122 verifies TOMLs:

Agent File Memory role
explorer explorer.toml Local codebase search — not internet
librarian librarian.toml External OSS research via host web tools
plan plan.toml Strategic planning output to plans/
codex-ultrawork-reviewer codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml Verification gate

Explorer explicitly excludes internet (explorer.toml:68): "External research is the librarian's job."

4. Ultragoal — repo-native goal ledger

Path helpers

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/paths.ts:4-17
export function ultragoalDir(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(repoRoot, ULTRAGOAL_DIR);  // .omo/ultragoal
}
export function ultragoalGoalsPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_GOALS);  // goals.json
}
export function ultragoalLedgerPath(repoRoot: string): string {
  return join(ultragoalDir(repoRoot), ULTRAGOAL_LEDGER);  // ledger.jsonl
}

Persistence model: append-only ledger + structured goals file — audit trail for multi-session objectives, not RAG memory.

Steering hook

hooks/hooks.json:45-52 — UserPromptSubmit runs ultragoal steering with 10s timeout, status "checking OMO ultragoal steering".

Quality gate validation (quality-gate.ts:45-70)

Completion requires structured evidence blocks:

export function validateQualityGate(input: unknown): UltragoalQualityGate {
  if (cleaner["status"] !== "passed") invalid(...);
  if (verification["status"] !== "passed") invalid(...);
  if (review["recommendation"] !== "APPROVE") invalid(...);
  if (review["architectStatus"] !== "CLEAR") invalid(...);
  // criteriaCoverage.passCount >= totalCriteria
}

This is governance memory (what evidence was accepted), not semantic recall.

5. Telemetry — dedupe state only

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts:63-80
export function getPostHogActivityCaptureState(now: Date = new Date()): PostHogActivityCaptureState {
  const state = readPostHogActivityState();
  const dayUTC = getUtcDayString(now);
  const captureDaily = state.lastActiveDayUTC !== dayUTC;
  if (captureDaily) writePostHogActivityState({ ...state, lastActiveDayUTC: dayUTC });
  return { dayUTC, captureDaily };
}

Stores only lastActiveDayUTC — no conversation content, no tool logs, no embeddings.

SessionStart hook (hooks/hooks.json:14-22) fires telemetry with 5s timeout.

6. What is NOT a memory pipeline

Feature Status in LC Notes
MEMORY.md / profile files Absent No grep hits in 161_lazycodex/
SQLite / FTS memory index Absent Rules use JSON session files only
Vector embeddings Absent No embedding client in bundled components
Cross-session fact extraction Absent Rules cache is per-session dedupe
Chat replay database Codex host LC reads transcript_path in hooks but doesn't own store
Session resume DB Codex host SessionStart input includes source: resume (codex-hook.ts:33)

7. Hook transcript fields (read-only consumption)

Codex hooks receive transcript_path in stdin JSON (codex-hook.ts:28, 38, 50) — path to host transcript file. Components may read for context (comment-checker :49) but LC source shows no writer creating a parallel memory index from transcripts.

8. Inherited OmO workflow docs

packages/web/content/docs/start-work.md (referenced in prior corpus) documents Boulder / plan execution — marketing/docs layer, not runtime memory engine in LC checkout.

README workflow commands (README.md:48-52):

Command Output artifact
$ulw-loop Loop until Oracle verification
$ulw-plan plans/<slug>.md
$start-work Plan checkbox execution

Plan files are human-readable artifacts, not indexed memory.

9. Parity matrix — memory axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc) Grok-style agent
Session transcript Host-owned Hook read-only N/A
Learned memory file Host features vary None MEMORY.md
Vector search Host/plugins None Optional
Rules/context cache Via plugins codex-rules/sessions/*.json Skills/rules
Goal ledger N/A .omo/ultragoal/ Goals CLI
Evidence notepad N/A ultrawork /tmp/ulw-* Devlog

10. Data lifecycle diagram

sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant Codex
  participant Rules
  participant Cache as codex-rules/sessions/*.json
  participant UG as .omo/ultragoal/

  User->>Codex: prompt
  Codex->>Rules: UserPromptSubmit hook
  Rules->>Cache: hydrate + persist dedupe keys
  Rules-->>Codex: additional_context (rules text)
  Note over Cache: Cleared on PostCompact pending reinject
  User->>Codex: ultragoal checkpoint
  Codex->>UG: append ledger.jsonl

11. Security / privacy notes

  • Rules cache JSON may contain rule fingerprints and scope keys — not user secrets, but session-correlated (safePathSegment :129-130).
  • Ultragoal ledger is committed to repo if user commits .omo/ — treat as team-visible audit log.
  • Telemetry: opt-out via component env (see telemetry README) — not a memory store.
  • No encryption at rest in LC components — standard filesystem permissions apply.

12. Evidence quality bar

  • Rules cache path + schema with line citations
  • Negative claims: no vector index, no MEMORY.md
  • Ultrawork / ultragoal / telemetry layers distinguished
  • PostCompact reinjection flow documented
  • Explorer vs librarian scope split cited
  • Parity matrix vs co and semantic memory systems

13. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/persistent-cache.ts 14-77, 118-126 Session cache schema + paths
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts 24-69 Hook events + transcript fields
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py 1-45 Ultrawork directive injection
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/paths.ts 4-17 Goal/ledger paths
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/quality-gate.ts 45-70 Completion evidence schema
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts 16-80 Daily dedupe only
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 3-97 Lifecycle hook wiring

161_lazycodex/README.md 48-52 Workflow command artifacts

Related corpus docs

  • Config (rules env): 008_config-system/08_lc_config.md
  • Guardian (quality gates): 012_guardian-approval/12_lc_guardian.md
  • Web research (librarian): 014_web-search/14_lc_web-search.md
  • Codex memory depth: 010_memory-pipeline/10_co_memory.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC memory axis
2026-06-27 WP38: citations + negative claims
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — cache schema + lifecycle

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Rules cache dir ~/.codex/codex-rules PLUGIN_DATA override may change layout
Ultragoal paths .omo/ultragoal/ Schema fields may grow in OmO sync
Ultrawork notepad /tmp/ulw-* Path convention may change in detector
Semantic memory Documented absent Future OmO sync could add features — re-grep
28

축 11: 플러그인 시스템

AXIS

011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex does not implement OmO's full two-direction OpenCode plugin runtime. It vendored the Codex distribution surface: manifest JSON, hooks, skills sync, MCP registration, and bundled agent TOMLs.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Install path — delegation chain
    1. Marketplace manifest
    1. Aggregate plugin manifest
  • Component inventory (from description :20)
    1. npm workspaces — build topology
    1. Hooks manifest — aggregate wiring
    1. MCP registration (plugin surface)
    1. Skills sync — sync-skills.mjs
    1. Component isolation contract
  • Per-component plugin manifests
    1. Bundled Codex agents (outside root manifest fields)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  Install["bunx lazycodex-ai install"] --> OmO["omo install --platform=codex"]
  OmO --> Market[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"]
  Market --> PluginRoot["plugins/omo/"]
  PluginRoot --> Manifest[".codex-plugin/plugin.json"]
  Manifest --> Skills["skills/ (sync-skills.mjs)"]
  Manifest --> Hooks["hooks/hooks.json"]
  Manifest --> MCP[".mcp.json"]
  PluginRoot --> Components["components/* workspaces"]
  Components --> CC["comment-checker"]
  Components --> Rules["rules"]
  Components --> LSP["lsp + lsp-tools-mcp"]
  Components --> Tel["telemetry"]
  Components --> UG["ultragoal"]
  Components --> UW["ultrawork"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

README / docs

bunx lazycodex-ai install
# = bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:6-15
"plugins": [{
  "name": "omo",
  "source": "./plugins/omo",
  "category": "Developer Tools",
  "policy": {
    "installation": "AVAILABLE",
    "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
  }
}]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-16
{
  "name": "omo",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "OMO",
    "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"],
    "defaultPrompt": [
      "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
      "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
      "ulw: run this change with evidence."
    ]
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:1-22
{
  "name": "@code-yeongyu/omo-codex-plugin",
  "workspaces": [
    "components/comment-checker", "components/rules", "components/lsp",
    "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp", "components/telemetry",
    "components/ultragoal", "components/ultrawork"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
    "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
  }
}

README / docs

{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/rules/dist/cli.js\" hook session-start",
  "timeout": 10,
  "statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"
}

README / docs

"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$"

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

README / docs

const componentMarkers = [
  "components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js",
  "components/lsp/dist/cli.js",
  "components/rules/dist/cli.js",
  "components/telemetry/dist/cli.js",
  "components/ultragoal/dist/cli.js",
  "components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py",
];
for (const marker of componentMarkers) {
  assert.match(text, new RegExp(marker.replaceAll("/", "\\/")));
}
assert.doesNotMatch(text, /codex-(comment-checker|lsp|rules|telemetry|ultragoal|ultrawork)@/);

README / docs

const expectedComponentManifests = new Map([["rules", { hooks: "./hooks/hooks.json" }]]);
assert.deepEqual(componentNames, ["comment-checker", "lsp", "rules", "telemetry", "ultragoal", "ultrawork"]);

README / docs

// aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
test("#given aggregate plugin manifest #when inspected #then it owns the omo namespace", async () => {
  const manifest = await readJson(".codex-plugin/plugin.json");
  assert.equal(manifest.name, "omo");
  assert.equal(manifest.hooks, "./hooks/hooks.json");
  assert.equal(manifest.skills, "./skills/");
  assert.equal(manifest.mcpServers, "./.mcp.json");
});

README / docs

plugins/omo/                    ← single Codex plugin id "omo"
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json   ← public manifest
├── hooks/hooks.json            ← fans out to components
├── .mcp.json                   ← LSP MCP only
├── skills/                     ← synced skill tree
└── components/
    ├── comment-checker/        ← PostToolUse quality
    ├── rules/                  ← context injection (+ nested manifest)
    ├── lsp/                    ← hook + lsp-tools-mcp package
    ├── telemetry/              ← SessionStart analytics
    ├── ultragoal/              ← goal steering
    └── ultrawork/              ← ulw detector + agent TOMLs

README / docs

"defaultPrompt": [
  "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
  "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
  "ulw: run this change with evidence."
]

코퍼스 — 011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md (심화)

LazyCodex Plugin System

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex's plugin dimension is packaging and distribution. It ships marketplace sisyphuslabs with a single aggregate plugin omo that bundles OMO Codex components under one namespace while keeping each component isolated under components/.

LazyCodex does not implement OmO's full two-direction OpenCode plugin runtime. It vendored the Codex distribution surface: manifest JSON, hooks, skills sync, MCP registration, and bundled agent TOMLs.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  Install["bunx lazycodex-ai install"] --> OmO["omo install --platform=codex"]
  OmO --> Market[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"]
  Market --> PluginRoot["plugins/omo/"]
  PluginRoot --> Manifest[".codex-plugin/plugin.json"]
  Manifest --> Skills["skills/ (sync-skills.mjs)"]
  Manifest --> Hooks["hooks/hooks.json"]
  Manifest --> MCP[".mcp.json"]
  PluginRoot --> Components["components/* workspaces"]
  Components --> CC["comment-checker"]
  Components --> Rules["rules"]
  Components --> LSP["lsp + lsp-tools-mcp"]
  Components --> Tel["telemetry"]
  Components --> UG["ultragoal"]
  Components --> UW["ultrawork"]

1. Install path — delegation chain

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...forwardedArgs.slice(1)]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

User-facing shorthand (README.md:34-36):

bunx lazycodex-ai install
# = bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex

Plugin registration happens inside OmO installer — LC repo ships source snapshot for marketplace + aggregate plugin tree.

2. Marketplace manifest

// 161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json:6-15
"plugins": [{
  "name": "omo",
  "source": "./plugins/omo",
  "category": "Developer Tools",
  "policy": {
    "installation": "AVAILABLE",
    "authentication": "ON_INSTALL"
  }
}]
Field Meaning
name: sisyphuslabs Marketplace id (:2)
source: ./plugins/omo Relative plugin root in repo
installation: AVAILABLE Listed for install
authentication: ON_INSTALL Auth at install time (host/OmO)

3. Aggregate plugin manifest

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json:1-16
{
  "name": "omo",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "skills": "./skills/",
  "hooks": "./hooks/hooks.json",
  "mcpServers": "./.mcp.json",
  "interface": {
    "displayName": "OMO",
    "capabilities": ["Hooks", "MCP Tools", "Code Intelligence", "Workflow", "Context Injection"],
    "defaultPrompt": [
      "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
      "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
      "ulw: run this change with evidence."
    ]
  }
}

Component inventory (from description :20)

Component Role
comment-checker Post-edit comment quality
rules Context injection / project rules
lsp Diagnostics hook + LSP MCP
telemetry PostHog daily-active dedupe
ultragoal Repo goal ledger + steering
ultrawork ulw/ultrawork workflow mode

4. npm workspaces — build topology

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json:1-22
{
  "name": "@code-yeongyu/omo-codex-plugin",
  "workspaces": [
    "components/comment-checker", "components/rules", "components/lsp",
    "components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp", "components/telemetry",
    "components/ultragoal", "components/ultrawork"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "node scripts/sync-skills.mjs && node ../scripts/sync-telemetry-component.mjs && npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
    "test": "node --test test/*.test.mjs"
  }
}

Build order enforced by test (aggregate.test.mjs:73-84):

  1. sync-skills.mjs
  2. sync-telemetry-component.mjs
  3. npm run build --workspaces --if-present

5. Hooks manifest — aggregate wiring

Full hook graph (hooks/hooks.json):

Event Components invoked Timeout
SessionStart rules, telemetry 10s, 5s
UserPromptSubmit rules, ultrawork-detector.py, ultragoal 10s, 5s, 10s
PostToolUse (edit tools) comment-checker, lsp 30s, 60s
PostToolUse (apply_patch) rules (additional) 10s
PostCompact rules cache reset 10s

Example SessionStart entry (:3-12):

{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "node \"${PLUGIN_ROOT}/components/rules/dist/cli.js\" hook session-start",
  "timeout": 10,
  "statusMessage": "loading OMO project rules"
}

${PLUGIN_ROOT} is Codex host substitution — plugin path resolution is host-owned.

PostToolUse matcher (:57):

"matcher": "^(apply_patch|write|Write|edit|Edit|multi_edit|multiedit|MultiEdit)$"

6. MCP registration (plugin surface)

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

Single MCP server — see 09_lc_mcp.md for JSON-RPC depth.

7. Skills sync — sync-skills.mjs

Build script reference (package.json:18): node scripts/sync-skills.mjs runs before workspace builds.

Aggregate test (aggregate.test.mjs:135-161) verifies:

  • Skills reference spawn_agent(agent_type="explorer|librarian|plan")
  • Matching TOMLs exist at components/ultrawork/agents/<type>.toml

This ties skill surface to bundled Codex agent roles — plugin system links skills ↔ agents.

8. Component isolation contract

Test #given isolated components (aggregate.test.mjs:38-57):

const componentMarkers = [
  "components/comment-checker/dist/cli.js",
  "components/lsp/dist/cli.js",
  "components/rules/dist/cli.js",
  "components/telemetry/dist/cli.js",
  "components/ultragoal/dist/cli.js",
  "components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py",
];
for (const marker of componentMarkers) {
  assert.match(text, new RegExp(marker.replaceAll("/", "\\/")));
}
assert.doesNotMatch(text, /codex-(comment-checker|lsp|rules|telemetry|ultragoal|ultrawork)@/);

Design rule: hooks reference dist paths inside component roots, not separate marketplace plugin ids per component.

Per-component plugin manifests

Only rules declares nested .codex-plugin/plugin.json (aggregate.test.mjs:86-107):

const expectedComponentManifests = new Map([["rules", { hooks: "./hooks/hooks.json" }]]);
assert.deepEqual(componentNames, ["comment-checker", "lsp", "rules", "telemetry", "ultragoal", "ultrawork"]);

Other components are library packages consumed by aggregate hooks/MCP — not standalone Codex plugins in this snapshot.

9. Bundled Codex agents (outside root manifest fields)

Agent TOMLs live under components/ultrawork/agents/ (aggregate.test.mjs:110-122):

File name field
codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml codex-ultrawork-reviewer
explorer.toml explorer
librarian.toml librarian
plan.toml plan

Schema keys required by test (:126-131): name, description, nickname_candidates, model, model_reasoning_effort, developer_instructions.

These agents are packaged with ultrawork component — registered via OmO installer, not listed in root plugin.json fields.

10. Manifest ownership test

// aggregate.test.mjs:22-36
test("#given aggregate plugin manifest #when inspected #then it owns the omo namespace", async () => {
  const manifest = await readJson(".codex-plugin/plugin.json");
  assert.equal(manifest.name, "omo");
  assert.equal(manifest.hooks, "./hooks/hooks.json");
  assert.equal(manifest.skills, "./skills/");
  assert.equal(manifest.mcpServers, "./.mcp.json");
});

11. Topology — aggregate namespace pattern

plugins/omo/                    ← single Codex plugin id "omo"
├── .codex-plugin/plugin.json   ← public manifest
├── hooks/hooks.json            ← fans out to components
├── .mcp.json                   ← LSP MCP only
├── skills/                     ← synced skill tree
└── components/
    ├── comment-checker/        ← PostToolUse quality
    ├── rules/                  ← context injection (+ nested manifest)
    ├── lsp/                    ← hook + lsp-tools-mcp package
    ├── telemetry/              ← SessionStart analytics
    ├── ultragoal/              ← goal steering
    └── ultrawork/              ← ulw detector + agent TOMLs

Each feature remains maintainable in isolation (separate workspace, tests) while exposed as one plugin to Codex host.

12. Parity matrix — plugin axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
Plugin manifest schema Codex plugin vN .codex-plugin/plugin.json v5 fields
Skills path Host ~/.codex/skills "./skills/" relative
Hooks Host hook runner hooks/hooks.json subprocess commands
Marketplace Codex marketplace API .agents/plugins/marketplace.json snapshot
Component count N plugins 1 aggregate + 6 workspaces
MCP in manifest Optional ./.mcp.json (1 server)
Install CLI codex plugin install lazycodex-ai → OmO

13. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Missing Notes
OpenCode two-direction plugin loader LC targets --platform=codex only
Dynamic plugin marketplace CRUD Static JSON in repo
Plugin version resolver Single pinned omo@0.1.0 in manifest
Hot-reload plugin dev server Standard npm build/test
Per-component marketplace entries Isolation test forbids codex-*@ ids in hooks

14. Release / CI surfaces (plugin-adjacent)

.github/workflows in repo root runs npm CI for plugin workspaces and web package — ensures aggregate plugin builds before publish. Not runtime plugin logic; affects artifact integrity of dist/ paths referenced in hooks.

15. Evidence quality bar

  • Marketplace + aggregate manifest with line citations
  • Full hooks event table from hooks.json
  • Workspace list + build order from tests
  • Component isolation contract from aggregate.test.mjs
  • Skills ↔ agent TOML linkage test cited
  • Negative: no OpenCode runtime in LC tree

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 8-11 Install delegation
161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json 1-16 Marketplace snapshot
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 1-34 Aggregate manifest
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json 8-21 Workspaces + scripts
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 1-99 Hook fan-out
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json 1-8 MCP registration

Related corpus docs

  • Config env knobs: 008_config-system/08_lc_config.md
  • MCP depth: 009_mcp-orchestration/09_lc_mcp.md
  • Post-tool gates: 012_guardian-approval/12_lc_guardian.md
