변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC plugin axis
2026-06-27 WP38: parity vs co + extended evidence
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — topology + aggregate tests

17. Default prompts as plugin UX config

Root manifest ships starter prompts (plugin.json:27-31):

"defaultPrompt": [
  "Use OMO LSP diagnostics on this workspace.",
  "Show which OMO rules matched this file.",
  "ulw: run this change with evidence."
]

These are Codex plugin gallery quick actions — not executable code. They document the intended aggregate surface: LSP diagnostics, rules transparency, ultrawork evidence mode.

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Plugin version 0.1.0 in manifest Bump on OmO sync
Component list 6 workspaces New components may appear in upstream
Hook matchers Edit tool regex Codex may add new tool names
Agent TOMLs 4 files in ultrawork/agents Models/instructions drift with OmO
defaultPrompt Three gallery strings May change with UX iteration
29

축 12: 가디언·승인

AXIS

012_guardian-approval/12_lc_guardian.md

기초 설명

Distinguish three layers: (1) host permission before tool runs, (2) PostToolUse block after edits fail quality checks, (3) workflow verdict agents (reviewer / quality gate) that govern "done" — not sandbox policy.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Gate type taxonomy
    1. Hooks manifest — no PreToolUse / PermissionRequest
    1. Comment checker — PostToolUse block
  • Block output shape
  • Processing loop (:35-58)
    1. LSP diagnostics — PostToolUse block
  • Mutation tool detection (:27, 66-96)
  • Clean diagnostic bypass (:98-100)
    1. Ultrawork reviewer — workflow approval agent
    1. Ultragoal quality gate — completion governance
    1. Rules — context injection (not guardian)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  subgraph host ["Codex host (not LC code)"]
    PreApprove["file/shell/MCP approval"]
    Sandbox["sandbox policy"]
  end
  subgraph lc_hooks ["LC PostToolUse hooks"]
    CC["comment-checker → decision:block"]
    LSP["lsp diagnostics → decision:block"]
  end
  subgraph workflow ["LC workflow gates"]
    Reviewer["codex-ultrawork-reviewer TOML"]
    QG["ultragoal quality-gate.ts"]
  end
  ToolRun["apply_patch / write / edit"] --> PreApprove
  PreApprove --> ToolRun
  ToolRun --> CC
  ToolRun --> LSP
  CC --> BlockTurn["Hook block → user/model sees reason"]
  LSP --> BlockTurn
  UltraworkDone["ulw completion claim"] --> Reviewer
  GoalDone["ultragoal checkpoint"] --> QG

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json — events present:
// SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, PostCompact
// ABSENT: PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, SubagentStop

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:60-65
if (warnings.length === 0) return "";

return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

README / docs

export async function runCommentCheckerPostToolUse(input: CodexPostToolUseInput, ...): Promise<string> {
  const requests = extractCodexCommentCheckRequests(input);
  if (requests.length === 0) return "";
  for (const request of requests) {
    const result = await runner(toHookInput(request, context));
    if (result.status === "missing" || result.status === "pass") continue;
    if (result.status === "error") continue;
    // accumulate warnings
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:18-63
interface PostToolUseHookOutput {
  decision: "block";
  reason: string;
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: "PostToolUse";
    additionalContext: string;
  };
}

README / docs

const MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["apply_patch", "write", "edit", "multiedit", "multi_edit"]);

export function extractMutatedFilePaths(input: CodexPostToolUseInput): string[] {
  if (!isMutationTool(input.tool_name)) return [];
  if (isFailedToolResponse(input.tool_response)) return [];
  // collect path, filePath, file_path, paths[], patch payloads...
}

README / docs

function isCleanDiagnostics(diagnostics: string): boolean {
  return diagnostics.length === 0 || diagnostics.includes(CLEAN_DIAGNOSTICS_TEXT) || ...
}

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17
name = "codex-ultrawork-reviewer"
description = "Strict ultrawork verification reviewer..."
model = "gpt-5.2"
model_reasoning_effort = "xhigh"

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/quality-gate.ts:45-58
export function validateQualityGate(input: unknown): UltragoalQualityGate {
  if (cleaner["status"] !== "passed") invalid("aiSlopCleaner.status must be passed.", ...);
  if (verification["status"] !== "passed") invalid("verification.status must be passed.", ...);
  if (review["recommendation"] !== "APPROVE") invalid("recommendation must be APPROVE.", ...);
  if (review["architectStatus"] !== "CLEAR") invalid("architectStatus must be CLEAR.", ...);
  const totalCriteria = numberField(coverage["totalCriteria"], ...);
  const passCount = numberField(coverage["passCount"], ...);
  if (passCount < totalCriteria) invalid("criteriaCoverage.passCount must cover totalCriteria.", ...);
}

README / docs

# ultrawork-detector.py:10
# exit: 0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js — entire file is spawnSync forwarding
// No permission evaluation, no risk scoring, no ask/deny engine

코퍼스 — 012_guardian-approval/12_lc_guardian.md (심화)

LazyCodex Guardian / Approval

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex has no independent guardian/approval subsystem. It is a wrapper + plugin bundle. Execution approval (file/shell/MCP) remains Codex host-owned. LC-local evidence shows post-edit quality blockers, ultrawork reviewer gates, and ultragoal completion governance.

Distinguish three layers: (1) host permission before tool runs, (2) PostToolUse block after edits fail quality checks, (3) workflow verdict agents (reviewer / quality gate) that govern "done" — not sandbox policy.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph host ["Codex host (not LC code)"]
    PreApprove["file/shell/MCP approval"]
    Sandbox["sandbox policy"]
  end
  subgraph lc_hooks ["LC PostToolUse hooks"]
    CC["comment-checker → decision:block"]
    LSP["lsp diagnostics → decision:block"]
  end
  subgraph workflow ["LC workflow gates"]
    Reviewer["codex-ultrawork-reviewer TOML"]
    QG["ultragoal quality-gate.ts"]
  end
  ToolRun["apply_patch / write / edit"] --> PreApprove
  PreApprove --> ToolRun
  ToolRun --> CC
  ToolRun --> LSP
  CC --> BlockTurn["Hook block → user/model sees reason"]
  LSP --> BlockTurn
  UltraworkDone["ulw completion claim"] --> Reviewer
  GoalDone["ultragoal checkpoint"] --> QG

1. Gate type taxonomy

Gate Type Owner Behavior
Codex file/shell/MCP approval Pre-tool Codex host Permission modes in hook stdin (permission_mode field)
Comment checker Post-tool quality LC component decision: "block" on warnings (codex-hook.ts:62-65)
LSP diagnostics Post-tool quality LC component decision: "block" on errors (codex-hook.ts:55-63)
Ultrawork reviewer Workflow verdict Bundled agent TOML APPROVE vs REJECTION strings
Ultragoal quality gate Completion governance quality-gate.ts Structured evidence schema
Rules Context policy LC component Injects instructions — not permission decisions
Ultrawork detector Context injection Python hook exit: 0 always — never blocks (ultrawork-detector.py:10)

2. Hooks manifest — no PreToolUse / PermissionRequest

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json — events present:
// SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, PostCompact
// ABSENT: PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, SubagentStop

Grep of full hooks.json (99 lines) confirms LC bundle does not register pre-execution permission hooks — those would be host-native or OmO OpenCode paths.

Hook stdin always includes permission_mode (e.g. codex-hook.ts:21, 43, 54) — read-only consumption of host state, not evaluation.

3. Comment checker — PostToolUse block

Block output shape

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts:60-65
if (warnings.length === 0) return "";

return JSON.stringify({
  decision: "block",
  reason: formatWarnings(warnings),
});

Processing loop (:35-58)

export async function runCommentCheckerPostToolUse(input: CodexPostToolUseInput, ...): Promise<string> {
  const requests = extractCodexCommentCheckRequests(input);
  if (requests.length === 0) return "";
  for (const request of requests) {
    const result = await runner(toHookInput(request, context));
    if (result.status === "missing" || result.status === "pass") continue;
    if (result.status === "error") continue;
    // accumulate warnings
  }
}
Result status Hook effect
missing / pass No block
error Silent continue (no block)
warning message Contributes to decision: block

Hook wiring: hooks/hooks.json:59-64 — 30s timeout, matcher on edit tools.

4. LSP diagnostics — PostToolUse block

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts:18-63
interface PostToolUseHookOutput {
  decision: "block";
  reason: string;
  hookSpecificOutput: {
    hookEventName: "PostToolUse";
    additionalContext: string;
  };
}

Mutation tool detection (:27, 66-96)

const MUTATION_TOOL_NAMES = new Set(["apply_patch", "write", "edit", "multiedit", "multi_edit"]);

export function extractMutatedFilePaths(input: CodexPostToolUseInput): string[] {
  if (!isMutationTool(input.tool_name)) return [];
  if (isFailedToolResponse(input.tool_response)) return [];
  // collect path, filePath, file_path, paths[], patch payloads...
}

Clean diagnostic bypass (:98-100)

function isCleanDiagnostics(diagnostics: string): boolean {
  return diagnostics.length === 0 || diagnostics.includes(CLEAN_DIAGNOSTICS_TEXT) || ...
}

Only error-severity diagnostics fetched (:32 — severity: "error" in runLspDiagnosticsText).

Hook timeout: 60s (hooks/hooks.json:67-69) — longest PostToolUse hook.

5. Ultrawork reviewer — workflow approval agent

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml:1-17
name = "codex-ultrawork-reviewer"
description = "Strict ultrawork verification reviewer..."
model = "gpt-5.2"
model_reasoning_effort = "xhigh"

Verdict rules (developer_instructions):

  • UNCONDITIONAL APPROVAL — all criteria + real-surface evidence
  • REJECTION — missing evidence, avoidable risk, scope drift
  • "looks good but..." treated as rejection

This is LLM-as-judge, not deterministic policy engine — orchestrated by ultrawork workflow/skills, not hooks.json.

6. Ultragoal quality gate — completion governance

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/quality-gate.ts:45-58
export function validateQualityGate(input: unknown): UltragoalQualityGate {
  if (cleaner["status"] !== "passed") invalid("aiSlopCleaner.status must be passed.", ...);
  if (verification["status"] !== "passed") invalid("verification.status must be passed.", ...);
  if (review["recommendation"] !== "APPROVE") invalid("recommendation must be APPROVE.", ...);
  if (review["architectStatus"] !== "CLEAR") invalid("architectStatus must be CLEAR.", ...);
  const totalCriteria = numberField(coverage["totalCriteria"], ...);
  const passCount = numberField(coverage["passCount"], ...);
  if (passCount < totalCriteria) invalid("criteriaCoverage.passCount must cover totalCriteria.", ...);
}

Required sections:

Section Required state
aiSlopCleaner status: "passed" + evidence
verification status: "passed" + commands[] + evidence
codeReview recommendation: "APPROVE", architectStatus: "CLEAR"
criteriaCoverage passCount >= totalCriteria

Blocker classification helpers (:73-80) normalize external auth failures (GHCR 401/403 patterns) — governance, not tool permission.

Steering hook: hooks/hooks.json:45-52 — ultragoal user-prompt-submit (injection/steering, not block).

7. Rules — context injection (not guardian)

Rules hooks return additional_context JSON — they add instructions when matches fire. No decision: "block" in rules codex-hook.ts for standard paths.

PostCompact resets cache (hooks/hooks.json:85-97) — session hygiene, not approval.

Env master switch: CODEX_RULES_DISABLED (config.ts:8) disables rules entirely — soft off, not security boundary.

8. Ultrawork detector — non-blocking

# ultrawork-detector.py:10
# exit: 0 always (this hook never blocks the turn).

Injects large <ultrawork-mode> directive on ulw/ultrawork keywords — raises bar via prompt, not hook block.

9. CLI wrapper — no approval logic

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js — entire file is spawnSync forwarding
// No permission evaluation, no risk scoring, no ask/deny engine

10. Comparison — post-tool block vs host guardian

Aspect Codex host guardian LC PostToolUse hooks
Timing Before/during tool execution After successful edit tools
Scope All tools (shell, MCP, etc.) Matcher-limited edit tools
Mechanism Policy engine / TUI JSON decision: "block"
Reversible User approves/denies Model must fix and retry
Config config.toml, MDM Hook subprocess timeouts

LC hooks are quality gates, not replacement for host sandbox.

11. Parity matrix — guardian axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc)
Permission evaluator codex-rs policy Host only
Risk scoring Host features None in LC
PreToolUse hooks Host + extensions Not in hooks.json
Post-edit block Optional comment-checker + LSP
Reviewer agent N/A ultrawork reviewer TOML
Goal completion gate N/A ultragoal quality-gate.ts

12. Security notes

  • PostToolUse blocks run after patch applied — race: bad code briefly on disk before block surfaces to model.
  • Comment checker error status does not block (codex-hook.ts:53) — silent pass on checker failures.
  • LSP hook skips failed tool responses (codex-hook.ts:68) — no double-penalty on failed edits.
  • Reviewer agent is prompt-based — no cryptographic attestation of evidence.
  • No allowlist/denylist for URLs or paths in LC guardian layer (contrast web-search SSRF — host concern).

13. Operational workflow (ultrawork + gates)

  1. User prompts with ulw → detector injects ultrawork directive (non-blocking)
  2. Model edits files → host may approve tools per permission_mode
  3. PostToolUse → comment-checker + LSP may block with reasons
  4. Model fixes issues → loops until hooks pass
  5. Before "done" → spawn codex-ultrawork-reviewer for verdict
  6. Ultragoal checkpoints → validateQualityGate on structured JSON evidence

14. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Missing subsystem Evidence
Risk score / trust tiers No modules in 161_lazycodex/
Permission queue / UI Wrapper has no TUI
allow / deny / ask engine Not in component source
Shell command policy Host-only
MCP tool pre-approval Host MCP client
PreToolUse hook handlers Absent from hooks.json

15. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative claim: no LC permission evaluator
  • Comment checker block JSON with lines
  • LSP block JSON + mutation tool set
  • hooks.json event list proves no PreToolUse
  • Reviewer TOML verdict rules quoted
  • Quality gate validation table from quality-gate.ts
  • Ultrawork detector non-blocking cited

16. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 1-27 No approval logic
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 1-99 Hook events (no PreToolUse)
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/comment-checker/src/codex-hook.ts 35-65 Block on warnings
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/src/codex-hook.ts 18-96 LSP block + path extract
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/codex-ultrawork-reviewer.toml 1-17 Reviewer verdicts
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/hooks/ultrawork-detector.py 1-10 Non-blocking injection
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultragoal/src/quality-gate.ts 45-70 Completion schema
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/rules/src/codex-hook.ts 21-54 permission_mode read-only

Related corpus docs

  • Plugin hooks wiring: 011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md
  • MCP (no pre-approval): 009_mcp-orchestration/09_lc_mcp.md
  • Memory (evidence notepad): 010_memory-pipeline/10_lc_memory.md
  • Codex guardian depth: 012_guardian-approval/12_co_guardian.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC guardian axis
2026-06-27 WP38: source citations
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — gate taxonomy + block JSON

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Hook matchers Edit tool regex New Codex tool names may need matcher updates
Block JSON schema decision + reason Host hook protocol may extend fields
Reviewer model gpt-5.2 in TOML Model id drift on OmO sync
Quality gate fields 4 required sections Schema may grow in ultragoal
30

축 13: 기타

AXIS

013_etc/13_lc_etc.md

기초 설명

LazyCodex is intentionally thin: a CLI shim (bin/lazycodex-ai.js), marketplace snapshot, bundled plugins/omo aggregate plugin, and packages/web docs site. Heavy harness logic lives in delegated oh-my-openagent (OmO).

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture — repository layout
    1. Product positioning vs implementation evidence
    1. Documentation drift — track explicitly
    1. CLI distribution model
    1. Website — packages/web
    1. CI/CD surfaces
    1. Component packaging summary
    1. Telemetry — cross-cutting operational concern
    1. Bundled agent roles (ultrawork)
    1. Optional src/ submodule
    1. Marketplace branding
    1. Workflow commands (documented, OmO-implemented)

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart LR
  Root["161_lazycodex/"]
  Root --> Bin["bin/lazycodex-ai.js"]
  Root --> Agents[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"]
  Root --> Plugin["plugins/omo/"]
  Root --> Web["packages/web/"]
  Root --> GH[".github/workflows/"]
  Root --> Readme["README.md"]
  Plugin --> Comp["components/*"]
  Web --> Docs["content/docs/"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:13-20
if (dryRun) {
  console.log(["bunx", ...commandArgs].join(" "))
  process.exit(0)
}
const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, { stdio: "inherit" })

README / docs

bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

README / docs

comment-checker, rules, lsp, lsp-tools-mcp, telemetry, ultragoal, ultrawork

README / docs

sync-skills.mjs → sync-telemetry-component.mjs → npm run build --workspaces

README / docs

// .agents/plugins/marketplace.json:2-4
"name": "sisyphuslabs",
"interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" }

README / docs

cd plugins/omo && npm run check
# package.json:19 — "check": "npm run build && npm test"

README / docs

"author": {
  "name": "Yeongyu Kim",
  "email": "yeongyu@users.noreply.github.com",
  "url": "https://github.com/code-yeongyu"
}

코퍼스 — 013_etc/13_lc_etc.md (심화)

LazyCodex Residual / Cross-Cutting Notes

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex's residual surface is product packaging, documentation, release infrastructure, and marketing around OmO/Codex distribution. This axis captures cross-cutting concerns that do not map cleanly to config/MCP/memory/plugin/guardian/web-search.

LazyCodex is intentionally thin: a CLI shim (bin/lazycodex-ai.js), marketplace snapshot, bundled plugins/omo aggregate plugin, and packages/web docs site. Heavy harness logic lives in delegated oh-my-openagent (OmO).

Architecture — repository layout

flowchart LR
  Root["161_lazycodex/"]
  Root --> Bin["bin/lazycodex-ai.js"]
  Root --> Agents[".agents/plugins/marketplace.json"]
  Root --> Plugin["plugins/omo/"]
  Root --> Web["packages/web/"]
  Root --> GH[".github/workflows/"]
  Root --> Readme["README.md"]
  Plugin --> Comp["components/*"]
  Web --> Docs["content/docs/"]

1. Product positioning vs implementation evidence

README headline (README.md:6-7, 60-64):

Codex for no-brainers. LazyCodex wraps OmO so you don't have to think about setup. Think LazyVim for lazy.nvim, but for Codex.

Feature table (:68-77):

Feature README claim
Discipline Agents Sisyphus orchestrates Hephaestus, Oracle, Librarian
Parallel Execution Multiple agents on subtasks
Multi-Model Routing Automatic model selection
Skills System Extensible skill library
Hooks & Lifecycle Pre/post hooks
Zero Config Sensible defaults

Implementation split: README describes OmO capabilities; LC repo evidence is primarily wrapper + plugins/omo bundle + docs — not full OmO source tree.

2. Documentation drift — track explicitly

Drift item Marketing/docs Audited LC evidence
Platform availability README :25 — "Coming June 2026 · Currently available for OpenCode" Wrapper hardcodes --platform=codex (lazycodex-ai.js:10)
Command target README :46 — "LazyCodex adds three workflow commands to your OpenCode session" Install uses Codex platform flag
Submodule mention README architecture section references src submodule Local tree has plugins/omo + optional src/ submodule (3300+ files when present)
Zero config README :77 User still runs bunx lazycodex-ai install; OmO TUI unless --no-tui

Corpus rule: treat README as narrative; treat bin/lazycodex-ai.js + marketplace.json + plugins/omo/** as authoritative for LC-local behavior.

3. CLI distribution model

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:13-20
if (dryRun) {
  console.log(["bunx", ...commandArgs].join(" "))
  process.exit(0)
}
const result = spawnSync("bunx", commandArgs, { stdio: "inherit" })
Property Value
Global install Not required — README :32 "No global install"
Package runner bunx always
OmO package oh-my-openagent via --package
Dry run --dry-run prints command (:13-15)

Autonomous install variant (README.md:40-41):

bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

Flags forwarded to OmO installer — not parsed in LC shim.

4. Website — packages/web

Docs/marketing site (Next.js stack per README architecture section):

  • Centralized copy: packages/web/lib/site-config.ts (command strings, branding)
  • Content: packages/web/content/docs/*.md — overview, start-work, workflow commands
  • Not a search runtime — static/docs only (see 14_lc_web-search.md)

Web package does not implement agent harness, hooks, or MCP — pure product surface.

5. CI/CD surfaces

.github/workflows/ (repo root) typically includes:

Workflow class Purpose
npm CI Build/test plugins/omo workspaces
npm publish Release lazycodex-ai package
web CI/deploy Docs site to Cloudflare/OpenNext

CI validates that hook paths like components/*/dist/cli.js exist post-build — ties release pipeline to plugin artifact integrity (aggregate.test.mjs isolation tests).

6. Component packaging summary

Aggregate plugin workspaces (plugins/omo/package.json:8-16):

comment-checker, rules, lsp, lsp-tools-mcp, telemetry, ultragoal, ultrawork

Build pipeline (:18):

sync-skills.mjs → sync-telemetry-component.mjs → npm run build --workspaces

Each component maintains isolated README under components/<name>/README.md — telemetry README documents PostHog boundaries and opt-out env vars.

7. Telemetry — cross-cutting operational concern

PostHog daily-active capture (telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts:63-80):

  • Fires on SessionStart hook (hooks/hooks.json:14-22)
  • Stores only lastActiveDayUTC in posthog-activity.json
  • Not user content logging — operational analytics with dedupe

Cross-ref: 008_lc_config.md telemetry section, 010_lc_memory.md (explicitly not memory).

8. Bundled agent roles (ultrawork)

Agent TOMLs under plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/ (aggregate.test.mjs:117-122):

Agent Role
explorer Local codebase investigation
librarian External OSS/docs research (host web tools)
plan Strategic planning — writes plans, no product code
codex-ultrawork-reviewer Strict verification reviewer

Packaged outside root plugin.json agent fields — registered via OmO Codex installer.

Skills reference these via spawn_agent(agent_type=...) — verified by aggregate.test.mjs:135-161.

9. Optional src/ submodule

Glob shows 161_lazycodex/src/ with extensive OmO-related TypeScript (tools, plugin handlers, delegate-task, etc.) when submodule initialized — 3000+ files.

LC corpus axes primarily cite plugins/omo snapshot (Codex plugin distribution). Full OmO harness source in src/ is upstream parity reference, not what lazycodex-ai npm package ships as the thin wrapper layer.

If submodule absent in a checkout, wrapper + plugins/omo + web still constitute complete LC distribution evidence.

10. Marketplace branding

// .agents/plugins/marketplace.json:2-4
"name": "sisyphuslabs",
"interface": { "displayName": "Sisyphus Labs" }

Plugin display name (plugin.json:18): "OMO".

Brand color in manifest (plugin.json:32): #7C3AED — UI metadata for Codex plugin gallery.

11. Workflow commands (documented, OmO-implemented)

README table (:48-52):

Command Documented behavior
$ulw-loop Self-referential loop until Oracle verification; caps 500/100 iterations
$ulw-plan Prometheus planner → plans/<slug>.md
$start-work Execute plan checkboxes → ORCHESTRATION COMPLETE

Implementation lives in OmO/OpenCode skill layer — LC documents and distributes, does not define loop runtime in bin/.

12. Absences — confirmed negative findings

Grep/architecture pass across 161_lazycodex/ (wrapper + plugins/omo):

Subsystem Status
LazyCodex-native model router Absent
Provider auth stack Delegated to Codex/OmO
Enterprise MDM policy Absent in LC
App server / API backend Absent — web is static/docs
Persistent semantic memory engine Absent — see memory axis
Standalone web search runtime Absent — host-mediated
General MCP orchestrator Absent — single LSP MCP

13. Relationship to OmO (172_oh-my-openagent)

Concern LC repo OmO upstream
Codex install mutations Forwarded install-codex/*
OpenCode plugin runtime Docs mention Full implementation
Delegate-task tools In src/ submodule if present Primary
Codex plugin bundle plugins/omo vendored snapshot Source of truth for sync

LazyCodex = distribution channel; OmO = harness engine.

14. Parity matrix — residual concerns

Concern Full OmO LazyCodex checkout
CLI entry omo multi-platform lazycodex-ai → Codex only
Docs site omO + lazycodex.ai packages/web
Plugin components Many surfaces 6-workspace aggregate
OpenCode commands Native Documented, platform drift in README
Telemetry Component Bundled in plugin
Tests Full suite plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs + component tests

15. Release artifacts

Typical publish surfaces:

  • npm package exposing bin/lazycodex-ai.js
  • GitHub releases (workflows)
  • lazycodex.ai docs deploy

Version in aggregate plugin: 0.1.0 (plugin.json:3) — may lag OmO semver; track on sync.

15.1 npm package surface

The published lazycodex-ai npm package exposes only the wrapper binary — not the full plugins/omo tree as runtime dependency. Users who bunx lazycodex-ai install pull OmO which registers the vendored plugin snapshot from OmO's install path, not by compiling LC repo locally.

Local developers working in 161_lazycodex/ run aggregate plugin tests via:

cd plugins/omo && npm run check
# package.json:19 — "check": "npm run build && npm test"

This validates dist paths referenced in hooks/hooks.json before release.

15.2 License and attribution chain

Aggregate plugin declares MIT (plugin.json:12). Component workspaces inherit upstream OmO licensing. LC repo is a distribution fork snapshot — legal/provenance tracking belongs in release notes when syncing from 172_oh-my-openagent.

Author metadata (plugin.json:5-8):

"author": {
  "name": "Yeongyu Kim",
  "email": "yeongyu@users.noreply.github.com",
  "url": "https://github.com/code-yeongyu"
}

Repository links point to code-yeongyu/omo — LC is branded separately (LazyCodex / Sisyphus Labs marketplace) but shares authorship lineage.

15.3 packages/web command strings

Site config centralizes install copy so docs and landing page stay aligned with shim behavior. When README drift is fixed upstream, update both README.md and packages/web/lib/site-config.ts — corpus treats shim + marketplace as runtime truth, web as user-facing mirror.

Docs paths under packages/web/content/docs/ include overview, install, ultrawork, start-work — each describes OmO workflows re-exported through LazyCodex branding.

15.4 Submodule vs plugin snapshot maintenance

When src/ git submodule is initialized, it may contain a newer OmO harness than plugins/omo snapshot. Release maintainers sync:

  1. OmO upstream changes
  2. plugins/omo component copies
  3. sync-skills.mjs + sync-telemetry-component.mjs
  4. aggregate.test.mjs green

Corpus readers: if submodule and plugins/omo diverge, prefer plugins/omo paths for Codex plugin axis citations unless analyzing full OmO harness features explicitly.

16. Evidence quality bar

  • README vs shim drift documented with line refs
  • Repo layout diagram
  • CI/packaging cross-refs to aggregate tests
  • Telemetry + agent roles as cross-cutting
  • Negative absence table
  • OmO delegation boundary stated

17. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 1-27 Wrapper-only entry
161_lazycodex/README.md 25-41, 48-77 Product narrative + drift
161_lazycodex/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json 1-16 Marketplace branding
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/package.json 8-21 Workspace packaging
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 17-33 Interface metadata
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs 73-161 Build + agent tests
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/hooks/hooks.json 14-22 Telemetry hook
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/telemetry/src/posthog-activity-state.ts 63-80 Analytics dedupe

Related corpus docs

  • Config delegation: 008_config-system/08_lc_config.md
  • Plugin topology: 011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md
  • Web search (host): 014_web-search/14_lc_web-search.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md
  • Harmonization: 000_overview/00_corpus_harmonization_status.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC residual axis
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — drift table + layout

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
README platform wording OpenCode + Codex mixed May unify on Codex launch
Submodule Optional src/ Init state varies by clone
Web stack Next.js 15 / React 19 Upgrade independently
npm package name lazycodex-ai Verify registry on install docs
31

축 14: 웹 검색

AXIS

014_web-search/14_lc_web-search.md

기초 설명

The only MCP server in LC is LSP (.mcp.json) — local symbol/diagnostic tools, not internet search.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • Architecture
    1. Classification table
    1. CLI — no search commands
    1. MCP boundary — LSP only
  • LSP symbols ≠ web search
    1. Librarian agent — host tool orchestration
  • Agent metadata
  • Phase 0.5 — documentation discovery (web_search + webfetch)
  • Step 1 - find official documentation
  • Step 2 - version check
  • Step 3 - sitemap discovery
  • Step 4 - targeted investigation

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

flowchart TD
  User["User question about external library"] --> Orchestrator["OmO ultrawork / spawn_agent"]
  Orchestrator --> Librarian["librarian.toml agent"]
  Librarian --> HostSearch["Codex host web_search"]
  Librarian --> HostFetch["Codex host webfetch"]
  Librarian --> GhCLI["gh search / gh api / clone"]
  HostSearch --> Internet["Public web"]
  HostFetch --> Internet
  GhCLI --> GitHub["GitHub API + repos"]
  LSPMCP["LSP MCP symbols"] --> LocalCode["Local workspace only"]
  LSPMCP -.->|"NOT web search"| X["❌"]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

README / docs

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:619-636
{
  name: "symbols",
  aliases: ["lsp_symbols"],
  description: "List document symbols or search workspace symbols.",
  inputSchema: objectSchema({
    filePath: { type: "string", ... },
    scope: { enum: ["document", "workspace"], ... },
    query: { type: "string", description: "Workspace symbol query." },
  }, ["filePath", "scope"]),
}

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:1-6
name = "librarian"
description = "External open-source codebase and documentation researcher..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
service_tier = "fast"

README / docs

# librarian.toml:42-58 (representative)
## Step 1 - find official documentation
- `web_search("<library-name> official documentation")` -> pick the official URL
## Step 2 - version check
- `web_search("<library-name> v<version> documentation")`
## Step 3 - sitemap discovery
- `webfetch(<base>/sitemap.xml)`. Fallbacks: `/sitemap-0.xml`, ...
## Step 4 - targeted investigation
- `webfetch(<specific-doc-page-from-sitemap>)`

README / docs

# CRITICAL: DATE AWARENESS
Before any search, check the current date from the environment.
- NEVER query with last year's date.
- ALWAYS include the current year in time-sensitive queries

README / docs

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:68
- Do not browse the internet. External research is the librarian's job.

README / docs

https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/blob/<commit-sha>/<filepath>#L<start>-L<end>

README / docs

// aggregate.test.mjs:135-161
// Skills with spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian") must have matching TOML
assert.deepEqual(expected, ["explorer", "librarian", "plan"]);
const tomlPath = join(root, "components", "ultrawork", "agents", `${agentType}.toml`);

코퍼스 — 014_web-search/14_lc_web-search.md (심화)

LazyCodex Web Search / Research

분석 스냅샷: 161_lazycodex/ — LazyCodex does not own a general web-search engine, browser runtime, or first-party web-search MCP server. Web/search is host-mediated: Codex host tools (web_search, webfetch) invoked per bundled librarian agent instructions in librarian.toml.

The only MCP server in LC is LSP (.mcp.json) — local symbol/diagnostic tools, not internet search.

Architecture

flowchart TD
  User["User question about external library"] --> Orchestrator["OmO ultrawork / spawn_agent"]
  Orchestrator --> Librarian["librarian.toml agent"]
  Librarian --> HostSearch["Codex host web_search"]
  Librarian --> HostFetch["Codex host webfetch"]
  Librarian --> GhCLI["gh search / gh api / clone"]
  HostSearch --> Internet["Public web"]
  HostFetch --> Internet
  GhCLI --> GitHub["GitHub API + repos"]
  LSPMCP["LSP MCP symbols"] --> LocalCode["Local workspace only"]
  LSPMCP -.->|"NOT web search"| X["❌"]

1. Classification table

Surface Finding Evidence
CLI wrapper Delegates all commands to OmO lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
Plugin MCP Only lsp server .mcp.json:1-8
Web-search MCP Absent No exa/tavily/brave in .mcp.json
Librarian agent Instructs host web_search + webfetch librarian.toml:43-49, 145-149
Explorer agent No internet explorer.toml:68
LSP symbols tool Workspace symbol search tools.ts:619-636 — local only
Web site Docs/marketing packages/web/ — not search runtime

Verdict: classify lc as host-mediated web/search — no independent general search runtime in this source pass.

2. CLI — no search commands

// 161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js:8-11
const commandArgs =
  forwardedArgs[0] === "install"
    ? ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", "install", "--platform=codex", ...]
    : ["--package", "oh-my-openagent", "omo", ...forwardedArgs]

Grep of entire 161_lazycodex/bin/ — no /web, search, scrape, or URL fetch implementation.

Contrast Aider /web slash command — see 14_ai_web-search.md.

3. MCP boundary — LSP only

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json:1-8
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lsp": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["./components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/dist/cli.js", "mcp"],
      "cwd": "."
    }
  }
}

Aggregate test locks this contract (aggregate.test.mjs:60-70).

LSP symbols ≠ web search

// 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts:619-636
{
  name: "symbols",
  aliases: ["lsp_symbols"],
  description: "List document symbols or search workspace symbols.",
  inputSchema: objectSchema({
    filePath: { type: "string", ... },
    scope: { enum: ["document", "workspace"], ... },
    query: { type: "string", description: "Workspace symbol query." },
  }, ["filePath", "scope"]),
}

workspace scope queries LSP workspace symbols — indexed source in repo, not Google/Bing.

4. Librarian agent — host tool orchestration

Agent metadata

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml:1-6
name = "librarian"
description = "External open-source codebase and documentation researcher..."
model = "gpt-5.4-mini"
model_reasoning_effort = "low"
service_tier = "fast"

Read-only mandate (:207-208):

Tools I will NEVER call: edit, write, apply_patch ... Cloning into ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} is allowed; cloning into the working tree is not.

Phase 0.5 — documentation discovery (web_search + webfetch)

# librarian.toml:42-58 (representative)
## Step 1 - find official documentation
- `web_search("<library-name> official documentation")` -> pick the official URL
## Step 2 - version check
- `web_search("<library-name> v<version> documentation")`
## Step 3 - sitemap discovery
- `webfetch(<base>/sitemap.xml)`. Fallbacks: `/sitemap-0.xml`, ...
## Step 4 - targeted investigation
- `webfetch(<specific-doc-page-from-sitemap>)`

Critical: web_search and webfetch appear as tool names in agent instructions — not implemented in LC repo. Availability depends on Codex host tool registry at runtime.

Date awareness (:19-23)

# CRITICAL: DATE AWARENESS
Before any search, check the current date from the environment.
- NEVER query with last year's date.
- ALWAYS include the current year in time-sensitive queries

Prompt engineering for search quality — not LC code.

Tool reference section (:143-155)

Purpose Instructed tool
Official docs discovery web_search("library official documentation")
Versioned docs web_search + webfetch(/versions)
Sitemap webfetch(sitemap.xml)
Read page webfetch(url)
Latest info web_search("<query> <CURRENT_YEAR>")
Code search gh search code
Issues/PRs `gh search issues
Clone gh repo clone ... --depth 1

GitHub CLI tools are local subprocess tools (when gh installed) — distinct from hosted web_search.

5. Explorer — explicit non-web boundary

# 161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml:68
- Do not browse the internet. External research is the librarian's job.

Role separation prevents duplicate web traffic and confines local repo search to explorer.

6. Request type taxonomy (librarian)

From librarian.toml:27-34:

Type Pattern Primary tools
TYPE A — CONCEPTUAL "How do I use X?" Phase 0.5 docs + web_search + webfetch
TYPE B — IMPLEMENTATION "Show me source of Z" gh clone + rg + permalinks
TYPE C — CONTEXT/HISTORY "Why was X changed?" gh issues/PRs + git log/blame
TYPE D — COMPREHENSIVE Complex/ambiguous Phase 0.5 + parallel 6+ calls

Parallel requirements table (:167-172) — orchestration guidance in TOML, not LC scheduler code.

7. Citation contract — evidence output format

Mandatory permalink format (:128-139):

https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/blob/<commit-sha>/<filepath>#L<start>-L<end>

Never link to branch names (/blob/main/...) — SHA-pinned for verifiability.

This shapes search output quality — librarian returns citations, not raw search dumps.

8. Failure recovery (search paths)

From librarian.toml:192-201:

Failure Recovery
Docs indexer empty Clone repo, read source + README
gh search code empty Broaden query, search forks
gh API rate-limited Use cloned repo in ${TMPDIR}
Sitemap missing Fallback XML paths or parse nav
Sources disagree Surface disagreement — no guessing

LC does not implement retry logic — agent prompt policy only.

9. Skills ↔ librarian linkage

// aggregate.test.mjs:135-161
// Skills with spawn_agent(agent_type="librarian") must have matching TOML
assert.deepEqual(expected, ["explorer", "librarian", "plan"]);
const tomlPath = join(root, "components", "ultrawork", "agents", `${agentType}.toml`);

Web research is skill-orchestrated via agent spawn — not user slash command in LC.

10. Component README — no web-search component

plugins/omo/README.md lists components (comment-checker, rules, lsp, telemetry, ultragoal, ultrawork) — no web-search component directory.

Plugin capabilities (plugin.json:23) include "Code Intelligence" (LSP) and "Workflow" (ultrawork) — not "Web Search".

11. Parity matrix — web-search axis

Concern Codex (co) LazyCodex (lc) Aider (ai)
Built-in model tool web_search in codex-rs None in LC repo No — user /web scrape
Research agent Host tools librarian.toml prompts N/A
MCP web server Optional host config None — LSP only None
Config surface config.toml flags N/A — host gates --disable-playwright
GitHub research gh tools if available librarian instructs gh N/A
Local symbol search LSP / grep LSP MCP symbols repo tools

12. What LazyCodex does NOT implement

Feature Present elsewhere LC
Model web_search tool registration co, omc, ge No — not in LC source
Query decomposition API Responses API / grounding Prompt-only in librarian
web_fetch with pagination Copilot CLI Host webfetch if enabled
MCP Exa / Tavily OmO OpenCode No in .mcp.json
Playwright scrape slash Aider /web No
SSRF allowlist Some harnesses N/A — LC doesn't fetch URLs
Search result cache Various No

13. Host dependency checklist

After Codex upgrade, re-verify:

  1. Host exposes web_search tool to models using librarian
  2. Host exposes webfetch with acceptable size/SSRF policy
  3. gh CLI available on PATH for GitHub research paths
  4. Librarian model id in TOML still valid (gpt-5.4-mini at :4)

LC manifest does not gate or configure host search — zero lines in lazycodex-ai.js or .mcp.json.

14. Security notes

  • SSRF: Any URL librarian passes to webfetch is fetched by host — LC has no domain allowlist.
  • Read-only librarian: Cannot write/apply_patch — reduces exfil-via-edit risk; still can clone public repos to /tmp.
  • Secrets in URLs: Prompt says short quotes only (:210) — no LC enforcement code.
  • LSP MCP: Local paths only — no network in MCP layer.

15. Comparison with LSP MCP search confusion

Operators sometimes conflate:

Tool Scope Network
lsp_symbols query Workspace AST/index No
web_search Public internet Yes (host)
gh search code GitHub index Yes (GitHub API)
rg in cloned repo Temp clone No (after clone)

Document explicitly to avoid misclassifying LC as having "search MCP".

16. Web package — docs only

packages/web/content/docs/overview.md frames LazyCodex as distribution layer — marketing copy, not search backend.

No /api/search routes in LC web package (static docs site).

17. Operational workflow (librarian path)

  1. Orchestrator spawns librarian for external-library question
  2. Librarian classifies request TYPE A–D (librarian.toml:27-34)
  3. Phase 0.5: web_search → official docs URL → webfetch sitemap → targeted pages
  4. Parallel: gh search code, optional shallow clone, issues/PRs
  5. Synthesis: SHA-pinned markdown citations (:109-126)
  6. Explorer handles local repo if question is in working tree — not librarian

18. Evidence quality bar

  • Negative: no LC web_search implementation
  • .mcp.json shows LSP only
  • librarian.toml path:line for web_search/webfetch/gh
  • explorer.toml internet prohibition cited
  • LSP symbols distinguished from web search
  • Parity matrix vs co and ai
  • Host-mediated verdict explicit

19. Source citation index

Path Lines Role
161_lazycodex/bin/lazycodex-ai.js 8-11 No search CLI
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.mcp.json 1-8 LSP MCP only
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/librarian.toml 1-6, 19-58, 128-155, 192-210 Host web tool instructions
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/ultrawork/agents/explorer.toml 68 No internet rule
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/components/lsp/packages/lsp-tools-mcp/src/tools.ts 619-636 Local symbols tool
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/test/aggregate.test.mjs 60-70, 135-161 MCP + librarian TOML
161_lazycodex/plugins/omo/.codex-plugin/plugin.json 23 Capabilities (no web search)

Related corpus docs

  • MCP (LSP only): 009_mcp-orchestration/09_lc_mcp.md
  • Plugin agents: 011_plugin-system/11_lc_plugin.md
  • Aider user scrape: 014_web-search/14_ai_web-search.md
  • Codex hosted search: 014_web-search/14_co_web-search.md
  • OmO MCP search: 014_web-search/14_omo_web-search.md
  • Overview: 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

변경 기록

날짜 변경
2026-05-31 Initial LC web-search axis
2026-06-27 WP38: host-mediated verdict + corpus links
2026-06-27 WP52: expand to ≥12 KB — librarian.toml path:line depth

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current
Host web_search Assumed available to librarian Codex upgrades may change tool specs
Librarian model gpt-5.4-mini in TOML Model id drift on OmO sync
gh CLI Prompt assumes installed User environment dependent
MCP servers 1 (lsp) OmO sync may add servers to snapshot
32

역공학 (015)

RE

015_reverse-engineering

기초 설명

이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • 015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md
  • Evidence Levels
  • Hard Boundary
  • Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)
  • Current Upgrade Scope
  • Reading Rule For Existing Docs

소스 코드 스니펫

161_lazycodex/README.md

🚀 Install

One line. No global install, no npm i -g. Always use bunx:

bunx lazycodex-ai install

This is shorthand for bunx --package oh-my-openagent omo install --platform=codex. For a fully autonomous, no-TUI setup:

bunx lazycodex-ai install --no-tui --codex-autonomous

코퍼스 — 015_reverse-engineering/ (심화)

015_reverse-engineering/00_reverse_evidence_policy.md

Reverse Engineering Evidence Policy

이 축은 설치 help parity 문서의 다음 단계다. 기존 00~14 축은 주로 help/config/local-doc 표면을 비교했고, 이 축은 설치된 바이너리와 번들에서 복원 가능한 구조를 따로 기록한다.

Evidence Levels

Level 의미 사용할 수 있는 주장
installed-help --help, public command output, version output 명령/플래그/공개 subcommand가 존재한다
safe-local-doc 설치 패키지의 docs, changelog, examples, manifest 문서화된 schema/경로/기능 설명이 있다
bundle-key minified bundle에 남은 module key, source path, route name, proto name 내부 모듈/라우터/프로토/컴포넌트가 존재한다
binary-string native binary strings, file, otool, codesign, nm crate/package/protocol/tool/evaluator 이름이 embedded되어 있다
runtime-trace synthetic project에서 실제 명령을 실행한 결과 동작 semantics, precedence, permission enforcement를 확인했다
private-artifact-name 비공개 상태 파일의 경로/개수만 관찰 artifact가 존재한다. 내용/schema 주장은 금지

/Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/33_source_deobfuscation_inventory.md further separates source-like recovery levels. In this corpus, Cursor reaches preserved module-key recovery, while Grok/Kiro/AGY mostly expose native source-path, handler, protobuf, and runtime-trace evidence. Do not call this "full source recovered" unless a real source map or readable source archive is present.

Hard Boundary

역분석의 목적은 제품 구조 복원이지 사용자 상태 추출이 아니다. 다음은 내용 읽기 금지다.

  • auth/token/cookie/credential/login data
  • raw transcript, chat history, conversation DB/PB
  • logs and terminal captures
  • browser profile state, local storage, IndexedDB, session storage
  • memory/goal runtime stores
  • screenshots and browser recordings
  • private mcp.json, settings, config values unless explicitly redacted

비공개 artifact는 “존재한다”까지만 쓴다. 필드/schema/content 주장은 public docs, source/bundle evidence, sanitized fixture, synthetic runtime trace가 있을 때만 쓴다.

Installed CLI version pin (2026-06-27)

Single source of truth for pinned ay/cr/gr/ki binaries:

  • /Users/jun/Developer/codex/015_reverse-engineering/36_installed_cli_version_manifest.md

WP5 trace shards (38–41) extend hubs without changing evidence-level definitions above.

Current Upgrade Scope

2026-06-27 pass — installed CLI practical RE (goal 4e94e12d-a9b):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy v1.0.12+ help, changelog, strings; 38_ay_project_flags_trace.md
cr cursor-agent 2026.06.26-7079533 bundle string literals, help; 37, 39_cr_automations_help_trace.md
gr grok-0.2.67-macos-aarch64 inspect JSON, strings; 40_gr_0267_inspect_refresh.md
ki Kiro CLI.app 2.5.0 help surfaces; 41_ki_help_surface_trace.md

2026-05-31 pass에서 새로 역분석한 대상 (historical baseline):

Prefix Target Evidence kind
gr /Users/jun/.grok/downloads/grok-0.2.14-macos-aarch64, /Users/jun/.grok/bundled, /Users/jun/.grok/docs/user-guide Rust Mach-O strings, crate paths, ACP methods, bundled agents/roles/personas/skills
cr /Users/jun/.local/share/cursor-agent/versions/2026.05.28-418efe5 Webpack module keys, route extraction, proto module names, native helper inventory
ki /Applications/Kiro CLI.app, /Users/jun/.local/bin/kiro-cli* signed universal Mach-O binaries, Rust source-path strings, WebKit resources, dashboard/autocomplete assets
ay /Users/jun/.local/bin/agy, /Users/jun/.gemini/antigravity-cli Go/native strings, protobuf trajectory/cascade names, local cache/config metadata, helper binaries

Reading Rule For Existing Docs

When a 00~14 page says “없음” or “지원하지 않음”, interpret it as “installed help에서 노출되지 않음” unless this 15_reverse-engineering axis confirms absence from bundle/binary evidence too.

When a page states runtime semantics from config/cache observation, this axis upgrades or downgrades the claim:

  • bundle-key or binary-string confirms a module exists.
  • runtime-trace is still required for exact precedence, scheduling, isolation, merge, and permission behavior.
33

기술 참조 & 합성

20-80 + grand synthesis

기초 설명

이 문서는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code를 13개 아키텍처 차원에서 동시에 비교한 캡스톤 문서다. 개별 비교 문서의 결론을 단순 재배열하지 않고, 서로 다른 설계 철학이 어떤 제품 전략으로 수렴하는지까지 읽어내는 것을 목표로 한다.

이 문서에서 다루는 것

  • 000/00_grand_synthesis.md (발췌)
    1. 개요
  • 이 문서의 목적
  • 분석 범위
  • 분석 방법론
  • 이 문서를 읽는 방법
    1. 에이전트 프로필 요약
  • Codex
  • Aider

소스 코드 스니펫

README / docs

graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]

코퍼스 — 000_overview/00_lc_overview.md (심화)

000/00_grand_synthesis.md (발췌)

GRAND SYNTHESIS — 5개 코딩 에이전트 × 13개 차원 최종 종합 분석

이 문서는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code를 13개 아키텍처 차원에서 동시에 비교한 캡스톤 문서다. 개별 비교 문서의 결론을 단순 재배열하지 않고, 서로 다른 설계 철학이 어떤 제품 전략으로 수렴하는지까지 읽어내는 것을 목표로 한다.

graph TD
    A[5개 에이전트 개요] --> B[13개 차원 비교]
    B --> C[공통 패턴 추출]
    B --> D[핵심 차별점 추출]
    C --> E[기술 선택 시사점]
    D --> E
    E --> F[미래 전망과 설계 교훈]

1. 개요

이 문서의 목적

이 문서의 목적은 5개 에이전트를 한 번에 조망할 수 있는 상위 레퍼런스를 만드는 것이다. 개별 문서는 특정 축의 우열을 설명하지만, 실제 제품 선택은 언제나 여러 축의 동시 판단을 요구한다. 따라서 여기서는 TUI, prompt, tool, skill, subagent, app-server, sandbox, config, MCP, memory, plugin, guardian, 그리고 잔여 교차 관심사를 하나의 프레임으로 다시 묶는다.

  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]]

분석 범위

범위는 5개 에이전트와 13개 차원이다. 에이전트는 Codex, Aider, Gemini CLI, OpenCode, Claude Code다. 차원은 TUI, Prompt/Context, Tool Runtime, Skill, Subagents, App-Server Surface, Sandbox, Config, MCP, Memory, Plugin, Guardian, 잔여 교차 관심사(Git/VCS, 모델 라우팅, 에러 처리, 텔레메트리, IDE 통합, 패키징, 테스트, 인증, 멀티모달, 이벤트 시스템, 피처 플래그, 국제화)다. App-Server 축은 비교 문서 자체가 Codex·Gemini CLI·OpenCode 중심으로 작성되어 있으므로 Aider와 Claude Code는 “의도적 부재”까지 함께 해석한다.

  • 근거: [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[006_app-server-client-surfaces/00_surface]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[008_config-system/00_config]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[010_memory-pipeline/00_memory]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]], [[013_etc/00_etc]]

분석 방법론

첫째, 각 에이전트 개요 문서에서 기술 스택, 패키지 구조, 핵심 철학을 추출했다. 둘째, 13개 비교 문서에서 축별 우위와 결손, 스카우팅 점수, 디자인 패턴을 수집했다. 셋째, 같은 에이전트가 여러 축에서 반복적으로 보이는 패턴을 묶어 장기 전략으로 재해석했다. 넷째, 단일 승자를 찾기보다 “어떤 상황에서 어떤 설계가 더 설득력 있는가”를 기준으로 실무 추천안을 도출했다.

  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[00_ai_overview]], [[00_ge_overview]], [[00_oc_overview]], [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]

이 문서를 읽는 방법

제품 선택이 목적이면 3장, 7장, 8장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다. 포크나 재구현이 목적이면 2장, 5장, 6장, 9장을 먼저 보는 것이 좋다. 보안 검토가 목적이면 3장, 4장, 8장, 9장을 함께 읽는 편이 낫다. 마지막 부록은 문서 인덱스 역할을 하므로 세부 원문으로 돌아갈 때 사용하면 된다.

  • 근거: [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]], [[011_plugin-system/00_plugin]], [[012_guardian-approval/00_guardian]]

2. 에이전트 프로필 요약

에이전트 기술 스택 설계 철학 핵심 차별점 실무 적합도 근거 문서
Codex Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol 엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다. 가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합. 보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀 [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]
Aider Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git 도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다. RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-commit, 최소 마찰 CLI 경험. 개인 개발자, 빠른 코드 편집, 낮은 학습 비용이 중요한 사용 사례 [[00_ai_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]]
Gemini CLI TypeScript + React/Ink + Zod + A2A server + multi-package monorepo schema-first와 policy-first를 결합해 CLI를 확장 운영체계처럼 다룬다. PromptProvider/Scheduler/PolicyEngine/ModelRouter/ExtensionManager가 분리된 가장 운영형 TypeScript engine. 엔터프라이즈 CLI, 확장 운영, 정책 감사, TypeScript 기반 재구현 [[00_ge_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[004_skill-system/00_skill]]
OpenCode TypeScript + Bun + Effect-TS + Hono + SolidJS + Tauri agent runtime을 웹/데스크톱/SDK가 공유하는 product platform으로 본다. Effect-TS service layer, 21+ provider, plugin SDK, session replay, multi-surface delivery. 빠른 제품화, GUI/desktop 포함, TS로 직접 확장하고 싶은 팀 [[00_oc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]]
Claude Code TypeScript/JavaScript bundle + React/Ink + custom renderer + Anthropic runtime 제품 사용성을 최우선으로 하면서 hook, security chain, memory, MCP를 깊게 묶는다. 가장 풍부한 productized tool surface, deepest shell security, strongest file-based memory UX. 상용 CLI UX, 강한 shell security, memory-heavy workflows, enterprise policy integration [[00_cc_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]]

Codex

  • 정체성: 엔진을 층으로 분리하고, prompt·tool·skill·subagent·MCP를 제어면(control plane)으로 다룬다.
  • 기술 스택: Rust + TypeScript + React/Ink + app-server protocol
  • 핵심 차별점: 가장 분해 가능한 core engine, 가장 강한 terminal-native orchestration, 가장 균형 잡힌 sandbox/MCP 결합.
  • 실무에서 강한 상황: 보안, 오케스트레이션, 포크 가능한 core engine이 중요한 팀
  • 재구현 관점 한 줄 평: requirements 제약 채널과 prompt fragment provenance를 분리한 구조.
  • 근거: [[00_co_overview]], [[001_tui-design/00_tui]], [[002_prompt-context/00_prompt]], [[003_tool-runtime/00_tool]], [[005_subagents-collab/00_subagent]], [[007_sandbox-exec-policy/00_sandbox]], [[009_mcp-orchestration/00_mcp]]

Aider

  • 정체성: 도구 플랫폼보다 코드 편집 루프에 집중한다. 모델을 좋은 editor로 만드는 쪽에 최적화한다.
  • 기술 스택: Python + prompt_toolkit + rich + tree-sitter + Git
  • 핵심 차별점: RepoMap, edit 전략 다양성, git auto-com

LC101 · 코퍼스 근거 · 업데이트 2026-06-28

000_overview/00_lc_overview.md

CLI 101 · cc101 표면 + 개발자 심화
